t.asad: AND WHEN Abraham spoke to his father and his people, [he had this very truth in mind:] “Verily, far be it from me to worship what you worship
t.yusuf-ali: Behold! Abraham said to his father and his people: "I do indeed clear myself of what ye worship
t.pickthall: And when Abraham said unto his father and his folk: Lo! I am innocent of what ye worship
t.arberry: And when Abraham said to his father and his people, 'Surely I am quit of that you serve
t.hilali-khan: And (remember) when Ibrahim (Abraham) said to his father and his people: "Verily, I am innocent of what you worship
t.itani: When Abraham said to his father and his people, “I am innocent of what you worship.
t.mubarakpuri: And (remember) when Ibrahim said to his father and his people: "Verily, I am innocent of what you worship,"
t.qarib: And when Abraham said to his father and his people, "I have nothing to do with what you worship
t.wahiduddin: Call to mind when Abraham said to his father and his people, "I disown utterly that which you worship
t.shakir: And when Ibrahim said to his father and his people: Surely I am clear of what you worship
t.haleem: Abraham said to his father and his people, ‘I renounce what you worship
t.maariful: (Recall) when Ibrāhīm said to his father and to his people, “I disown that which you worship
t.khattab: ˹Remember, O Prophet˺ when Abraham declared to his father and his people, “I am totally free of whatever ˹gods˺ you worship
t.ghali: And as Ibrahim (Abraham) said to his father and his people, "Surely I (myself) am completely quit of whatever you worship
t.irving: Thus Abraham told his father and his folk: "I am innocent of what you are serving
t.iwq: And mention, O Muhammad, when Abraham said to his father and his people, "Indeed, I am disassociated from that which you worship

t_id.kemenag: Dan ingatlah ketika Ibrahim berkata kepada bapaknya dan kaumnya: "Sesungguhnya aku tidak bertanggung jawab terhadap apa yang kamu sembah,
t_id.sabiq: Ceritakanlah, wahai Muhammad, kepada orang-orang yang mendustakan rasul itu, kisah Nabi Ibrâhîm ketika ia berkata kepada ayah dan kaumnya, "Aku sungguh-sungguh terlepas dari penyembahan tuhan kalian yang palsu itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan) ingatlah (ketika Ibrahim berkata kepada bapaknya dan kaumnya, "Sesungguhnya aku tidak bertanggung jawab) atau berlepas diri (terhadap apa yang kalian sembah.)
t_id.iwq: Dan ingatlah ketika Ibrahim berkata kepada bapaknya dan kaumnya: "Sesungguhnya aku tidak bertanggung jawab terhadap apa yang kamu sembah,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «و» اذكر «قال إبراهيم لأبيه وقومه إنني بَرَاء» أي بريء «مما تعبدون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِذۡ قَالَ إِبۡرَٰهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوۡمِهِۦٓ إِنَّنِي بَرَآءٞ مِّمَّا تَعۡبُدُونَ (بَرَاءٌ: بَرِيءٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan) ingatlah (ketika Ibrahim berkata kepada bapaknya dan kaumnya, "Sesungguhnya aku tidak bertanggung jawab) atau berlepas diri (terhadap apa yang kalian sembah)

ar: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِی فَطَرَنِی فَإِنَّهُۥ سَیَهۡدِینِ
ar_simple: إِلَّا الَّذِي فَطَرَنِي فَإِنَّهُ سَيَهْدِينِ
transliteration: illā alladhī faṭaranī fa-innahu sayahdīni

t.sahih-int: Except for He who created me; and indeed, He will guide me
t.asad: None [do I worship] but Him who has brought me into being: and, behold, it is He who will guide me!”
t.yusuf-ali: (I worship) only Him Who made me, and He will certainly guide me
t.pickthall: Save Him Who did create me, for He will surely guide me
t.arberry: except Him who originated me; and He will guide me
t.hilali-khan: Except Him (i.e. I worship none but Allah Alone) Who did create me, and verily, He will guide me
t.itani: Except for He who created me, for He will guide me.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Except Him Who created me; and verily, He will guide me."
t.qarib: I worship only the One who created me and gave me purpose, He will guide me
t.wahiduddin: [I worship] only Him who created me, and He will certainly guide me
t.shakir: Save Him Who created me, for surely He will guide me
t.haleem: I worship only Him who created me, and it is He who will guide me,’
t.maariful: except the One who has originated me; so He will guide me.”
t.khattab: except the One Who originated me, and He will surely guide me.”
t.ghali: Except Him Who originated me; so surely He will soon guide me
t.irving: Only the One Who originated me will guide me
t.iwq: Except for He who created me; and verily, He will guide me

t_id.kemenag: tetapi (aku menyembah) Tuhan Yang menjadikanku; karena sesungguhnya Dia akan memberi hidayah kepadaku".
t_id.sabiq: Aku hanya menyembah Allah, Tuhan yang telah menciptakanku, karena hanya Dialah yang akan menunjukkanku ke jalan yang benar."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tetapi aku menyembah Tuhan Yang menjadikanku) menyembah Allah yang telah menciptakan aku (karena sesungguhnya Dia akan memberi taufik kepadaku") artinya Dia pasti membimbingku kepada agama-Nya.
t_id.iwq: tetapi (aku menyembah) Tuhan Yang menjadikanku; karena sesungguhnya Dia akan memberi hidayah kepadaku".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إلا الذي فطرني» خلقني «فإنه سيهدين» يرشدني لدينه
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِي فَطَرَنِي فَإِنَّهُۥ سَيَهۡدِينِ (فَطَرَنِي: خَلَقَنِي)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tetapi aku menyembah Tuhan Yang menjadikanku) menyembah Allah yang telah menciptakan aku (karena sesungguhnya Dia akan memberi taufik kepadaku") artinya Dia pasti membimbingku kepada agama-Nya

ar: وَجَعَلَهَا كَلِمَةَۢ بَاقِیَةࣰ فِی عَقِبِهِۦ لَعَلَّهُمۡ یَرۡجِعُونَ
ar_simple: وَجَعَلَهَا كَلِمَةً بَاقِيَةً فِي عَقِبِهِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
transliteration: wajaʿalahā kalimatan bāqiyatan fī ʿaqibihi laʿallahum yarjiʿūna

t.sahih-int: And he made it a word remaining among his descendants that they might return [to it]
t.asad: And he uttered this as a word destined to endure among those who would come after him, so that they might [always] return [to it]
t.yusuf-ali: And he left it as a Word to endure among those who came after him, that they may turn back (to Allah)
t.pickthall: And he made it a word enduring among his seed, that haply they might return
t.arberry: And he made it a word enduring among his posterity; haply so they would return
t.hilali-khan: And he made it [i.e. La ilaha ill-Allah (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah Alone)] a Word lasting among his offspring (True Monotheism), that they may turn back (i.e. to repent to Allah or receive admonition)
t.itani: And he made it an enduring word in his progeny, so that they may return.
t.mubarakpuri: And he made it a word lasting among his offspring, that they may turn back.
t.qarib: He made this as an enduring word remaining among Abraham's descendants that they might return [to it]
t.wahiduddin: and he left these words to endure among his descendants, so that they might return [to God]
t.shakir: And he made it a word to continue in his posterity that they may return
t.haleem: and he bequeathed these words to his descendants so that they might return [to God]
t.maariful: He made it a word lasting among his posterity, so that they may return
t.khattab: And he left this enduring declaration among his descendants, so they may ˹always˺ turn back ˹to Allah˺
t.ghali: And he made it a word enduring among his posterity that possibly they would return
t.irving: He left it as an enduring word with his posterity so they might return (to it)
t.iwq: And he made it a word remaining among his descendants that they might return to it

t_id.kemenag: Dan (lbrahim a. s.) menjadikan kalimat tauhid itu kalimat yang kekal pada keturunannya supaya mereka kembali kepada kalimat tauhid itu.
t_id.sabiq: Dengan mendeklarasikan pernyataan tauhid itu, Ibrâhîm mengabadikan ucapannya untuk anak keturunannya, dengan harapan semoga mereka kembali dan mempercayai pernyataan itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Ibrahim menjadikannya) kalimat tauhid, yang tersimpul dari perkataannya, sebagaimana yang disitir oleh firman-Nya, "Sesungguhnya aku pergi menghadap kepada Rabbku, dan Dia akan memberi petunjuk kepadaku..." (Q.S. Ash shaffat, 99). (sebagai kalimat yang kekal pada keturunannya) pada anak cucunya, maka tetap akan ada orang-orang yang mengesakan Allah di antara keturunannya itu (supaya mereka) penduduk Mekah (kembali) meninggalkan apa yang biasa mereka lakukan, yaitu menyembah berhala, kemudian memeluk agama bapak moyang mereka, yakni Nabi Ibrahim.
t_id.iwq: Dan (lbrahim a. s.) menjadikan kalimat tauhid itu kalimat yang kekal pada keturunannya supaya mereka kembali kepada kalimat tauhid itu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (وجعلها) أي كلمة التوحيد المفهومة من قوله "" إني ذاهب إلى ربي سيهدين "" (كلمة باقية في عقبه) ذريته فلا يزال فيهم من يوحد الله (لعلهم) أي أهل مكة (يرجعون) عما هم عليه إلى دين إبراهيم أبيهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَجَعَلَهَا كَلِمَةَۢ بَاقِيَةٗ فِي عَقِبِهِۦ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَرۡجِعُونَ (كَلِمَةً بَاقِيَةً: أَيْ: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللُه: بَاقِيَةٌ, عَقِبِهِ: ذُرِّيَّتِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Ibrahim menjadikannya) kalimat tauhid, yang tersimpul dari perkataannya, sebagaimana yang disitir oleh firman-Nya, "Sesungguhnya aku pergi menghadap kepada Rabbku, dan Dia akan memberi petunjuk kepadaku..." (Q.S. Ash shaffat, 99). (sebagai kalimat yang kekal pada keturunannya) pada anak cucunya, maka tetap akan ada orang-orang yang mengesakan Allah di antara keturunannya itu (supaya mereka) penduduk Mekah (kembali) meninggalkan apa yang biasa mereka lakukan, yaitu menyembah berhala, kemudian memeluk agama bapak moyang mereka, yakni Nabi Ibrahim

ar: بَلۡ مَتَّعۡتُ هَـٰۤؤُلَاۤءِ وَءَابَاۤءَهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ جَاۤءَهُمُ ٱلۡحَقُّ وَرَسُولࣱ مُّبِینࣱ
ar_simple: بَلْ مَتَّعْتُ هَٰؤُلَاءِ وَآبَاءَهُمْ حَتَّىٰ جَاءَهُمُ الْحَقُّ وَرَسُولٌ مُبِينٌ
transliteration: bal mattaʿtu hāulāi waābāahum ḥattā jāahumu l-ḥaqu warasūlun mubīnun

t.sahih-int: However, I gave enjoyment to these [people of Makkah] and their fathers until there came to them the truth and a clear Messenger
t.asad: Now [as for those who did come after him,] I allowed them - as [I had allowed] their forebears - to enjoy their lives freely until the truth should come unto them through an apostle who would make all things clear
t.yusuf-ali: Yea, I have given the good things of this life to these (men) and their fathers, until the Truth has come to them, and a messenger making things clear
t.pickthall: Nay, but I let these and their fathers enjoy life (only) till there should come unto them the Truth and a messenger making plain
t.arberry: Nay, but I gave these and their fathers enjoyment of days, until the truth came unto them, and a manifest Messenger
t.hilali-khan: Nay, but I gave (the good things of this life) to these (polytheists) and their fathers to enjoy, till there came to them the truth (the Quran), and a Messenger (Muhammad SAW) making things clear
t.itani: I gave these and their forefathers some enjoyment, until the truth and a manifest messenger came to them.
t.mubarakpuri: Nay, but I gave to these and their fathers to enjoy, till there came to them the truth, and a Messenger making things clear.
t.qarib: Now, I let these people of yours [Prophet] and their forefathers have some enjoyment, until the Truth and a messenger who made things clear came to them
t.wahiduddin: Yes, I gave the good things of this life to these [men] and their fathers, until the truth came to them, and a messenger expounding things clearly
t.shakir: Nay! I gave them and their fathers to enjoy until there came to them the truth and an Apostle making manifest (the truth)
t.haleem: I have let these people and their fathers enjoy long lives, and now I have given them the Truth and a messenger to make things clear
t.maariful: But, I gave these and their fathers enjoyment, until the truth came to them, and a messenger as well who explains (it)
t.khattab: In fact, I had allowed enjoyment for these ˹Meccans˺ and their forefathers, until the truth came to them along with a messenger making things clear
t.ghali: No indeed, (but) I made these and their fathers enjoy (life) until the Truth came to them and an evident Messenger
t.irving: Indeed I have let those (men) as well as their forefathers enjoy things until Truth and a plain messenger should come to them
t.iwq: However, I gave enjoyment to these people of Makkah and their fathers until there came to them the truth and a clear Messenger

t_id.kemenag: Tetapi Aku telah memberikan kenikmatan hidup kepada mereka dan bapak-bapak mereka sehingga datanglah kepada mereka kebenaran (Al Quran) dan seorang rasul yang memberi penjelasan.
t_id.sabiq: Orang-orang musyrik tidak memenuhi harapan Ibrâhîm, dan Aku pun tidak mempercepat hukuman untuk mereka. Mereka yang hidup pada zamanmu sekarang, wahai Muhammad, Aku berikan berbagai anugerah dan kenikmatan seperti halnya leluhur-leluhur mereka dahulu, sampai turunnya al-Qur'ân yang mengajak kepada kebenaran dan sampai datang kepada mereka seorang rasul yang menjelaskan dan mengajak untuk mengimaninya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tetapi Aku telah memberikan kenikmatan hidup kepada mereka) kepada orang-orang musyrik itu (dan bapak-bapak mereka) dan Aku tidak menyegerakan hukuman-Ku kepada mereka (sehingga datanglah kebenaran kepada mereka) Alquran yang membawa kebenaran (dan seorang rasul yang memberi penjelasan) yang menampakkan kepada mereka hukum-hukum syariat, yaitu Nabi Muhammad saw.
t_id.iwq: Tetapi Aku telah memberikan kenikmatan hidup kepada mereka dan bapak-bapak mereka sehingga datanglah kepada mereka kebenaran (Al Al-Quran) dan seorang rasul yang memberi penjelasan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «بل متعت هؤلاء» المشركين «وآباءهم» ولم أعاجلهم بالعقوبة «حتى جاءهم الحق» القرآن «ورسول مبين» مظهر لهم الأحكام الشرعية، وهو محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم
tafsir.siraj-ar: بَلۡ مَتَّعۡتُ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَءَابَآءَهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ جَآءَهُمُ ٱلۡحَقُّ وَرَسُولٞ مُّبِينٞ (مَتَّعْتُ هَؤُلَاءِ: لَمْ أُعَاجِلْهُمْ بِالعُقُوبَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tetapi Aku telah memberikan kenikmatan hidup kepada mereka) kepada orang-orang musyrik itu (dan bapak-bapak mereka) dan Aku tidak menyegerakan hukuman-Ku kepada mereka (sehingga datanglah kebenaran kepada mereka) Alquran yang membawa kebenaran (dan seorang rasul yang memberi penjelasan) yang menampakkan kepada mereka hukum-hukum syariat, yaitu Nabi Muhammad saw

ar: وَلَمَّا جَاۤءَهُمُ ٱلۡحَقُّ قَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَا سِحۡرࣱ وَإِنَّا بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ
ar_simple: وَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمُ الْحَقُّ قَالُوا هَٰذَا سِحْرٌ وَإِنَّا بِهِ كَافِرُونَ
transliteration: walammā jāahumu l-ḥaqu qālū hādhā siḥ'run wa-innā bihi kāfirūna

t.sahih-int: But when the truth came to them, they said, "This is magic, and indeed we are, concerning it, disbelievers
t.asad: but now that the truth has come to them, they say, “All this is mere spellbinding eloquence” - and, behold, we deny that there is any truth in it!”
t.yusuf-ali: But when the Truth came to them, they said: "This is sorcery, and we do reject it
t.pickthall: And now that the Truth hath come unto them they say: This is mere magic, and lo! we are disbelievers therein
t.arberry: And when the truth came to them, they said, 'This is a sorcery, and in it we are unbelievers
t.hilali-khan: And when the truth (this Quran) came to them, they (the disbelievers in this Quran) said: "This is magic, and we disbelieve therein
t.itani: But when the truth came to them, they said, “This is sorcery, and we refuse to believe in it.”
t.mubarakpuri: And when the truth came to them, they said: "This is magic, and we disbelieve therein."
t.qarib: But when the Truth came to them, they said, "This is magic and we refuse to believe it
t.wahiduddin: but when the truth came to them, they said, "This is sorcery, and we reject it
t.shakir: And when there came to them the truth they said: This is magic, and surely we are disbelievers in it
t.haleem: yet when the Truth came to them, they said, ‘This is sorcery. We do not believe in it,’
t.maariful: But when the truth came to them, they said, “This is magic, and we totally disbelieve in it.”
t.khattab: ˹But˺ when the truth came to them, they said, “This is magic, and we totally reject it.”
t.ghali: And as soon as the Truth came to them, they said, "This is a sorcery, and surely in it we are disbelievers
t.irving: Yet whenever Truth did come to them, they said: "This is magic! We disbelieve in it
t.iwq: But when the truth came to them, they said, "This is magic, and verily we are, concerning it, disbelievers

t_id.kemenag: Dan tatkala kebenaran (Al Quran) itu datang kepada mereka, mereka berkata: "Ini adalah sihir dan sesungguhnya kami adalah orang-orang yang mengingkarinya".
t_id.sabiq: Dan ketika al-Qur'ân itu diturunkan kepada mereka untuk membimbing mereka ke jalan tauhid, kitab suci itu malah mereka masukkan ke dalam kesyirikan mereka dengan menyebutnya, dengan nada menghina, sebagai sihir dan kamuflase. Mereka tetap berada dalam kakafiran.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tatkala kebenaran itu datang kepada mereka) yakni Alquran (mereka berkata, "Ini adalah sihir dan sesungguhnya kami adalah orang-orang yang mengingkarinya.")
t_id.iwq: Dan tatkala kebenaran (Al Al-Quran) itu datang kepada mereka, mereka berkata: "Ini adalah sihir dan sesungguhnya kami adalah orang-orang yang mengingkarinya".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولما جاءهم الحق» القرآن «قالوا هذا سحر وإنا به كافرون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلۡحَقُّ قَالُواْ هَٰذَا سِحۡرٞ وَإِنَّا بِهِۦ كَٰفِرُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tatkala kebenaran itu datang kepada mereka) yakni Alquran (mereka berkata, "Ini adalah sihir dan sesungguhnya kami adalah orang-orang yang mengingkarinya)

ar: وَقَالُوا۟ لَوۡلَا نُزِّلَ هَـٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانُ عَلَىٰ رَجُلࣲ مِّنَ ٱلۡقَرۡیَتَیۡنِ عَظِیمٍ
ar_simple: وَقَالُوا لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنُ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ
transliteration: waqālū lawlā nuzzila hādhā l-qur'ānu ʿalā rajulin mina l-qaryatayni ʿaẓīmin

t.sahih-int: And they said, "Why was this Qur'an not sent down upon a great man from [one of] the two cities
t.asad: And they say, too, “Why was not this Qur’an bestowed from on high on some great man of the two cities?”
t.yusuf-ali: Also, they say: "Why is not this Qur'an sent down to some leading man in either of the two (chief) cities
t.pickthall: And they say: If only this Qur'an had been revealed to some great man of the two towns
t.arberry: They say, 'Why was this Koran not sent down upon some man of moment in the two cities
t.hilali-khan: And they say: "Why is not this Quran sent down to some great man of the two towns (Makkah and Ta'if)
t.itani: They also said, “If only this Quran was sent down to a man of importance from the two cities.”
t.mubarakpuri: And they say: "Why is not this Qur'an sent down to some great man of the two towns"
t.qarib: They also said, "If only this Qur'an was sent down to a great man from the two cities
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Why was this Quran not sent down to one of the great men of the two cities
t.shakir: And they say: Why was not this Quran revealed to a man of importance in the two towns
t.haleem: and they said, ‘Why was this Quran not sent down to a distinguished man, from either of the two cities?’
t.maariful: They say, “Why was this Qur’ān not revealed on a great man from (either of) the two towns?”
t.khattab: And they exclaimed, “If only this Quran was revealed to a great man from ˹one of˺ the two cities!”
t.ghali: And they have said, "Had this Qur'an been successively sent down upon a man of eminence in the two towns?" (Makkah and At-Taif)
t.irving: They said: ´If this Reading were only sent down to some important man from either town
t.iwq: And they said, "Why was this Qur'an not sent down upon a great man from one of the two cities

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka berkata: "Mengapa Al Quran ini tidak diturunkan kepada seorang besar dari salah satu dua negeri (Mekah dan Thaif) ini?"
t_id.sabiq: Dengan maksud menganggap remeh pribadi Muhammad dan dengan menganggap bahwa turunnya al-Qur'ân itu terlalu berat untuk seseorang seperti dia, orang-orang musyrik berkata, "Mengapa al-Qur'ân yang dianggap sebagai wahyu Allah itu tidak diturunkan kepada seorang tokoh pembesar dari Mekah atau Thaif?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata, "Mengapa tidak) kenapa tidak (diturunkan Alquran ini kepada seorang besar dari) kalangan penduduk (da negeri) yakni Mekah dan Madinah, maksudnya dari salah satu antara keduanya (yang besar ini?") yang dimaksud oleh mereka adalah Al Walid Ibnu Mughirah di Mekah, atau Urwah ibnu Mas'ud Ats Tsaqafi di Thaif.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka berkata: "Mengapa Al Al-Quran ini tidak diturunkan kepada seorang besar dari salat satu doa negeri (Mekah dan Thaif) ini?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقالوا لولا» هلا «نزل هذا القرآن على رجل من» أهل «القريتين» من أية منهما «عظيم» أي الوليد بن المغيرة بمكة أو عروة بن مسعود الثقفي بالطائف
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالُواْ لَوۡلَا نُزِّلَ هَٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانُ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٖ مِّنَ ٱلۡقَرۡيَتَيۡنِ عَظِيمٍ (لَوْلَا: هَلَّا, الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ: مَكَّةَ، وَالطَّائِفِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata, "Mengapa tidak) kenapa tidak (diturunkan Alquran ini kepada seorang besar dari) kalangan penduduk (da negeri) yakni Mekah dan Madinah, maksudnya dari salah satu antara keduanya (yang besar ini?") yang dimaksud oleh mereka adalah Al Walid Ibnu Mughirah di Mekah, atau Urwah ibnu Mas'ud Ats Tsaqafi di Thaif

ar: أَهُمۡ یَقۡسِمُونَ رَحۡمَتَ رَبِّكَۚ نَحۡنُ قَسَمۡنَا بَیۡنَهُم مَّعِیشَتَهُمۡ فِی ٱلۡحَیَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنۡیَاۚ وَرَفَعۡنَا بَعۡضَهُمۡ فَوۡقَ بَعۡضࣲ دَرَجَـٰتࣲ لِّیَتَّخِذَ بَعۡضُهُم بَعۡضࣰا سُخۡرِیࣰّاۗ وَرَحۡمَتُ رَبِّكَ خَیۡرࣱ مِّمَّا یَجۡمَعُونَ
ar_simple: أَهُمْ يَقْسِمُونَ رَحْمَتَ رَبِّكَ ۚ نَحْنُ قَسَمْنَا بَيْنَهُمْ مَعِيشَتَهُمْ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ۚ وَرَفَعْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجَاتٍ لِيَتَّخِذَ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا سُخْرِيًّا ۗ وَرَحْمَتُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ مِمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ
transliteration: ahum yaqsimūna raḥmata rabbika naḥnu qasamnā baynahum maʿīshatahum fī l-ḥayati l-dun'yā warafaʿnā baʿḍahum fawqa baʿḍin darajātin liyattakhidha baʿḍuhum baʿḍan sukh'riyyan waraḥmatu rabbika khayrun mimmā yajmaʿūna

t.sahih-int: Do they distribute the mercy of your Lord? It is We who have apportioned among them their livelihood in the life of this world and have raised some of them above others in degrees [of rank] that they may make use of one another for service. But the mercy of your Lord is better than whatever they accumulate
t.asad: But is it they who distribute thy Sustainer’s grace? [Nay, as] it is We who distribute their means of livelihood among them in the life of this world, and raise some of them by degrees above others, to the end that they might avail themselves of one another’s help - [so, too, it is We who bestow gifts of the spirit upon whomever We will]: and this thy Sustainer’s grace is better than all [the worldly wealth] that they may amass
t.yusuf-ali: Is it they who would portion out the Mercy of thy Lord? It is We Who portion out between them their livelihood in the life of this world: and We raise some of them above others in ranks, so that some may command work from others. But the Mercy of thy Lord is better than the (wealth) which they amass
t.pickthall: Is it they who apportion thy Lord's mercy? We have apportioned among them their livelihood in the life of the world, and raised some of them above others in rank that some of them may take labour from others; and the mercy of thy Lord is better than (the wealth) that they amass
t.arberry: What, is it they who divide the mercy of the Lord? We have divided between them their livelihood in the present life, and raised some of them above others in rank, that some of them may take others in servitude; and the mercy of thy Lord is better than that they amass
t.hilali-khan: Is it they who would portion out the Mercy of your Lord? It is We Who portion out between them their livelihood in this world, and We raised some of them above others in ranks, so that some may employ others in their work. But the Mercy (Paradise) of your Lord (O Muhammad SAW) is better than the (wealth of this world) which they amass
t.itani: Is it they who allocate the mercy of your Lord? It is We who have allocated their livelihood in this life, and We elevated some of them in rank above others, that some of them would take others in service. But your Lord’s mercy is better than what they amass.
t.mubarakpuri: Is it they who would portion out the mercy of your Lord It is We Who portion out between them their livelihood in this world, and We raised some of them above others in ranks, so that some may employ others in their work. But the mercy of your Lord is better than what they amass.
t.qarib: Is it they who distribute your Lord's blessing? It is We who have allocated their livelihood among them in this life. We have raised some of them in rank, so that some would hire others for service. But your Lord's mercy is better than whatever they accumulate
t.wahiduddin: Is it they who apportion the mercy of your Lord? It is We who distribute among them their livelihood in the life of this world, and raise some of them above others in rank, so that they may take one another into service; and the mercy of your Lord is better than [the wealth] which they amass
t.shakir: Will they distribute the mercy of your Lord? We distribute among them their livelihood in the life of this world, and We j have exalted some of them above others in degrees, that some of them may take others in subjection; and the mercy of your Lord is better than what they amass
t.haleem: Are they the ones who share out your Lord’s grace? We are the ones who give them their share of livelihood in this world and We have raised some of them above others in rank, so that some may take others into service: your Lord’s grace is better than anything they accumulate
t.maariful: Is it they who allocate the mercy of your Lord? We have allocated among them their livelihood in the worldly life, and have raised some of them over others in ranks, so that some of them may put some others to work. And the mercy of your Lord is much better than what they accumulate
t.khattab: Is it they who distribute your Lord’s mercy? We ˹alone˺ have distributed their ˹very˺ livelihood among them in this worldly life and raised some of them in rank above others so that some may employ others in service. ˹But˺ your Lord’s mercy is far better than whatever ˹wealth˺ they amass
t.ghali: Is it they who divide the mercy of your Lord? We have divided among them their subsistence in the present life (Literally: the lowly life, i.e., the life of this world) and raised some of them above (some) others in degrees, that some of them may take to themselves (some) others in subjection; and the mercy of your Lord is more charitable (i.e., better) than whatever they heap (Literally: gather) together
t.irving: Are they dividing up your Lord´s mercy? We divide up their livelihood among them during worldly life, and raise some of them higher in rank than others so that some of them may take advantage of the labor of others. Your Lord´s mercy is better than what they are collecting
t.iwq: Do they distribute the mercy of your Lord? It is We who have apportioned among them their livelihood in the life of this world and have raised some of them above others in degrees of rank that they may make use of one another for service. But the mercy of your Lord is better than whatever they accumulate

t_id.kemenag: Apakah mereka yang membagi-bagi rahmat Tuhanmu? Kami telah menentukan antara mereka penghidupan mereka dalam kehidupan dunia, dan kami telah meninggikan sebahagian mereka atas sebagian yang lain beberapa derajat, agar sebagian mereka dapat mempergunakan sebagian yang lain. Dan rahmat Tuhanmu lebih baik dari apa yang mereka kumpulkan.
t_id.sabiq: Orang-orang musyrik itu tidak memiliki kunci risalah sehingga dengan seenaknya memberikan risalah kepada tokoh mereka. Bahkan Kamilah yang menanggung penghidupan mereka karena mereka tidak mampu melakukan sendiri hal itu. Sebagian mereka Kami berikan rezeki dan kedudukan lebih banyak dan lebih baik dari yang lain, agar mereka dapat saling menolong dalam memenuhi kebutuhan hidupnya. Masing-masing menopang yang lain dalam mencari penghidupan dan mengatur kehidupan. Dan karunia kenabian, dengan kebahagiaan di dunia dan akhirat sebagai konsekwensinya, jauh lebih baik dari kedudukan yang paling tinggi di dunia sekalipun.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Apakah mereka yang membagi-bagi rahmat Rabbmu?) yang dimaksud dengan rahmat adalah kenabian (Kami telah menentukan antara mereka penghidupan mereka dalam kehidupan dunia) maka Kami jadikan sebagian dari mereka kaya dan sebagian lainnya miskin (dan Kami telah meninggikan sebagian mereka) dengan diberi kekayaan (atas sebagian yang lain beberapa derajat, agar sebagian mereka dapat mempergunakan) golongan orang-orang yang berkecukupan (sebagian yang lain) atas golongan orang-orang yang miskin (sebagai pekerja) maksudnya, pekerja berupah; huruf Ya di sini menunjukkan makna Nasab, dan menurut suatu qiraat lafal Sukhriyyan dibaca Sikhriyyan yaitu dengan dikasrahkan huruf Sin-nya (Dan rahmat Rabbmu) yakni surga Rabbmu (lebih baik daripada apa yang mereka kumpulkan) di dunia.
t_id.iwq: Apakah mereka yang membagi-bagi rahmat Tuhanmu? Kami telah menentukan antara mereka penghidupan mereka dalam kehidupan dunia, dan kami telah meninggikan sebahagian mereka atas sebagian yang lain beberapa derajat, agar sebagian mereka dapat mempergunakan sebagian yang lain. Dan rahmat Tuhanmu lebih baik dari apa yang mereka kumpulkan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أهم يقسمون رحمة ربك» النبوة «نحن قسمنا بينهم معيشتهم في الحياة الدنيا» فجعلنا بعضهم غنيا وبعضهم فقيراً «ورفعنا بعضهم» بالغنى «فوق بعض درجات ليتخذ بعضهم» الغني «بعضاً» الفقير «سخريا» مسخراً في العمل له بالأجرة، والياء للنسب، وقرئ بكسر السين «ورحمة ربك» أي الجنة «خير مما يجمعون» في الدنيا
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَهُمۡ يَقۡسِمُونَ رَحۡمَتَ رَبِّكَۚ نَحۡنُ قَسَمۡنَا بَيۡنَهُم مَّعِيشَتَهُمۡ فِي ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنۡيَاۚ وَرَفَعۡنَا بَعۡضَهُمۡ فَوۡقَ بَعۡضٖ دَرَجَٰتٖ لِّيَتَّخِذَ بَعۡضُهُم بَعۡضٗا سُخۡرِيّٗاۗ وَرَحۡمَتُ رَبِّكَ خَيۡرٞ مِّمَّا يَجۡمَعُونَ (رَحْمَةَ رَبِّكَ: النُّبُوَّةَ, سُخْرِيًّا: مُسَخَّرًا فِي العَمَلِ, وَرَحْمَتُ رَبِّكَ: الجَنَّةُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Apakah mereka yang membagi-bagi rahmat Rabbmu?) yang dimaksud dengan rahmat adalah kenabian (Kami telah menentukan antara mereka penghidupan mereka dalam kehidupan dunia) maka Kami jadikan sebagian dari mereka kaya dan sebagian lainnya miskin (dan Kami telah meninggikan sebagian mereka) dengan diberi kekayaan (atas sebagian yang lain beberapa derajat, agar sebagian mereka dapat mempergunakan) golongan orang-orang yang berkecukupan (sebagian yang lain) atas golongan orang-orang yang miskin (sebagai pekerja) maksudnya, pekerja berupah; huruf Ya di sini menunjukkan makna Nasab, dan menurut suatu qiraat lafal Sukhriyyan dibaca Sikhriyyan yaitu dengan dikasrahkan huruf Sin-nya (Dan rahmat Rabbmu) yakni surga Rabbmu (lebih baik daripada apa yang mereka kumpulkan) di dunia

ar: وَلَوۡلَاۤ أَن یَكُونَ ٱلنَّاسُ أُمَّةࣰ وَ ٰحِدَةࣰ لَّجَعَلۡنَا لِمَن یَكۡفُرُ بِٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ لِبُیُوتِهِمۡ سُقُفࣰا مِّن فِضَّةࣲ وَمَعَارِجَ عَلَیۡهَا یَظۡهَرُونَ
ar_simple: وَلَوْلَا أَنْ يَكُونَ النَّاسُ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً لَجَعَلْنَا لِمَنْ يَكْفُرُ بِالرَّحْمَٰنِ لِبُيُوتِهِمْ سُقُفًا مِنْ فِضَّةٍ وَمَعَارِجَ عَلَيْهَا يَظْهَرُونَ
transliteration: walawlā an yakūna l-nāsu ummatan wāḥidatan lajaʿalnā liman yakfuru bil-raḥmāni libuyūtihim suqufan min fiḍḍatin wamaʿārija ʿalayhā yaẓharūna

t.sahih-int: And if it were not that the people would become one community [of disbelievers], We would have made for those who disbelieve in the Most Merciful - for their houses - ceilings and stairways of silver upon which to mount
t.asad: And were it not that [with the prospect of boundless riches before them] all people would become one [evil] community, We might indeed have provided for those who [now] deny the Most Gracious roofs of silver for their houses, and [silver] stairways whereon to ascend
t.yusuf-ali: And were it not that (all) men might become of one (evil) way of life, We would provide, for everyone that blasphemes against (Allah) Most Gracious, silver roofs for their houses and (silver) stair-ways on which to go up
t.pickthall: And were it not that mankind would have become one community, We might well have appointed, for those who disbelieve in the Beneficent, roofs of silver for their houses and stairs (of silver) whereby to mount
t.arberry: And were it not that mankind would be one nation, We would have appointed for those who disbelieve in the All-merciful roofs of silver to their houses, and stairs whereon to mount
t.hilali-khan: And were it not that all mankind would have become of one community (all disbelievers, desiring worldly life only), We would have provided for those who disbelieve in the Most Beneficent (Allah), silver roofs for their houses, and elevators (and stair-ways, etc. of silver) whereby they ascend
t.itani: Were it not that humanity would become a single community, We would have provided those who disbelieve in the Most Gracious with roofs of silver to their houses, and stairways by which they ascend.
t.mubarakpuri: And were it not that mankind would have become of one community, We would have provided for those who disbelieve in the Most Gracious, silver roofs for their houses, and elevators whereby they ascend,
t.qarib: Were it not that people might become a single community, We would have provided those who deny the Merciful-to-all, silver roofs for their houses and silver stairways to ascend
t.wahiduddin: If it were not that all mankind might have become one community [of disbelievers], We could have given all those who deny the Lord of Mercy, houses with roofs of silver, silver staircases to ascend
t.shakir: And were it not that all people had been a single nation, We would certainly have assigned to those who disbelieve in the Beneficent Allah (to make) of silver the roofs of their houses and the stairs by which they ascend
t.haleem: If it were not that all mankind might have become a single nation [of disbelievers], We could have given all those who disbelieve in the Lord of Mercy houses with roofs of silver, sweeping staircases to ascend
t.maariful: Were it not that all people would become of a single creed (i.e. disbelief), We would have caused, for those who disbelieve in RaHmān, roofs of their houses to be made of silver, and the stairs as well, on which they would climb
t.khattab: Were it not that people might ˹be tempted to˺ become one community ˹of disbelievers˺, We would have supplied the homes of ˹only˺ those who disbelieve in the Most Compassionate with silver roofs and ˹silver˺ stairways to ascend
t.ghali: And had it not been that mankind would be one nation, We would indeed have made for whomever disbelieve in The All-Merciful roofs of silver to their homes, and stairways whereon they mount
t.irving: If it were not that mankind has been a single nation, We would grant anyone who disbelieves in the Mercy-giving silver roofs for their houses and staircases to climb up on
t.iwq: And if it were not that the people would become one community of disbelievers, We would have made for those who disbelieve in the Ever-Merciful - for their houses - ceilings and stairways of silver upon which to mount

t_id.kemenag: Dan sekiranya bukan karena hendak menghindari manusia menjadi umat yang satu (dalam kekafiran), tentulah kami buatkan bagi orang-orang yang kafir kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah loteng-loteng perak bagi rumah mereka dan (juga) tangga-tangga (perak) yang mereka menaikinya.
t_id.sabiq: Kalau bukan karena Kami tidak menginginkan semua orang menjadi kafir ketika melihat bahwa orang-orang kafir itu mendapat rezeki yang amat luas, tentu Kami akan menjadikan atap dan tangga rumah orang-orang kafir itu terbuat dari perak, karena begitu rendah dan sepelenya dunia bagi Kami.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sekiranya bukan karena hendak menghindari manusia menjadi umat yang satu) dalam kekafiran (tentulah Kami buatkan bagi orang-orang yang kafir kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah bagi rumah-rumah mereka) lafal Libuyutihim menjadi Badal dari lafal Liman (loteng-loteng) dapat dibaca Saqfan atau Suqfan keduanya adalah bentuk jamak (dari perak dan juga tangga-tangga) dari perak pula (yang mereka menaikinya) yang dapat mereka naiki untuk mencapai atap rumah-rumah mereka.
t_id.iwq: Dan sekiranya bukan karena hendak menghindari manusia menjadi umat yang satu (dalam kekafiran), tentulah kami buatkan bagi orang-orang yang kafir kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah loteng-loteng perak bagi rumah mereka dan (juga) tangga-tangga (perak) yang mereka menaikinya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولولا أن يكون الناس أمة واحدة» على الكفر «لجعلنا لمن يكفر بالرحمن لبيوتهم» بدل من لمن «سَقْفاً» بفتح السين وسكون القاف وبضمهما جمعاً «من فضة ومعارج» كالدرج من فضة «عليها يظهرون» يعلون إلى السطح
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَوۡلَآ أَن يَكُونَ ٱلنَّاسُ أُمَّةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ لَّجَعَلۡنَا لِمَن يَكۡفُرُ بِٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ لِبُيُوتِهِمۡ سُقُفٗا مِّن فِضَّةٖ وَمَعَارِجَ عَلَيۡهَا يَظۡهَرُونَ (أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً: جَمَاعَةً وَاحِدَةً عَلَى الكُفْرِ, وَمَعَارِجَ: سَلَالِمَ مِنْ فِضَّةٍ, يَظْهَرُونَ: يَصْعَدُونَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sekiranya bukan karena hendak menghindari manusia menjadi umat yang satu) dalam kekafiran (tentulah Kami buatkan bagi orang-orang yang kafir kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah bagi rumah-rumah mereka) lafal Libuyutihim menjadi Badal dari lafal Liman (loteng-loteng) dapat dibaca Saqfan atau Suqfan keduanya adalah bentuk jamak (dari perak dan juga tangga-tangga) dari perak pula (yang mereka menaikinya) yang dapat mereka naiki untuk mencapai atap rumah-rumah mereka

ar: وَلِبُیُوتِهِمۡ أَبۡوَ ٰبࣰا وَسُرُرًا عَلَیۡهَا یَتَّكِءُونَ
ar_simple: وَلِبُيُوتِهِمْ أَبْوَابًا وَسُرُرًا عَلَيْهَا يَتَّكِئُونَ
transliteration: walibuyūtihim abwāban wasururan ʿalayhā yattakiūna

t.sahih-int: And for their houses - doors and couches [of silver] upon which to recline
t.asad: and [silver] doors for their houses, and [silver] couches whereon to recline
t.yusuf-ali: And (silver) doors to their houses, and thrones (of silver) on which they could recline
t.pickthall: And for their houses doors (of silver) and couches of silver whereon to recline
t.arberry: and doors to their houses, and couches whereon to recline
t.hilali-khan: And for their houses, doors (of silver), and thrones (of silver) on which they could recline
t.itani: And doors to their houses, and furnishings on which they recline.
t.mubarakpuri: And for their houses, doors, and thrones on which they could recline,
t.qarib: and silver doors to their houses and furnishings on which to recline
t.wahiduddin: and silver doors to their houses and silver couches on which to recline
t.shakir: And the doors of their houses and the couches on which they recline
t.haleem: massive gates, couches to sit on
t.maariful: and doors of their homes, and the coaches on which they would recline
t.khattab: as well as ˹silver˺ gates and thrones to recline on
t.ghali: And (silver) doors to their homes, and settees (of silver) whereon they recline
t.irving: and doors for their houses and couches to recline on
t.iwq: And for their houses - doors and couches of silver upon which to recline

t_id.kemenag: Dan (Kami buatkan pula) pintu-pintu (perak) bagi rumah-rumah mereka dan (begitu pula) dipan-dipan yang mereka bertelekan atasnya.
t_id.sabiq: Tentu Kami akan menjadikan pintu dan dipan rumah mereka dari perak pula untuk mereka nikmati dan sebagai tempat mereka bersandar. Dan tentu Kami akan mejadikan segalanya untuk perhiasan mereka. Semua kenikmatan hidup yang Kami gambarkan kepadamu itu hanyalah kenikmatan yang bersifat sementara, terbatas hanya pada kehidupan dunia. Dan pahala akhirat yang ada di sisi Tuhan Sang Pencipta dan Pemelihara, hanya disediakan untuk orang-orang yang menjauhi syirik dan dosa-dosa besar.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami buatkan pula -pintu-pintu bagi rumah-rumah mereka) yang juga terbuat dari perak (dan) begitu pula Kami buatkan untuk mereka (dipan-dipan) yang terbuat dari perak; lafal Sururan adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Sarirun artinya, ranjang atau dipan (yang mereka bertelekan atasnya.)
t_id.iwq: Dan (Kami buatkan pula) pintu-pintu (perak) bagi rumah-rumah mereka dan (begitu pula) dipan-dipan yang mereka bertelekan atasnya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولبيوتهم أبواباً» من فضة «و» جعلنا لهم «سرراً» من فضة جمع سرير «عليها يتكئون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلِبُيُوتِهِمۡ أَبۡوَٰبٗا وَسُرُرًا عَلَيۡهَا يَتَّكِـُٔونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami buatkan pula -pintu-pintu bagi rumah-rumah mereka) yang juga terbuat dari perak (dan) begitu pula Kami buatkan untuk mereka (dipan-dipan) yang terbuat dari perak; lafal Sururan adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Sarirun artinya, ranjang atau dipan (yang mereka bertelekan atasnya)

ar: وَزُخۡرُفࣰاۚ وَإِن كُلُّ ذَ ٰلِكَ لَمَّا مَتَـٰعُ ٱلۡحَیَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنۡیَاۚ وَٱلۡءَاخِرَةُ عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلۡمُتَّقِینَ
ar_simple: وَزُخْرُفًا ۚ وَإِنْ كُلُّ ذَٰلِكَ لَمَّا مَتَاعُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ۚ وَالْآخِرَةُ عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ
transliteration: wazukh'rufan wa-in kullu dhālika lammā matāʿu l-ḥayati l-dun'yā wal-ākhiratu ʿinda rabbika lil'muttaqīna

t.sahih-int: And gold ornament. But all that is not but the enjoyment of worldly life. And the Hereafter with your Lord is for the righteous
t.asad: and gold [beyond count]. Yet all this would have been nothing but a [brief] enjoyment of life in this world - whereas [happiness in] the life to come awaits the God-conscious with thy Sustainer
t.yusuf-ali: And also adornments of gold. But all this were nothing but conveniences of the present life: The Hereafter, in the sight of thy Lord is for the Righteous
t.pickthall: And ornaments of gold. Yet all that would have been but a provision of the life of the world. And the Hereafter with your Lord would have been for those who keep from evil
t.arberry: and ornaments; surely all this is but the enjoyment of the present life, and the world to come with thy Lord is for the godfearing
t.hilali-khan: And adornments of gold. Yet all this (i.e. the roofs, doors, stairs, elevators, thrones etc. of their houses) would have been nothing but an enjoyment of this world. And the Hereafter with your Lord is only for the Muttaqun
t.itani: And decorations. Yet all that is nothing but the stuff of this life. Yet the Hereafter, with your Lord, is for the righteous.
t.mubarakpuri: And adornments of gold. Yet all this would have been nothing but an enjoyment of this world. And the Hereafter with your Lord is for those who have Taqwa.
t.qarib: and gold ornaments. But all that is merely the possessions of this life, while the Hereafter with your Lord is for those who are mindful of Him
t.wahiduddin: and ornaments of gold. But all of these are but the provision of this present life; it is the life to come that the Lord reserves for those who fear Him
t.shakir: And (other) embellishments of gold; and all this is naught but provision of this world's life, and the hereafter is with your Lord only for those who guard (against evil)
t.haleem: and golden ornaments. All of these are mere enjoyments of this life; your Lord reserves the next life for those who take heed of Him
t.maariful: and (would have made some of these things) of gold-ornaments. And all this is nothing but an enjoyment of the worldly life. And the Hereafter, with your Lord, is (destined) for the God-fearing
t.khattab: and ornaments ˹of gold˺. Yet all this is no more than a ˹fleeting˺ enjoyment in this worldly life. ˹But˺ the Hereafter with your Lord is ˹only˺ for those mindful ˹of Him˺
t.ghali: And decorations. And, decidedly, as yet, this is only the enjoyment of the present life, and the Hereafter in the Providence of your Lord is for the pious
t.irving: and [similar] luxury! All that means nothing except enjoyment during worldly life while those who do their duty will [spend] the Hereafter with your Lord
t.iwq: And gold ornament. But all that is not but the enjoyment of worldly life. And the Hereafter with your Lord is for the righteous

t_id.kemenag: Dan (Kami buatkan pula) perhiasan-perhiasan (dari emas untuk mereka). Dan semuanya itu tidak lain hanyalah kesenangan kehidupan dunia, dan kehidupan akhirat itu di sisi Tuhanmu adalah bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa.
t_id.sabiq: Tentu Kami akan menjadikan pintu dan dipan rumah mereka dari perak pula untuk mereka nikmati dan sebagai tempat mereka bersandar. Dan tentu Kami akan mejadikan segalanya untuk perhiasan mereka. Semua kenikmatan hidup yang Kami gambarkan kepadamu itu hanyalah kenikmatan yang bersifat sementara, terbatas hanya pada kehidupan dunia. Dan pahala akhirat yang ada di sisi Tuhan Sang Pencipta dan Pemelihara, hanya disediakan untuk orang-orang yang menjauhi syirik dan dosa-dosa besar.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami buatkan pula perhiasan-perhiasan) dari emas untuk mereka. Makna ayat, seandainya tidak karena khawatir orang mukmin akan menjadi kafir, bila Kami anugerahkan kepadanya hal-hal tersebut sebagaimana yang telah Kami berikan kepada orang kafir, tentulah Kami akan memberikan kepada orang mukmin hal-hal itu. Karena keduniaan itu tidak ada artinya di sisi Kami, dan kelak di akhirat tidak berharga sama sekali bila dibandingkan dengan nikmat surga. (Dan sesungguhnya) lafal In di sini adalah bentuk Takhfif dari Inna yang Tsaqilah; artinya sesungguhnya (semuanya itu tiada lain) jika dibaca Lama dengan cara Takhfif, maka huruf Ma adalah Zaidah, jika dibaca Lamma dengan memakai Tasydid pada huruf Mim maknanya sama dengan lafal Illa, dan lafal In bermakna Nafi. Menurut bacaan pertama arti ayat ini ialah, "Dan sesungguhnya semuanya itu hanyalah. " Menurut bacaan kedua artinya menjadi, "Dan tiadalah semuanya itu melainkan (kesenangan kehidupan dunia) yang dapat dipakai untuk bersenang-senang kemudian lenyap sesudah itu (dan kehidupan di akhirat itu) yakni di surga (di sisi Rabbmu bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa.)
t_id.iwq: Dan (Kami buatkan pula) perhiasan-perhiasan (dari emas untuk mereka). Dan semuanya itu tidak lain hanyalah kesenangan kehidupan dunia, dan kehidupan akhirat itu di sisi Tuhanmu adalah bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وزخرفاً» ذهباً، المعنى لولا خوف الكفر على المؤمن من إعطاء الكافر ما ذكر لأعطيناه ذلك لقلة خطر الدنيا عندنا وعدم حظه في الآخرة في النعيم «وإن» مخففة من الثقيلة «كل ذلك لما» بالتخفيف فما زائدة، وبالتشديد بمعنى إلا فإن نافية «متاع الحياة الدنيا» يتمتع به فيها ثم يزول «والآخرة» الجنة «عند ربك للمتقين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَزُخۡرُفٗاۚ وَإِن كُلُّ ذَٰلِكَ لَمَّا مَتَٰعُ ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنۡيَاۚ وَٱلۡأٓخِرَةُ عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ (وَزُخْرُفًا: ذَهَبًا, وَإِن كُلُّ ذَلِكَ لَمَّا: مَا كُلُّ ذَلِكَ إِلَّا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami buatkan pula perhiasan-perhiasan) dari emas untuk mereka. Makna ayat, seandainya tidak karena khawatir orang mukmin akan menjadi kafir, bila Kami anugerahkan kepadanya hal-hal tersebut sebagaimana yang telah Kami berikan kepada orang kafir, tentulah Kami akan memberikan kepada orang mukmin hal-hal itu. Karena keduniaan itu tidak ada artinya di sisi Kami, dan kelak di akhirat tidak berharga sama sekali bila dibandingkan dengan nikmat surga. (Dan sesungguhnya) lafal In di sini adalah bentuk Takhfif dari Inna yang Tsaqilah; artinya sesungguhnya (semuanya itu tiada lain) jika dibaca Lama dengan cara Takhfif, maka huruf Ma adalah Zaidah, jika dibaca Lamma dengan memakai Tasydid pada huruf Mim maknanya sama dengan lafal Illa, dan lafal In bermakna Nafi. Menurut bacaan pertama arti ayat ini ialah, "Dan sesungguhnya semuanya itu hanyalah. " Menurut bacaan kedua artinya menjadi, "Dan tiadalah semuanya itu melainkan (kesenangan kehidupan dunia) yang dapat dipakai untuk bersenang-senang kemudian lenyap sesudah itu (dan kehidupan di akhirat itu) yakni di surga (di sisi Rabbmu bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa)

ar: وَمَن یَعۡشُ عَن ذِكۡرِ ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ نُقَیِّضۡ لَهُۥ شَیۡطَـٰنࣰا فَهُوَ لَهُۥ قَرِینࣱ
ar_simple: وَمَنْ يَعْشُ عَنْ ذِكْرِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ نُقَيِّضْ لَهُ شَيْطَانًا فَهُوَ لَهُ قَرِينٌ
transliteration: waman yaʿshu ʿan dhik'ri l-raḥmāni nuqayyiḍ lahu shayṭānan fahuwa lahu qarīnun

t.sahih-int: And whoever is blinded from remembrance of the Most Merciful - We appoint for him a devil, and he is to him a companion
t.asad: But as for anyone who chooses to remain blind to the remembrance of the Most Gracious, to him We assign an [enduring] evil impulse, to become his other self
t.yusuf-ali: If anyone withdraws himself from remembrance of (Allah) Most Gracious, We appoint for him an evil one, to be an intimate companion to him
t.pickthall: And he whose sight is dim to the remembrance of the Beneficent, We assign unto him a devil who becometh his comrade
t.arberry: Whoso blinds himself to the Remembrance of the All-merciful, to him We assign a Satan for comrade
t.hilali-khan: And whosoever turns away (blinds himself) from the remembrance of the Most Beneficent (Allah) (i.e. this Quran and worship of Allah), We appoint for him Shaitan (Satan - devil) to be a Qarin (an intimate companion) to him
t.itani: Whoever shuns the remembrance of the Most Gracious, We assign for him a devil, to be his companion.
t.mubarakpuri: And whosoever Ya`shu (turns away blindly) from the remembrance of the Most Gracious, We appoint for him Shaytan to be a Qarin (a companion) to him.
t.qarib: Whoever overlooks the remembrance of the Merciful, We assign him a devil, for a companion
t.wahiduddin: As for one who turns away from the remembrance of the Gracious God, We appoint for him a devil, who will become his intimate companion
t.shakir: And whoever turns himself away from the remembrance of the Beneficent Allah, We appoint for him a Shaitan, so he becomes his associate
t.haleem: We assign an evil one as a comrade for whoever turns away from the revelations of the Lord of Mercy
t.maariful: Whoever makes himself blind against the advice of RaHmān, We assign for him a devil who accompanies him all the time
t.khattab: And whoever turns a blind eye to the Reminder of the Most Compassionate, We place at the disposal of each a devilish one as their close associate
t.ghali: And whoever is purblind to the Remembrance of The All-Merciful, We preordain for him a Shaytan (An ever-Vicious (one), i.e., a devil) who (becomes his) comrade
t.irving: We assign anyone who fails to remember the Mercy-giving at nightfall, a devil who will become a soulmate for him
t.iwq: And whoever is blinded from remembrance of the Ever-Merciful - We appoint for him a devil, and he is to him a companion

t_id.kemenag: Barangsiapa yang berpaling dari pengajaran Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah (Al Quran), kami adakan baginya syaitan (yang menyesatkan) maka syaitan itulah yang menjadi teman yang selalu menyertainya.
t_id.sabiq: Orang yang berpura-pura buta untuk melihat al-Qur'ân yang diturunkan oleh Sang Maha Pemurah sebagai peringatan bagi seluruh alam semesta, akan Kami berikan jalan bagi setan untuk menguasai dirinya. Dengan demikian, setan akan selalu bersamanya: menggoda dan menyesatkan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Barang siapa yang berpaling) yaitu memalingkan diri (dari pengajaran Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah) dari Alquran (Kami adakan) Kami jadikan (baginya setan, maka setan itulah yang menjadi teman yang selalu menyertainya) yakni tidak pernah berpisah darinya.
t_id.iwq: Barangsiapa yang berpaling dari pengajaran Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah (Al Al-Quran), kami adakan baginya syaitan (yang menyesatkan) maka syaitan itulah yang menjadi teman yang selalu menyertainya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ومن يعش» يعرض «عن ذكر الرحمن» أي القرآن «نقيض» نسبب «له شيطاناً فهو له قرين» لا يفارقه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَن يَعۡشُ عَن ذِكۡرِ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ نُقَيِّضۡ لَهُۥ شَيۡطَٰنٗا فَهُوَ لَهُۥ قَرِينٞ (يَعْشُ: يُعْرِضْ, نُقَيِّضْ: نُهَيِّئْ، وَنُيَسِّرْ, قَرِينٌ: مُلَازِمٌ، وَمُصَاحِبٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Barang siapa yang berpaling) yaitu memalingkan diri (dari pengajaran Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah) dari Alquran (Kami adakan) Kami jadikan (baginya setan, maka setan itulah yang menjadi teman yang selalu menyertainya) yakni tidak pernah berpisah darinya

ar: وَإِنَّهُمۡ لَیَصُدُّونَهُمۡ عَنِ ٱلسَّبِیلِ وَیَحۡسَبُونَ أَنَّهُم مُّهۡتَدُونَ
ar_simple: وَإِنَّهُمْ لَيَصُدُّونَهُمْ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ مُهْتَدُونَ
transliteration: wa-innahum layaṣuddūnahum ʿani l-sabīli wayaḥsabūna annahum muh'tadūna

t.sahih-int: And indeed, the devils avert them from the way [of guidance] while they think that they are [rightly] guided
t.asad: whereupon, behold, these [evil impulses] bar all such from the path [of truth], making them think that they are guided aright
t.yusuf-ali: Such (evil ones) really hinder them from the Path, but they think that they are being guided aright
t.pickthall: And lo! they surely turn them from the way of Allah, and yet they deem that they are rightly guided
t.arberry: and they bar them from the way, and they think they are guided
t.hilali-khan: And verily, they (Satans / devils) hinder them from the Path (of Allah), but they think that they are guided aright
t.itani: They hinder them from the path, though they think they are guided.
t.mubarakpuri: And verily, they hinder them from the path, but they think that they are guided aright!
t.qarib: They hinder them from the path, though they think they are guided
t.wahiduddin: Devils divert men from the [right] way, while they think that they are rightly guided
t.shakir: And most surely they turn them away from the path, and they think that they are guided aright
t.haleem: evil ones bar people from the right path, even though they may think they are well guided
t.maariful: And they (the devils) prevent such people from the (right) way, while they deem themselves to be on the right path
t.khattab: who will certainly hinder them from the ˹Right˺ Way while they think they are ˹rightly˺ guided
t.ghali: And surely they indeed bar them from the way, and they reckon that they are right-guided
t.irving: They divert them from the [right] way while they may reckon they are guided
t.iwq: And indeed, the devils avert them from the way of guidance while they think that they are rightly guided

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya syaitan-syaitan itu benar-benar menghalangi mereka dari jalan yang benar dan mereka menyangka bahwa mereka mendapat petunjuk.
t_id.sabiq: Setan orang yang berpura-pura buta untuk melihat al-Qur'ân itu benar-benar akan menghalanginya dari jalan yang diserukan Allah, sementara ia merasa bahwa dirinya, dengan mengikuti setan-setan itu, berada pada jalan yang benar.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka) setan-setan itu (benar-benar menghalangi mereka) menghalangi orang-orang yang berpaling itu (dari jalan yang benar) atau jalan petunjuk (dan mereka menyangka bahwa mereka mendapat petunjuk) disebutkannya Dhamir dengan memakai kata jamak karena memandang segi makna yang dikandung lafal Man.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya syaitan-syaitan itu benar-benar menghalangi mereka dari jalan yang benar dan mereka menyangka bahwa mereka mendapat petunjuk.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإنهم» أي الشياطين «ليصدونهم» أي العاشين «عن السبيل» أي طرق الهدى «ويحسبون أنهم مهتدون» في الجمع رعاية معنى من
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِنَّهُمۡ لَيَصُدُّونَهُمۡ عَنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَيَحۡسَبُونَ أَنَّهُم مُّهۡتَدُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka) setan-setan itu (benar-benar menghalangi mereka) menghalangi orang-orang yang berpaling itu (dari jalan yang benar) atau jalan petunjuk (dan mereka menyangka bahwa mereka mendapat petunjuk) disebutkannya Dhamir dengan memakai kata jamak karena memandang segi makna yang dikandung lafal Man

ar: حَتَّىٰۤ إِذَا جَاۤءَنَا قَالَ یَـٰلَیۡتَ بَیۡنِی وَبَیۡنَكَ بُعۡدَ ٱلۡمَشۡرِقَیۡنِ فَبِئۡسَ ٱلۡقَرِینُ
ar_simple: حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءَنَا قَالَ يَا لَيْتَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكَ بُعْدَ الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ فَبِئْسَ الْقَرِينُ
transliteration: ḥattā idhā jāanā qāla yālayta baynī wabaynaka buʿ'da l-mashriqayni fabi'sa l-qarīnu

t.sahih-int: Until, when he comes to Us [at Judgement], he says [to his companion], "Oh, I wish there was between me and you the distance between the east and west - how wretched a companion
t.asad: But in the end, when he [who has thus sinned] appears before us [on Judgment Day], he will say [to his other self], “Would that between me and thee there had been the distance of east and west!” for, evil indeed [has proved] that other self
t.yusuf-ali: At length, when (such a one) comes to Us, he says (to his evil companion): "Would that between me and thee were the distance of East and West!" Ah! evil is the companion (indeed)
t.pickthall: Till, when he cometh unto Us, he saith (unto his comrade): Ah, would that between me and thee there were the distance of the two horizons - an evil comrade
t.arberry: till, when he comes to Us, he says, 'Would there had been between me and thee the distance of the two Easts!' An evil comrade
t.hilali-khan: Till, when (such a one) comes to Us, he says [to his Qarin (Satan / devil companion)] "Would that between me and you were the distance of the two easts (or the east and west)" a worst (type of) companion (indeed)
t.itani: Until, when He comes to Us, he will say, “If only there were between me and you the distance of the two Easts.” What an evil companion!
t.mubarakpuri: Till, when (such a one) comes to Us, he says, "Would that between me and you were the distance of the two easts (or the east and west) a worst companion (indeed)!"
t.qarib: Then, when such a person comes to Us, he says [to his companion] "I wish you had been as far away from me as east is from west. What an evil companion
t.wahiduddin: When such a person comes to Us, he will say [to his comrade], "If only you had been as far away from me as east is from west. What an evil comrade
t.shakir: Until when he comes to Us, he says: O would that between me and you there were the distance of the East and the West; so evil is the associate
t.haleem: When such a person comes to Us, he will say [to his comrade], ‘If only you had been as far away from me as east is from west. What an evil comrade!’
t.maariful: until when such a person will come to us, he will say (to the devil), “Would that there were the distance of East and West between me and you, because you were the worst companion.”
t.khattab: But when such a person comes to Us, one will say ˹to their associate˺, “I wish you were as distant from me as the east is from the west! What an evil associate ˹you were˺!”
t.ghali: Until, when he comes to Us, he says, "Oh, would that there had been between me and you the distance of the two easts!" (This is sometimes understood to mean the east and west; or the rising of the sun and moon; or the rising of the sun in summer and winter) Miserable then is the comrade
t.irving: until when he comes up to Us, he says: "Alas, if the distance between the two Easts only lay between you and me! He´s such an awful soulmate
t.iwq: Until, when he comes to Us at Judgement, he says to his companion, "Oh, I wish there was between me and you the distance between the east and west - how wretched a companion

t_id.kemenag: Sehingga apabila orang-orang yang berpaling itu datang kepada kami (di hari kiamat) dia berkata: "Aduhai, semoga (jarak) antaraku dan kamu seperti jarak antara masyrik dan maghrib, maka syaitan itu adalah sejahat-jahat teman (yang menyertai manusia)".
t_id.sabiq: Sehingga, ketika orang yang berpura-pura buta untuk melihat al-Qur'ân itu datang menemui Allah pada hari kiamat, dan melihat sendiri akibat perbuatannya, ia berkata kepada temannya dengan nada menyesal, "Andai saja aku dulu, di dunia, jauh darimu sejauh jarak antara timur dari barat. Kamu benar-benar teman- teman yang paling jahat sampai menjerumuskan aku ke jurang kenistaan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sehingga apabila orang yang berpaling itu datang kepada Kami) bersama dengan temannya atau setannya di hari kiamat kelak (dia berkata,) orang yang berpaling itu kepada temannya atau setannya ("Aduhai) huruf Ya di sini menunjukkan makna Tanbih (seandainya jarak antara aku dan kamu seperti jarak antara masyriq dan Magrib) yakni sejauh jarak antara timur dan barat (maka sejelek-jelek teman) bagiku adalah kamu." Lalu Allah berfirman:
t_id.iwq: Sehingga apabila orang-orang yang berpaling itu datang kepada kami (di hari kiamat) dia berkata: "Aduhai, semoga (jarak) antaraku dan kamu seperti jarak antara masyrik dan maghrib, maka syaitan itu adalah sejahat-jahat teman (yang menyertai manusia)".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «حتى إذا جاءنا» العاشي بقرينه يوم القيامة «قال» له «يا» للتنبيه «ليت بيني وبينك بعد المشرقين» أي مثل بعد ما بين المشرق والمغرب «فبئس القرين» أنت لي، قال تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَنَا قَالَ يَٰلَيۡتَ بَيۡنِي وَبَيۡنَكَ بُعۡدَ ٱلۡمَشۡرِقَيۡنِ فَبِئۡسَ ٱلۡقَرِينُ (بُعْدَ الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ: مِثْلَ تَبَاعُدِ مَا بَيْنَ المَشْرِقِ، وَالمَغْرِبِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sehingga apabila orang yang berpaling itu datang kepada Kami) bersama dengan temannya atau setannya di hari kiamat kelak (dia berkata,) orang yang berpaling itu kepada temannya atau setannya ("Aduhai) huruf Ya di sini menunjukkan makna Tanbih (seandainya jarak antara aku dan kamu seperti jarak antara masyriq dan Magrib) yakni sejauh jarak antara timur dan barat (maka sejelek-jelek teman) bagiku adalah kamu." Lalu Allah berfirman

ar: وَلَن یَنفَعَكُمُ ٱلۡیَوۡمَ إِذ ظَّلَمۡتُمۡ أَنَّكُمۡ فِی ٱلۡعَذَابِ مُشۡتَرِكُونَ
ar_simple: وَلَنْ يَنْفَعَكُمُ الْيَوْمَ إِذْ ظَلَمْتُمْ أَنَّكُمْ فِي الْعَذَابِ مُشْتَرِكُونَ
transliteration: walan yanfaʿakumu l-yawma idh ẓalamtum annakum fī l-ʿadhābi mush'tarikūna

t.sahih-int: And never will it benefit you that Day, when you have wronged, that you are [all] sharing in the punishment
t.asad: On that Day it will not profit you in the least [to know] that, since you have sinned [together], you are now to share your suffering [as well]
t.yusuf-ali: When ye have done wrong, it will avail you nothing, that Day, that ye shall be partners in Punishment
t.pickthall: And it profiteth you not this day, because ye did wrong, that ye will be sharers in the doom
t.arberry: It shall not profit you today, since you did evil, that you are partners in the chastisement
t.hilali-khan: It will profit you not this Day (O you who turn away from Allah's remembrance and His worship, etc.) as you did wrong, (and) that you will be sharers (you and your Qarin) in the punishment
t.itani: It will not benefit you on that Day, since you did wrong. You are partners in the suffering.
t.mubarakpuri: It will profit you not this Day as you did wrong, (and) that you will be sharers in the punishment.
t.qarib: Since you were both unjust, It will not benefit you today to know, that you are partners in the punishment
t.wahiduddin: It will be said [to such a person], "You have done wrong. Having partners in punishment will be of no avail to you today
t.shakir: And since you were unjust, it will not profit you this day that you are sharers in the chastisement
t.haleem: [It will be said to them], ‘You have done wrong. Having partners in punishment will not console you today.’
t.maariful: And (it will be said to such people,) “Since you were wrongdoers, it will never benefit you today that you are sharing the punishment with each other.”
t.khattab: ˹It will be said to both,˺ “Since you all did wrong, sharing in the punishment will be of no benefit to you this Day.”
t.ghali: And it will never profit you today, as you did injustice, that you are partners in the torment
t.irving: It will never benefit you today to have any partners in torment since you have already gone wrong
t.iwq: And never will it benefit you that Day, when you have wronged, that you are all sharing in the punishment

t_id.kemenag: (Harapanmu itu) sekali-kali tidak akan memberi manfaat kepadamu di hari itu karena kamu telah menganiaya (dirimu sendiri). Sesungguhnya kamu bersekutu dalam azab itu.
t_id.sabiq: Pada hari itu, sebagai penghinaan, kepada mereka dikatakan, "Hari ini, kebersamaan kalian dengan setan di dalam siksaan, tidak akan meringankan siksaan itu, karena kalian telah menzalimi diri sendiri dengan mengambil sikap kafir. Masing-masing merasakan penderitaan akibat siksa yang amat berat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sekali-kali tidak akan memberi manfaat kepada kalian) angan-angan dan penyesalan kalian itu, hai orang-orang yang berpaling (di hari ini karena kalian telah berbuat aniaya) maksudnya telah jelaslah kelaliman kalian dengan sebab menyekutukan Allah sewaktu di dunia. Lafal Idz merupakan Badal dari lafal Al Yaumu. (Bahwasanya kalian) bersama dengan teman-teman kalian (bersekutu dalam azab ini) adanya illat dalam ayat ini diperkirakan keberadaannya, tidak disebutkan karena kurang penting.
t_id.iwq: (Harapanmu itu) sekali-kali tidak akan memberi manfaat kepadamu di hari itu karena kamu telah menganiaya (dirimu sendiri). Sesungguhnya kamu bersekutu dalam azab itu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولن ينفعكم» أي العاشين تمنيكم وندمك «اليوم إذ ظلمتم» أي تبين لكم ظلمكم بالإشراك في الدنيا «أنكم» مع قرنائكم «في العذاب مشتركون» علة بتقدير اللام لعدم النفع وإذ بدل من اليوم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَن يَنفَعَكُمُ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ إِذ ظَّلَمۡتُمۡ أَنَّكُمۡ فِي ٱلۡعَذَابِ مُشۡتَرِكُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sekali-kali tidak akan memberi manfaat kepada kalian) angan-angan dan penyesalan kalian itu, hai orang-orang yang berpaling (di hari ini karena kalian telah berbuat aniaya) maksudnya telah jelaslah kelaliman kalian dengan sebab menyekutukan Allah sewaktu di dunia. Lafal Idz merupakan Badal dari lafal Al Yaumu. (Bahwasanya kalian) bersama dengan teman-teman kalian (bersekutu dalam azab ini) adanya illat dalam ayat ini diperkirakan keberadaannya, tidak disebutkan karena kurang penting

ar: أَفَأَنتَ تُسۡمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ أَوۡ تَهۡدِی ٱلۡعُمۡیَ وَمَن كَانَ فِی ضَلَـٰلࣲ مُّبِینࣲ
ar_simple: أَفَأَنْتَ تُسْمِعُ الصُّمَّ أَوْ تَهْدِي الْعُمْيَ وَمَنْ كَانَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُبِينٍ
transliteration: afa-anta tus'miʿu l-ṣuma aw tahdī l-ʿum'ya waman kāna fī ḍalālin mubīnin

t.sahih-int: Then will you make the deaf hear, [O Muhammad], or guide the blind or he who is in clear error
t.asad: CANST THOU, perchance, [O Muhammad,] make the deaf hear, or show the right way to the blind or to such as are obviously lost in error
t.yusuf-ali: Canst thou then make the deaf to hear, or give direction to the blind or to such as (wander) in manifest error
t.pickthall: Canst thou (Muhammad) make the deaf to hear, or canst thou guide the blind or him who is in error manifest
t.arberry: What, shalt thou make the deaf to hear, or shalt thou guide the blind and him who is in manifest error
t.hilali-khan: Can you (O Muhammad SAW) make the deaf to hear, or can you guide the blind or him who is in manifest error
t.itani: Can you make the deaf hear, or guide the blind, and him who is in evident error?
t.mubarakpuri: Can you make the deaf to hear, or can you guide the blind or him who is in manifest error
t.qarib: Can you make the deaf hear, or guide the blind and whoever is clearly misguided
t.wahiduddin: Can you [Prophet] make the deaf hear? Or guide either the blind or those who are in manifest error
t.shakir: What! can you then make the deaf to hear or guide the blind and him who is in clear error
t.haleem: Can you [Prophet] make the deaf hear? Or guide either the blind or those who are in gross error
t.maariful: So, can you (O prophet) make the deaf to hear, or can you show the way to the blind and the one who is in open error
t.khattab: Can you make the deaf hear, or guide the blind or those clearly astray
t.ghali: Will you (i.e., the Prophet) then make the deaf to hear, or will you guide the blind and him who is in evident error
t.irving: Will you make the deaf hear or guide the blind, as well as anyone else who is in obvious error
t.iwq: Then will you make the deaf hear, O Muhammad, or guide the blind or he who is in clear error

t_id.kemenag: Maka apakah kamu dapat menjadikan orang yang pekak bisa mendengar atau (dapatkah) kamu memberi petunjuk kepada orang yang buta (hatinya) dan kepada orang yang tetap dalam kesesatan yang nyata?
t_id.sabiq: Mampukan kamu memberi petunjuk kepada mereka yang amat jauh tersesat? Dapatkah kamu membuat orang tuli--untuk mendengarkan kebenaran--menjadi dapat mendengar? Atau, orang buta--untuk mengambil pelajaran--menjadi dapat melihat? Atau orang yang, menurut ilmu Allah, mati dalam keadaan sesat? Tidak, kamu tidak mampu. Karena kekafiran mereka telah begitu merasuk kuat, hingga tidak lagi dapat mengambil manfaat dari apa yang mereka lihat dan dengar.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka apakah kamu dapat menjadikan orang yang pekak dapat mendengar, atau dapatkah kamu memberi petunjuk kepada orang yang buta hatinya dan kepada orang yang tetap dalam kesesatan yang nyata?) jelas sesatnya, maksudnya mereka tidak beriman.
t_id.iwq: Maka apakah kamu dapat menjadikan orang yang pekak bisa mendengar atau (dapatkah) kamu memberi petunjuk kepada orang yang buta (hatinya) dan kepada orang yang tetap dalam kesesatan yang nyata?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أفأنت تسمع الصم أو تهدي العمي ومن كان في ضلال مبين» بيّن، أي فهم لا يؤمنون
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَفَأَنتَ تُسۡمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ أَوۡ تَهۡدِي ٱلۡعُمۡيَ وَمَن كَانَ فِي ضَلَٰلٖ مُّبِينٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka apakah kamu dapat menjadikan orang yang pekak dapat mendengar, atau dapatkah kamu memberi petunjuk kepada orang yang buta hatinya dan kepada orang yang tetap dalam kesesatan yang nyata?) jelas sesatnya, maksudnya mereka tidak beriman

ar: فَإِمَّا نَذۡهَبَنَّ بِكَ فَإِنَّا مِنۡهُم مُّنتَقِمُونَ
ar_simple: فَإِمَّا نَذْهَبَنَّ بِكَ فَإِنَّا مِنْهُمْ مُنْتَقِمُونَ
transliteration: fa-immā nadhhabanna bika fa-innā min'hum muntaqimūna

t.sahih-int: And whether [or not] We take you away [in death], indeed, We will take retribution upon them
t.asad: But whether We do [or do not] take thee away [ere thy message prevails] - verily, We shall inflict Our retribution on them
t.yusuf-ali: Even if We take thee away, We shall be sure to exact retribution from them
t.pickthall: And if We take thee away, We surely shall take vengeance on them
t.arberry: Whether We take thee away, We shall take vengeance upon them
t.hilali-khan: And even if We take you (O Muhammad SAW) away, We shall indeed take vengeance on them
t.itani: Even if We take you away, We will wreak vengeance upon them.
t.mubarakpuri: And even if We take you away, We shall indeed take vengeance on them.
t.qarib: Whether We take you away, We will afflict retribution on them
t.wahiduddin: Even if We take you away from the world, We shall surely take vengeance on them
t.shakir: But if We should take you away, still We shall inflict retribution on them
t.haleem: Either We shall take you away and punish them- indeed We will
t.maariful: So, even if We take you away, We will surely take vengeance on them
t.khattab: Even if We take you away ˹from this world˺, We will surely inflict punishment upon them
t.ghali: So, in case ever We definitely cause you to go away, then surely We will be the Avengers against them
t.irving: Even should We take you away, We will still be avenged on them
t.iwq: And whether or not We take you away in death, indeed, We will take retribution upon them

t_id.kemenag: Sungguh, jika Kami mewafatkan kamu (sebelum kamu mencapai kemenangan) maka sesungguhnya Kami akan menyiksa mereka (di akhirat).
t_id.sabiq: Jika Kami mematikan kamu sebelum Kami perlihatkan kepadamu penyiksaan mereka--sehingga, dengan demikian, Kami melegakan dadamu dan orang-orang Mukmin--Kami pasti akan tetap membalas mereka di dunia dan di akhirat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sungguh, jika) lafal Imma asalnya adalah gabungan antara Syarthiyyah dan Ma Zaidah (Kami mewafatkan kamu) sebelum Kami mengazab mereka (maka sesungguhnya Kami akan menyiksa mereka) di akhirat.
t_id.iwq: Sungguh, jika Kami mewafatkan kamu (sebelum kamu mencapai kemenangan) maka sesungguhnya Kami akan menyiksa mereka (di akhirat).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فإما» في إدغام نون إن الشرطية في ما الزائدة «نذهبن بك» بأن نميتك قبل تعذيبهم «فإنا منهم منتقمون» في الآخرة
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَإِمَّا نَذۡهَبَنَّ بِكَ فَإِنَّا مِنۡهُم مُّنتَقِمُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sungguh, jika) lafal Imma asalnya adalah gabungan antara Syarthiyyah dan Ma Zaidah (Kami mewafatkan kamu) sebelum Kami mengazab mereka (maka sesungguhnya Kami akan menyiksa mereka) di akhirat

ar: أَوۡ نُرِیَنَّكَ ٱلَّذِی وَعَدۡنَـٰهُمۡ فَإِنَّا عَلَیۡهِم مُّقۡتَدِرُونَ
ar_simple: أَوْ نُرِيَنَّكَ الَّذِي وَعَدْنَاهُمْ فَإِنَّا عَلَيْهِمْ مُقْتَدِرُونَ
transliteration: aw nuriyannaka alladhī waʿadnāhum fa-innā ʿalayhim muq'tadirūna

t.sahih-int: Or whether [or not] We show you that which We have promised them, indeed, We are Perfect in Ability
t.asad: and whether We show thee [or do not show thee in this world] the fulfillment of what We have promised them - verily, We have full power over them
t.yusuf-ali: Or We shall show thee that (accomplished) which We have promised them: for verily We shall prevail over them
t.pickthall: Or (if) We show thee that wherewith We threaten them; for lo! We have complete command of them
t.arberry: or We show thee a part of that We promised them, surely We have power over them
t.hilali-khan: Or (if) We show you that wherewith We threaten them, then verily, We have perfect command over them
t.itani: Or show you what We have promised them; for We have absolute power over them.
t.mubarakpuri: Or (if) We show you that wherewith We threaten them, then verily, We have perfect command over them.
t.qarib: Or, We show you what We have promised them; for We have perfect ability to control them
t.wahiduddin: or We shall show you what We have promised them; for surely We have complete power over them
t.shakir: Rather We will certainly show you that which We have promised them; for surely We are the possessors of full power over them
t.haleem: or We shall let you witness the punishment We threatened them with; We have full power over them
t.maariful: Or (if) We show you (in your life) that (punishment) with which We have threatened them, then We have full control over them
t.khattab: Or if We show you what We threaten them with, We certainly have full power over them
t.ghali: Or We definitely show you that which We promised them. So, surely We are Supreme Determiners over them
t.irving: Or shall We show you what We have promised them? We are Competent to deal with them
t.iwq: Or whether or not We show you that which We have promised them, indeed, We are Perfect in Ability

t_id.kemenag: Atau Kami memperlihatkan kepadamu (azab) yang telah Kami ancamkan kepada mereka. Maka sesungguhnya Kami berkuasa atas mereka.
t_id.sabiq: Atau, jika kamu ingin agar Kami memperlihatkan siksaan yang telah Kami janjikan untuk mereka sebelum matimu, Kami pasti akan memperlihatkannya kepadamu, karena Kami memang berkuasa atas mereka dengan kekuasaan dan keperkasaan Kami.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Atau kami memperlihatkan kepadamu) sewaktu kamu masih hidup (apa yang telah Kami ancamkan kepada mereka) yakni azab yang Kami ancamkan itu (maka sesungguhnya Kami atas mereka) maksudnya, untuk mengazab mereka (berkuasa) sangat berkuasa atau sangat mampu.
t_id.iwq: Atau Kami memperlihatkan kepadamu (azab) yang telah Kami ancamkan kepada mereka. Maka sesungguhnya Kami berkuasa atas mereka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أو نرينك» في حياتك «الذي وعدناهم» به من العذاب «فإنا عليهم» على عذابهم «مقتدرون» قادرون
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَوۡ نُرِيَنَّكَ ٱلَّذِي وَعَدۡنَٰهُمۡ فَإِنَّا عَلَيۡهِم مُّقۡتَدِرُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Atau kami memperlihatkan kepadamu) sewaktu kamu masih hidup (apa yang telah Kami ancamkan kepada mereka) yakni azab yang Kami ancamkan itu (maka sesungguhnya Kami atas mereka) maksudnya, untuk mengazab mereka (berkuasa) sangat berkuasa atau sangat mampu

ar: فَٱسۡتَمۡسِكۡ بِٱلَّذِیۤ أُوحِیَ إِلَیۡكَۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ صِرَ ٰطࣲ مُّسۡتَقِیمࣲ
ar_simple: فَاسْتَمْسِكْ بِالَّذِي أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ
transliteration: fa-is'tamsik bi-alladhī ūḥiya ilayka innaka ʿalā ṣirāṭin mus'taqīmin

t.sahih-int: So adhere to that which is revealed to you. Indeed, you are on a straight path
t.asad: So hold fast to all that has been revealed to thee: for, behold, thou art on a straight way
t.yusuf-ali: So hold thou fast to the Revelation sent down to thee; verily thou art on a Straight Way
t.pickthall: So hold thou fast to that which is inspired in thee. Lo! thou art on a right path
t.arberry: So hold thou fast unto that which has been revealed unto thee; surely thou art upon a straight path
t.hilali-khan: So hold you (O Muhammad SAW) fast to that which is inspired in you. Verily, you are on a Straight Path
t.itani: So adhere to what is revealed to you. You are upon a straight path.
t.mubarakpuri: So hold you fast to that which is revealed to you. Verily, you are on the straight path.
t.qarib: So adhere to what is revealed to you. You are on a straight path
t.wahiduddin: So, hold fast to the Book that has been revealed to you, you are surely on the right path
t.shakir: Therefore hold fast to that which has been revealed to you; surely you are on the right path
t.haleem: Hold fast to what has been revealed to you- you truly are on the right path
t.maariful: So, hold fast to that which has been revealed to you. Surely, you are on the straight path
t.khattab: So hold firmly to what has been revealed to you ˹O Prophet˺. You are truly on the Straight Path
t.ghali: So uphold steadfastly that which has been revealed to you; surely you are upon a straight Path
t.irving: Hold on to whatever has been revealed to you; you are on a Straight Road
t.iwq: Thus adhere to that which is revealed to you. Indeed, you are on a straight path

t_id.kemenag: Maka berpegang teguhlah kamu kepada agama yang telah diwahyukan kepadamu. Sesungguhnya kamu berada di atas jalan yang lurus.
t_id.sabiq: Kalau memang salah satu dari dua hal itu terjadi--dan memang pasti terjadi--maka berpegang teguhlah kepada al-Qur'ân yang telah Kami wahyukan kepadamu. Tetaplah melaksanakan ajaran-ajarannya, karena kamu berada pada jalan yang benar dan lurus.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka berpegang teguhlah kamu kepada apa yang telah diwahyukan kepadamu) yakni Alquran. (Sesungguhnya kamu berada di atas jalan) atau tuntunan (yang lurus.)
t_id.iwq: Maka berpegang teguhlah kamu kepada agama yang telah diwahyukan kepadamu. Sesungguhnya kamu berada di atas jalan yang lurus.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فاستمسك بالذي أوحي إليك» أي القرآن «إنك على صراط» طريق «مستقيم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَٱسۡتَمۡسِكۡ بِٱلَّذِيٓ أُوحِيَ إِلَيۡكَۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٖ مُّسۡتَقِيمٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka berpegang teguhlah kamu kepada apa yang telah diwahyukan kepadamu) yakni Alquran. (Sesungguhnya kamu berada di atas jalan) atau tuntunan (yang lurus)

ar: وَإِنَّهُۥ لَذِكۡرࣱ لَّكَ وَلِقَوۡمِكَۖ وَسَوۡفَ تُسۡءَلُونَ
ar_simple: وَإِنَّهُ لَذِكْرٌ لَكَ وَلِقَوْمِكَ ۖ وَسَوْفَ تُسْأَلُونَ
transliteration: wa-innahu ladhik'run laka waliqawmika wasawfa tus'alūna

t.sahih-int: And indeed, it is a remembrance for you and your people, and you [all] are going to be questioned
t.asad: and, verily, this [revelation] shall indeed become [a source of] eminence for thee and thy people: but in time you all will be called to account [for what you have done with it]
t.yusuf-ali: The (Qur'an) is indeed the message, for thee and for thy people; and soon shall ye (all) be brought to account
t.pickthall: And lo! it is in truth a Reminder for thee and for thy folk; and ye will be questioned
t.arberry: Surely it is a Reminder to thee and to thy people; and assuredly you will be questioned
t.hilali-khan: And verily, this (the Quran) is indeed a Reminder for you (O Muhammad SAW) and your people (Quraish people, or your followers), and you will be questioned (about it)
t.itani: It is a message for you, and for your people; and you will be questioned.
t.mubarakpuri: And verily, this is indeed a Dhikr for you and your people, and you will be questioned.
t.qarib: It is a remembrance for you and your people, and you are all going to be questioned
t.wahiduddin: it is certainly a reminder to you and to your people and you will soon be called to account
t.shakir: And most surely it is a reminder for you and your people, and you shall soon be questioned
t.haleem: for it is a reminder for you and your people: you will all be questioned
