t.maariful: And certainly this (Qur’ān) is a word of honour for you and your people, and you will be questioned
t.khattab: Surely this ˹Quran˺ is a glory for you and your people. And you will ˹all˺ be questioned ˹about it˺
t.ghali: And surely it is indeed a Remembrance to you and to your people, and surely you (all) will eventually be questioned (about it)
t.irving: It is a Reminder for you and your folk, and you will (all) be questioned
t.iwq: And indeed, it is a remembrance for you and your people, and you all are going to be questioned

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya Al Quran itu benar-benar adalah suatu kemuliaan besar bagimu dan bagi kaummu dan kelak kamu akan diminta pertanggungan jawab.
t_id.sabiq: Al-Qur'ân ini, sungguh merupakan kehormatan yang amat besar bagimu, Muhammad, dan bagi umatmu. Sebab, kitab suci ini diturunkan kepadamu dengan bahasa yang digunakan oleh bangsa Arab. Kelak, pada hari kiamat, kalian akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban: adakah kalian memenuhi haknya dan mensyukuri nikmatnya atau tidak.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Alquran itu benar-benar adalah suatu kemuliaan besar) benar-benar merupakan kemuliaan yang besar (bagimu dan bagi kaummu) karena diturunkan dengan memakai bahasa mereka (dan kelak kalian akan diminta pertanggungan jawab) tentang pengamalannya.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya Al Al-Quran itu benar-benar adalah suatu kemuliaan besar bagimu dan bagi kaummu dan kelak kamu akan diminta pertanggungan jawab.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإنه لذكر» لشرف «لك ولقومك» لنزوله بلغتهم «وسوف تُسألون» عن القيام بحقه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِنَّهُۥ لَذِكۡرٞ لَّكَ وَلِقَوۡمِكَۖ وَسَوۡفَ تُسۡـَٔلُونَ (لَذِكْرٌ: لَشَرَفٌ؛ لِأَنَّهُ أُنْزِلَ بِلُغَتِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Alquran itu benar-benar adalah suatu kemuliaan besar) benar-benar merupakan kemuliaan yang besar (bagimu dan bagi kaummu) karena diturunkan dengan memakai bahasa mereka (dan kelak kalian akan diminta pertanggungan jawab) tentang pengamalannya

ar: وَسۡءَلۡ مَنۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِكَ مِن رُّسُلِنَاۤ أَجَعَلۡنَا مِن دُونِ ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ ءَالِهَةࣰ یُعۡبَدُونَ
ar_simple: وَاسْأَلْ مَنْ أَرْسَلْنَا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رُسُلِنَا أَجَعَلْنَا مِنْ دُونِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ آلِهَةً يُعْبَدُونَ
transliteration: wasal man arsalnā min qablika min rusulinā ajaʿalnā min dūni l-raḥmāni ālihatan yuʿ'badūna

t.sahih-int: And ask those We sent before you of Our messengers; have We made besides the Most Merciful deities to be worshipped
t.asad: Yet [above all else,] ask any of Our apostles whom We sent forth before thy time whether We have ever allowed that deities other than the Most Gracious be worshipped
t.yusuf-ali: And question thou our messengers whom We sent before thee; did We appoint any deities other than (Allah) Most Gracious, to be worshipped
t.pickthall: And ask those of Our messengers whom We sent before thee: Did We ever appoint gods to be worshipped beside the Beneficent
t.arberry: Ask those of Our Messengers We sent before thee: Have We appointed, apart from the All-merciful, gods to be served
t.hilali-khan: And ask (O Muhammad SAW) those of Our Messengers whom We sent before you: "Did We ever appoint aliha (gods) to be worshipped besides the Most Beneficent (Allah)
t.itani: Ask those of Our messengers We sent before you: “Did We appoint gods besides the Most Gracious to be worshiped?”
t.mubarakpuri: And ask whom We sent before you of Our Messengers: "Did We ever appoint gods to be worshipped besides the Most Gracious"
t.qarib: Ask those of Our messengers We sent before you; did We ever appoint any deities besides the Merciful to be worshipped
t.wahiduddin: Ask those of Our messengers whom We sent before you, "Did We ever appoint gods to be worshipped besides the Beneficent One
t.shakir: And ask those of Our apostles whom We sent before you: Did We ever appoint gods to be worshipped besides the Beneficent Allah
t.haleem: Ask the prophets We sent before you: ‘Did We ever appoint any gods to be worshipped besides the Lord of Mercy?’
t.maariful: Ask Our messengers whom We sent before you whether We had appointed gods to be worshipped besides RaHmān
t.khattab: Ask ˹the followers of˺ the messengers that We already sent before you if We ˹ever˺ appointed ˹other˺ gods to be worshipped besides the Most Compassionate
t.ghali: And ask the ones of Our Messengers We sent before you. Have We made, apart from The All-Merciful, gods to be worshiped
t.irving: Question any of Our messengers We have sent before you: "Did We set up any [other] gods they should serve instead of the Mercy-giving
t.iwq: And ask those We sent before you of Our messengers; have We made besides the Ever-Merciful deities to be worshipped

t_id.kemenag: Dan tanyakanlah kepada rasul-rasul Kami yang telah Kami utus sebelum kamu: "Adakah Kami menentukan tuhan-tuhan untuk disembah selain Allah Yang Maha Pemurah?"
t_id.sabiq: Amatilah syariat agama yang dibawa oleh rasul-rasul yang Kami utus sebelummu, adakah kamu dapatkan di dalamnya seruan untuk menyembah tuhan selain Allah? Tidak ada. Kalau begitu, orang-orang yang menyembah tuhan selain Allah, itu benar-benar tersesat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tanyakanlah kepada rasul-rasul Kami yang telah Kami utus sebelum kamu, "Adakah Kami menentukan selain Allah Yang Maha Pemurah) (sebagai tuhan-tuhan untuk disembah), menurut suatu pendapat bahwa hal ini memang berdasarkan kenyataan, yaitu seumpamanya Allah mengumpulkan rasul-rasul itu pada malam sewaktu nabi diisra-kan. Menurut pendapat yang lain bahwa yang dimaksud adalah umat-umat dari kalangan ahli kitab. Kedua pendapat tadi tidak usah diselidiki kebenarannya, karena makna yang dimaksud dari perintah menanyakan ini ialah untuk menetapkan terhadap orang-orang musyrik Quraisy, bahwasanya tiada seorang utusan pun dari Allah dan tiada pula suatu kitab pun yang diturunkan-Nya yang memerintahkan untuk menyembah kepada selain Allah.
t_id.iwq: Dan tanyakanlah kepada rasul-rasul Kami yang telah Kami utus sebelum kamu: "Adakah Kami menentukan tuhan-tuhan untuk disembah selain Allah Yang Maha Pemurah?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «واسأل من أرسلنا من قبلك من رسلنا أجعلنا من دون الرحمن» أي غيره «آلهة يعبدون» قيل هو على ظاهره بأن جمع له الرسل ليلة الإسراء، وقيل المراد أمم من أي أهل الكتابين، ولم يسأل على واحد من القولين لأن المراد من الأمر بالسؤال التقرير لمشركي قريش أنه لم يأت رسول من الله ولا كتاب بعبادة غير الله
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَسۡـَٔلۡ مَنۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِكَ مِن رُّسُلِنَآ أَجَعَلۡنَا مِن دُونِ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ ءَالِهَةٗ يُعۡبَدُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tanyakanlah kepada rasul-rasul Kami yang telah Kami utus sebelum kamu, "Adakah Kami menentukan selain Allah Yang Maha Pemurah) (sebagai tuhan-tuhan untuk disembah), menurut suatu pendapat bahwa hal ini memang berdasarkan kenyataan, yaitu seumpamanya Allah mengumpulkan rasul-rasul itu pada malam sewaktu nabi diisra-kan. Menurut pendapat yang lain bahwa yang dimaksud adalah umat-umat dari kalangan ahli kitab. Kedua pendapat tadi tidak usah diselidiki kebenarannya, karena makna yang dimaksud dari perintah menanyakan ini ialah untuk menetapkan terhadap orang-orang musyrik Quraisy, bahwasanya tiada seorang utusan pun dari Allah dan tiada pula suatu kitab pun yang diturunkan-Nya yang memerintahkan untuk menyembah kepada selain Allah

ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مُوسَىٰ بِءَایَـٰتِنَاۤ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ وَمَلَإِی۟هِۦ فَقَالَ إِنِّی رَسُولُ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَـٰلَمِینَ
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ بِآيَاتِنَا إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَئِهِ فَقَالَ إِنِّي رَسُولُ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
transliteration: walaqad arsalnā mūsā biāyātinā ilā fir'ʿawna wamala-ihi faqāla innī rasūlu rabbi l-ʿālamīna

t.sahih-int: And certainly did We send Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his establishment, and he said, "Indeed, I am the messenger of the Lord of the worlds
t.asad: THUS, INDEED, have We sent Moses with Our messages unto Pharaoh and his great ones; and he said: “Behold, I am an apostle of the Sustainer of all the worlds!”
t.yusuf-ali: We did send Moses aforetime, with Our Signs, to Pharaoh and his Chiefs: He said, "I am a messenger of the Lord of the Worlds
t.pickthall: And verily We sent Moses with Our revelations unto Pharaoh and his chiefs, and he said: I am a messenger of the Lord of the Worlds
t.arberry: We also sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his Council, and he said, 'Surely, I am the Messenger of the Lord of all Being
t.hilali-khan: And indeed We did send Musa (Moses) with Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) to Fir'aun (Pharaoh) and his chiefs (inviting them to Allah's Religion of Islam) He said: "Verily, I am a Messenger of the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)
t.itani: We sent Moses with Our revelations to Pharaoh and his dignitaries. He said, “I am the Messenger of the Lord of the Worlds.”
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed We did send Musa with Our Ayat to Fir`awn and his chiefs. He said: "Verily, I am a Messenger of the Lord of all that exists."
t.qarib: We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his inner circle. He said, "I am the Messenger of the Lord of the worlds
t.wahiduddin: We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his nobles. He said, "I am the messenger of the Lord of the Universe
t.shakir: And certainly We sent Musa with Our communications to Firon and his chiefs, so he said: Surely I am the apostle of the Lord of the worlds
t.haleem: We sent Moses to Pharaoh and his courtiers and he said, ‘I am truly a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds,’
t.maariful: We sent Mūsā with Our signs to Fir’aun (Pharaoh) and his chiefs; so he said, “I am the messenger of the Lord of the worlds.”
t.khattab: Indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his chiefs, and he said: “I am a messenger of the Lord of all worlds.”
t.ghali: And indeed We already sent M?asu (Moses) with Our signs to Firaawn (Pharaoh) and his chiefs. So he said, "Surely I am the Messenger of The Lord of the worlds
t.irving: We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his courtiers, and he said: "I am a messenger from the Lord of the Universe
t.iwq: And certainly did We send Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his establishment, and he said, "Indeed, I am the messenger of Lord of all worlds

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesunguhnya Kami telah mengutus Musa dengan membawa mukjizat-mukjizat Kami kepada Fir'aun dan pemuka-pemuka kaumnya. Maka Musa berkata: "Sesungguhnya aku adalah utusan dari Tuhan seru sekalian alam".
t_id.sabiq: Kami, sungguh, benar-benar telah mengutus Mûsâ, dengan membawa mukjizat yang membuktikan kebenarannya, kepada Fir'aun dan kaumnya. Mûsâ berkata, "Aku adalah rasul Sang Pencipta dan Pemelihara alam semesta yang diutus kepada kalian." Tetapi mereka malah menuntut agar Mûsâ mendatangkan mukjizat yang lain.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah mengutus Musa dengan membawa mukjizat-mukjizat Kami kepada Firaun dan pemuka-pemuka kaumnya) yaitu bangsa Kobtik, (maka Musa berkata, "Sesungguhnya aku adalah utusan dari Rabb seru sekalian alam.")
t_id.iwq: Dan sesunguhnya Kami telah mengutus Musa dengan membawa mukjizat-mukjizat Kami kepada Fir'aun dan pemuka-pemuka kaumnya. Maka Musa berkata: "Sesungguhnya aku adalah utusan dari Tuhan seru sekalian alam".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد أرسلنا موسى بآياتنا إلى فرعون وملئه» أي القبط «فقال إني رسول رب العالمين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مُوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ وَمَلَإِيْهِۦ فَقَالَ إِنِّي رَسُولُ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ (وَمَلَئِهِ: أَشْرَافُ قَوْمِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah mengutus Musa dengan membawa mukjizat-mukjizat Kami kepada Firaun dan pemuka-pemuka kaumnya) yaitu bangsa Kobtik, (maka Musa berkata, "Sesungguhnya aku adalah utusan dari Rabb seru sekalian alam)

ar: فَلَمَّا جَاۤءَهُم بِءَایَـٰتِنَاۤ إِذَا هُم مِّنۡهَا یَضۡحَكُونَ
ar_simple: فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ بِآيَاتِنَا إِذَا هُمْ مِنْهَا يَضْحَكُونَ
transliteration: falammā jāahum biāyātinā idhā hum min'hā yaḍḥakūna

t.sahih-int: But when he brought them Our signs, at once they laughed at them
t.asad: But as soon as he came before them with Our [miraculous] signs, lo! they derided them
t.yusuf-ali: But when he came to them with Our Signs, behold they ridiculed them
t.pickthall: But when he brought them Our tokens, behold! they laughed at them
t.arberry: But when he brought them Our signs, lo, they laughed at them
t.hilali-khan: But when he came to them with Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) behold! They laughed at them
t.itani: But when he showed them Our signs, they started laughing at them.
t.mubarakpuri: But when he came to them with Our Ayat, behold, they laughed at them.
t.qarib: But when he came to them with Our signs, they laughed at them
t.wahiduddin: but when he came to them with Our signs, they ridiculed them
t.shakir: But when he came to them with Our signs, lo! they laughed at them
t.haleem: but when he presented Our signs to them, they laughed
t.maariful: So, when he came to them with Our signs, they at once started laughing at them
t.khattab: But as soon as he came to them with Our signs, they laughed at them
t.ghali: Yet, as soon as he came to them with Our signs, only then did they laugh at them
t.irving: When he brought them Our signs, they merely laughed at them
t.iwq: But when he brought them Our signs, at once they laughed at them

t_id.kemenag: Maka tatkala dia datang kepada mereka dengan membawa mukjizat-mukjizat Kami dengan serta merta mereka mentertawakannya.
t_id.sabiq: Dan ketika Mûsâ datang membawa mukjizat yang menguatkan misi kerasulannya, mereka segera menyambut kedatanganya dengan tertawa, sebagai cemoohan dan ejekan, bukan malah merenungkannya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala dia datang kepada mereka dengan membawa mukjirat-mukjizat Kami) yang menunjukkan kebenaran risalah-Nya (dengan serta merta mereka menertawakannya.)
t_id.iwq: Maka tatkala dia datang kepada mereka dengan membawa mukjizat-mukjizat Kami dengan serta merta mereka mentertawakannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلما جاءهم بآياتنا» الدالة على رسالته «إذا هم منها يضحكون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُم بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآ إِذَا هُم مِّنۡهَا يَضۡحَكُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala dia datang kepada mereka dengan membawa mukjirat-mukjizat Kami) yang menunjukkan kebenaran risalah-Nya (dengan serta merta mereka menertawakannya)

ar: وَمَا نُرِیهِم مِّنۡ ءَایَةٍ إِلَّا هِیَ أَكۡبَرُ مِنۡ أُخۡتِهَاۖ وَأَخَذۡنَـٰهُم بِٱلۡعَذَابِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ یَرۡجِعُونَ
ar_simple: وَمَا نُرِيهِمْ مِنْ آيَةٍ إِلَّا هِيَ أَكْبَرُ مِنْ أُخْتِهَا ۖ وَأَخَذْنَاهُمْ بِالْعَذَابِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
transliteration: wamā nurīhim min āyatin illā hiya akbaru min ukh'tihā wa-akhadhnāhum bil-ʿadhābi laʿallahum yarjiʿūna

t.sahih-int: And We showed them not a sign except that it was greater than its sister, and We seized them with affliction that perhaps they might return [to faith]
t.asad: although each sign that We showed them was weightier than the preceding one: and [each time] We took them to task through suffering, so that they might return [to Us]
t.yusuf-ali: We showed them Sign after Sign, each greater than its fellow, and We seized them with Punishment, in order that they might turn (to Us)
t.pickthall: And every token that We showed them was greater than its sister (token), and We grasped them with the torment, that haply they might turn again
t.arberry: And not a sign We showed them, but it was greater than its sister sign; and We seized them with chastisement, that haply they should return
t.hilali-khan: And not an Ayah (sign, etc.) We showed them but it was greater than its fellow, and We seized them with torment, in order that they might turn [from their polytheism to Allah's Religion (Islamic Monotheism]
t.itani: Each sign We showed them was more marvelous than its counterpart. And We afflicted them with the plagues, so that they may repent.
t.mubarakpuri: And not an Ayah We showed them but it was greater than its fellow preceding it, and We seized them with torment, in order that they might turn.
t.qarib: Each sign We showed them was more marvelous than the previous one. So We afflicted them with punishment, so that they might return
t.wahiduddin: even though each sign We showed them was greater than the previous one. We afflicted them with torment so that they might return [to the right path]
t.shakir: And We did not show them a sign but it was greater than its like, and We overtook them with chastisement that they may turn
t.haleem: even though each sign We showed them was greater than the previous one. We inflicted torment on them so that they might return to the right path
t.maariful: And We did not show them a sign, but it used to be greater than the one that preceded it. And We seized them with punishment, so that they may return
t.khattab: although every sign We showed them was greater than the one before. Ultimately, We seized them with torments so that they might return ˹to the Right Path˺
t.ghali: And in no way did We show them any sign except that it was greater than its sister sign; and We took (hold) of them with the torment, that possibly they would return
t.irving: Yet We did not show them any sign unless it was even greater than the one that had preceded it. We afflicted them with torment so that they might return
t.iwq: And We showed them not a sign except that it was greater than its sister, and We seized them with affliction that perhaps they might return to faith

t_id.kemenag: Dan tidaklah Kami perlihatkan kepada mereka sesuatu mukjizat kecuali mukjizat itu lebih besar dari mukjizat-mukjizat yang sebelumnya. Dan Kami timpakan kepada mereka azab supaya mereka kembali (ke jalan yang benar).
t_id.sabiq: Setiap mukjizat yang datang menyusul mukjizat-mukjizat sebelumnya selalu lebih besar dari yang sebelumnya itu. Karena, memang, mukjizat-mukjizat itu amat jelas membuktikan kebenaran rasul, dapat menjelaskan sendiri maksud itu dan karena begitu sempurnanya. Sehingga, jika diperhatikan, akan dikatakan, "Mukjizat itu lebih besar dari mukjizat sebelumnya." Dan ketika mereka tetap berada dalam kesewenang-wenangan, Kami pun menimpakan berbagai macam musibah dan bencana kepada mereka agar mereka meninggalkan kesesatan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidaklah Kami perlihatkan kepada mereka sesuatu tanda) yang menunjukkan azab Kami seperti banjir, topan, yaitu berupa air bah yang melanda rumah-rumah mereka yang ketinggiannya mencapai leher orang yang sedang duduk, hal ini berlangsung selama. tujuh hari, dan juga belalang-belalang yang memusnahkan tanaman-tanaman mereka (melainkan tanda atau azab itu lebih besar daripada azab-azab lainnya) yang sebelumnya. (Dan Kami timpakan kepada mereka azab supaya mereka kembali) sadar dari kekafirannya.
t_id.iwq: Dan tidaklah Kami perlihatkan kepada mereka sesuatu mukjizat kecuali mukjizat itu lebih besar dari mukjizat-mukjizat yang sebelumnya. Dan Kami timpakan kepada mereka azab supaya mereka kembali (ke jalan yang benar).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وما نريهم من آية» من آيات العذاب كالطوفان، وهو ماء دخل بيوتهم ووصل إلى حلوق الجالسين سبعة أيام، والجراد «إلا هي أكبر من أختها» قرينتها التي قبلها «وأخذناهم بالعذاب لعلهم يرجعون» عن الكفر
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَا نُرِيهِم مِّنۡ ءَايَةٍ إِلَّا هِيَ أَكۡبَرُ مِنۡ أُخۡتِهَاۖ وَأَخَذۡنَٰهُم بِٱلۡعَذَابِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَرۡجِعُونَ (آيَةٍ: حُجَّةٍ عَلَى صِدْقِ دَعْوَتِهِ, بِالْعَذَابِ: مِنَ الجَرَادِ، وَالقُمَّلِ، وَالضَّفَادِعِ، وَنَحْوِهَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidaklah Kami perlihatkan kepada mereka sesuatu tanda) yang menunjukkan azab Kami seperti banjir, topan, yaitu berupa air bah yang melanda rumah-rumah mereka yang ketinggiannya mencapai leher orang yang sedang duduk, hal ini berlangsung selama. tujuh hari, dan juga belalang-belalang yang memusnahkan tanaman-tanaman mereka (melainkan tanda atau azab itu lebih besar daripada azab-azab lainnya) yang sebelumnya. (Dan Kami timpakan kepada mereka azab supaya mereka kembali) sadar dari kekafirannya

ar: وَقَالُوا۟ یَـٰۤأَیُّهَ ٱلسَّاحِرُ ٱدۡعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ بِمَا عَهِدَ عِندَكَ إِنَّنَا لَمُهۡتَدُونَ
ar_simple: وَقَالُوا يَا أَيُّهَ السَّاحِرُ ادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ بِمَا عَهِدَ عِنْدَكَ إِنَّنَا لَمُهْتَدُونَ
transliteration: waqālū yāayyuha l-sāḥiru ud'ʿu lanā rabbaka bimā ʿahida ʿindaka innanā lamuh'tadūna

t.sahih-int: And they said [to Moses], "O magician, invoke for us your Lord by what He has promised you. Indeed, we will be guided
t.asad: And [every time] they exclaimed: “O thou sorcerer! Pray for us to thy Sustainer on the strength of the covenant [of prophethood] which He has made with thee: for, verily, we shall now follow the right way!”
t.yusuf-ali: And they said, "O thou sorcerer! Invoke thy Lord for us according to His covenant with thee; for we shall truly accept guidance
t.pickthall: And they said: O wizard! Entreat thy Lord for us by the pact that He hath made with thee. Lo! we verily will walk aright
t.arberry: And they said, 'Sorcerer, pray to thy Lord for us by the covenant He has made with thee, and surely we shall be right-guided
t.hilali-khan: And they said [to Musa (Moses)]: "O you sorcerer! Invoke your Lord for us according to what He has covenanted with you. Verily, We shall guide ourselves (aright)
t.itani: They said, “O sorcerer, pray to your Lord for us, according to His pledge to you, and then we will be guided.”
t.mubarakpuri: And they said (to Musa): "O you sorcerer! Invoke your Lord for us according to what He has made a pact with you. Verily, We shall guide ourselves."
t.qarib: They said "Magician, pray to your Lord for us, as He has a covenant with you, and We will be guided
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Sorcerer, call on your Lord for us, by virtue of His pledge to you: we shall certainly accept guidance
t.shakir: And they said: O magician! call on your Lord for our sake, as He has made the covenant with you; we shall surely be the followers of the right way
t.haleem: They said, ‘Sorcerer, call on your Lord for us, by virtue of His pledge to you: we shall certainly accept guidance,’
t.maariful: And they said, “O magician, pray for us to your Lord by the covenant He has made with you; we will certainly come to guidance.”
t.khattab: ˹Then˺ they pleaded, “O ˹mighty˺ magician! Pray to your Lord on our behalf, by virtue of the covenant He made with you. We will certainly accept guidance.”
t.ghali: And they said, "O you sorcerer, invoke your Lord for us by what He has covenanted in your presence. Surely we (ourselves) will indeed be right-guided
t.irving: They said: "Mr. Magician, appeal to your Lord for us concerning what He had pledged to you; we will act as if we are guided
t.iwq: And they said to Moses, "O magician, invoke for us your Lord by what He has promised you. Indeed, we will be guided

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka berkata: "Hai ahli sihir, berdoalah kepada Tuhanmu untuk (melepaskan) kami sesuai dengan apa yang telah dijanjikan-Nya kepadamu; sesungguhnya kami (jika doamu dikabulkan) benar-benar akan menjadi orang yang mendapat petunjuk.
t_id.sabiq: Ketika berbagai macam musibah dan bencana itu mengenai semua mereka secara merata, mereka pun meminta pertolongan kepada Mûsâ dengan mengatakan, "Wahai tukang sihir,"--padahal Mûsâ adalah seorang alim, bukan tukang sihir--"mintakanlah untuk kami kepada Tuhanmu seraya bertawassul dengan janji-Nya kepadamu agar Dia melepaskan siksaan ini dari kami. Kalau siksaan ini dilepaskan, kami pasti akan mau mengikuti petunjuk."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata) kepada Musa tatkala mereka melihat adanya azab itu, ("Hai ahli sihir!) maksudnya, hai orang yang alim lagi sempurna ilmunya! Dikatakan demikian karena menurut mereka ilmu sihir itu adalah ilmu yang paling diagungkan di kalaagan mereka (Berdoalah kepada Rabbmu untuk kami, sesuai dengan apa yang telah dijanjikan-Nya kepadamu) yakni Dia akan melepaskan kami dari azab ini jika kami beriman (sesungguhnya kami benar-benar akan menjadi orang-orang yang mendapat petunjuk") atau mau beriman.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka berkata: "Hai ahli sihir, berdoalah kepada Tuhanmu untuk (melepaskan) kami sesuai dengan apa yang telah dijanjikan-Nya kepadamu; sesungguhnya kami (jika doamu dikabulkan) benar-benar akan menjadi orang yang mendapat petunjuk.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقالوا» لموسى لما رأوا العذاب «يا أيها الساحر» أي العالم الكامل لأن السحر عندهم علم عظيم «ادع لنا ربك بما عهد عندك» عن كشف العذاب عنا إن آمنا «إننا لمهتدون» أي مؤمنون
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالُواْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَ ٱلسَّاحِرُ ٱدۡعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ بِمَا عَهِدَ عِندَكَ إِنَّنَا لَمُهۡتَدُونَ (السَّاحِرُ: العَالِمُ (وَكَانَ السَّاحِرُ فِيهِمْ عَظِيمًا يُوَقِّرُونَهُ، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ صِفَةَ ذَمٍّ), بِمَا عَهِدَ عِندَكَ: بِعَهْدِهِ الَّذِي عَهِدَ إِلَيْكَ، وَمَا خَصَّكَ بِهِ مِنَ الفَضَائِلِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata) kepada Musa tatkala mereka melihat adanya azab itu, ("Hai ahli sihir!) maksudnya, hai orang yang alim lagi sempurna ilmunya! Dikatakan demikian karena menurut mereka ilmu sihir itu adalah ilmu yang paling diagungkan di kalaagan mereka (Berdoalah kepada Rabbmu untuk kami, sesuai dengan apa yang telah dijanjikan-Nya kepadamu) yakni Dia akan melepaskan kami dari azab ini jika kami beriman (sesungguhnya kami benar-benar akan menjadi orang-orang yang mendapat petunjuk") atau mau beriman

ar: فَلَمَّا كَشَفۡنَا عَنۡهُمُ ٱلۡعَذَابَ إِذَا هُمۡ یَنكُثُونَ
ar_simple: فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابَ إِذَا هُمْ يَنْكُثُونَ
transliteration: falammā kashafnā ʿanhumu l-ʿadhāba idhā hum yankuthūna

t.sahih-int: But when We removed from them the affliction, at once they broke their word
t.asad: But whenever We removed the suffering from them, lo! they would break their word
t.yusuf-ali: But when We removed the Penalty from them, behold, they broke their word
t.pickthall: But when We eased them of the torment, behold! they broke their word
t.arberry: But when We removed from them the chastisement, behold, they broke their troth
t.hilali-khan: But when We removed the torment from them, behold! They broke their covenant (that they will believe if We remove the torment for them)
t.itani: But when We lifted the torment from them, they immediately broke their promise.
t.mubarakpuri: But when We removed the torment from them, behold, they broke their covenant.
t.qarib: But when We lifted the punishment from them, they broke their word
t.wahiduddin: but when We relieved them of the torment, they broke their word
t.shakir: But when We removed from them the chastisement, lo! they broke the pledge
t.haleem: but as soon as We relieved their torment they broke their word
t.maariful: Then, once We removed the punishment from them, in no time they broke their promise
t.khattab: But as soon as We removed the torments from them, they broke their promise
t.ghali: Yet, as soon as We lifted off them the torment, only then did they breach (their promise)
t.irving: Yet once We removed torment from them, why they broke their word
t.iwq: But when We removed from them the affliction, at once they broke their word

t_id.kemenag: Maka tatkala Kami hilangkan azab itu dari mereka, dengan serta merta mereka memungkiri (janjinya).
t_id.sabiq: Tetapi ketika Allah melepaskan penderitan mereka berkat doa Mûsâ, serta merta mereka mengingkari janjinya untuk beriman.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala Kami hilangkan) berkat doa Musa (azab itu dari mereka, dengan serta merta mereka memungkiri) janjinya, bahkan mereka masih tetap melaju di dalam kekafirannya.
t_id.iwq: Maka tatkala Kami hilangkan azab itu dari mereka, dengan serta merta mereka memungkiri (janjinya).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلما كشفنا» بدعاء موسى «عنهم العذاب إذا هم ينكثون» ينقضون عهدهم ويصرون على كفرهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَمَّا كَشَفۡنَا عَنۡهُمُ ٱلۡعَذَابَ إِذَا هُمۡ يَنكُثُونَ (يَنكُثُونَ: يَغْدِرُونَ، وَيُصِرُّونَ عَلَى الكُفْرِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala Kami hilangkan) berkat doa Musa (azab itu dari mereka, dengan serta merta mereka memungkiri) janjinya, bahkan mereka masih tetap melaju di dalam kekafirannya

ar: وَنَادَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنُ فِی قَوۡمِهِۦ قَالَ یَـٰقَوۡمِ أَلَیۡسَ لِی مُلۡكُ مِصۡرَ وَهَـٰذِهِ ٱلۡأَنۡهَـٰرُ تَجۡرِی مِن تَحۡتِیۤۚ أَفَلَا تُبۡصِرُونَ
ar_simple: وَنَادَىٰ فِرْعَوْنُ فِي قَوْمِهِ قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ أَلَيْسَ لِي مُلْكُ مِصْرَ وَهَٰذِهِ الْأَنْهَارُ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِي ۖ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ
transliteration: wanādā fir'ʿawnu fī qawmihi qāla yāqawmi alaysa lī mul'ku miṣ'ra wahādhihi l-anhāru tajrī min taḥtī afalā tub'ṣirūna

t.sahih-int: And Pharaoh called out among his people; he said, "O my people, does not the kingdom of Egypt belong to me, and these rivers flowing beneath me; then do you not see
t.asad: And Pharaoh issued a call to his people, saying: “O my people! Does not the dominion over Egypt belong to me, since all these running waters flow at my feet? Can you not, then, see [that I am your lord supreme]
t.yusuf-ali: And Pharaoh proclaimed among his people, saying: "O my people! Does not the dominion of Egypt belong to me, (witness) these streams flowing underneath my (palace)? What! see ye not then
t.pickthall: And Pharaoh caused a proclamation to be made among his people saying: O my people! Is not mine the sovereignty of Egypt and these rivers flowing under me? Can ye not then discern
t.arberry: And Pharaoh proclaimed among his people: 'O my people, do I not possess the kingdom of Egypt, and these rivers flowing beneath me? What, do you not see
t.hilali-khan: And Fir'aun (Pharaoh) proclaimed among his people, saying: "O my people! Is not mine the dominion of Egypt, and these rivers flowing underneath me. See you not then
t.itani: Pharaoh proclaimed among his people, saying, “O my people, do I not own the Kingdom of Egypt, and these rivers flow beneath me? Do you not see?
t.mubarakpuri: And Fir`awn proclaimed among his people (saying): "O my people! Is not mine the dominion of Egypt, and these rivers flowing underneath me. See you not then"
t.qarib: Then Pharaoh called out among his people; he said, "My people, does not the kingdom of Egypt belong to me, and these rivers that flow under my feet? Do not you see
t.wahiduddin: Pharaoh called to his people, "My people, is the Kingdom of Egypt not mine? And these rivers that flow at my feet, are they not mine? Do you not see
t.shakir: And Firon proclaimed amongst his people: O my people! is not the kingdom of Egypt mine? And these rivers flow beneath me; do you not then see
t.haleem: Pharaoh proclaimed to his people, ‘My people, is the Kingdom of Egypt not mine? And these rivers that flow at my feet, are they not mine? Do you not see
t.maariful: And Fir’aun (Pharaoh) proclaimed among his people, saying, “Does not the kingdom of Egypt belong to me, and these rivers are flowing right underneath me? Do you, then, not discern
t.khattab: And Pharaoh called out to his people, boasting, “O my people! Am I not sovereign over Egypt as well as ˹all˺ these streams flowing at my feet? Can you not see
t.ghali: And Firaawn (Pharaoh) called out upon his people (and) said, "O my people, is not the kingdom of Misr (i.e., Egypt) mine, and are not these rivers running from beneath me? Do you not then behold
t.irving: Pharaoh announced it among his people; he said: "My people, do I not hold control over Egypt and these rivers flowing by me? Do you not notice anything
t.iwq: And Pharaoh called out among his people; he said, "O my people, does not the kingdom of Egypt belong to me, and these rivers flowing beneath me; then do you not see

t_id.kemenag: Dan Fir'aun berseru kepada kaumnya (seraya) berkata: "Hai kaumku, bukankah kerajaan Mesir ini kepunyaanku dan (bukankah) sungai-sungai ini mengalir di bawahku; maka apakah kamu tidak melihat(nya)?
t_id.sabiq: Dan Fir'aun pun berseru di hadapan pengikut-pengikutnya sambil mendeklarasikan kekuatan dan kekuasaanya, "Bukankah negeri Mesir ini milikku, bukan milik yang lain? Bukankah sungai-sungai yang kalian saksikan ini mengalir dari bawah istanaku? Apakah kalian buta, sehingga tidak dapat melihat kekuatanku dan kelemahan Mûsâ?" Dengan seruan itu, Fir'aun bermaksud ingin menanamkan pengaruh pada diri mereka supaya patuh kepadanya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Firaun berseru) dengan nada penuh kesombongan (kepada kaumnya seraya berkata, "Hai kaumku, bukankah kerajaan Mesir ini kepunyaanku dan sungai-sungai ini) yaitu sungai Nil dan anak-anaknya (mengalir di bawahku) di bawah keraton-keratonku, adalah kepunyaanku juga (maka apakah kalian tidak melihat) keagungan dan kebesaranku?
t_id.iwq: Dan Fir'aun berseru kepada kaumnya (seraya) berkata: "Hai kaumku, bukankah kerajaan Mesir ini kepunyaanku dan (bukankah) sungai-sungai ini mengalir di bawahku; maka apakah kamu tidak melihat(nya)?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ونادى فرعون» افتخاراً «في قومه قال يا قوم أليس لي ملك مصر وهذه الأنهار» من النيل «تجري من تحتي» أي تحت قصوري «أفلا تبصرون» عظمتي
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَنَادَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنُ فِي قَوۡمِهِۦ قَالَ يَٰقَوۡمِ أَلَيۡسَ لِي مُلۡكُ مِصۡرَ وَهَٰذِهِ ٱلۡأَنۡهَٰرُ تَجۡرِي مِن تَحۡتِيٓۚ أَفَلَا تُبۡصِرُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Firaun berseru) dengan nada penuh kesombongan (kepada kaumnya seraya berkata, "Hai kaumku, bukankah kerajaan Mesir ini kepunyaanku dan sungai-sungai ini) yaitu sungai Nil dan anak-anaknya (mengalir di bawahku) di bawah keraton-keratonku, adalah kepunyaanku juga (maka apakah kalian tidak melihat) keagungan dan kebesaranku

ar: أَمۡ أَنَا۠ خَیۡرࣱ مِّنۡ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِی هُوَ مَهِینࣱ وَلَا یَكَادُ یُبِینُ
ar_simple: أَمْ أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِنْ هَٰذَا الَّذِي هُوَ مَهِينٌ وَلَا يَكَادُ يُبِينُ
transliteration: am anā khayrun min hādhā alladhī huwa mahīnun walā yakādu yubīnu

t.sahih-int: Or am I [not] better than this one who is insignificant and hardly makes himself clear
t.asad: Am I not better than this contemptible man who can hardly make his meaning clear
t.yusuf-ali: Am I not better than this (Moses), who is a contemptible wretch and can scarcely express himself clearly
t.pickthall: I am surely better than this fellow, who is despicable and can hardly make (his meaning) plain
t.arberry: Or am I better than this man, who is contemptible and scarcely makes things clear
t.hilali-khan: Am I not better than this one [Musa (Moses)], who is Mahin [has no honour nor any respect, and is weak and despicable] and can scarcely express himself clearly
t.itani: Am I not better than this miserable wretch, who can barely express himself?
t.mubarakpuri: "Am I not better than this one (Musa) who is despicable and can scarcely express himself clearly"
t.qarib: Am I not better than this insignificant person who can barely make himself understood
t.wahiduddin: Am I not better than this contemptible man who can hardly make his meaning clear
t.shakir: Nay! I am better than this fellow, who is contemptible, and who can hardly speak distinctly
t.haleem: Am I not better than this contemptible wretch who can scarcely express himself
t.maariful: Or (do you not see that) I am much better than this one (Mūsā) who is worthless and can hardly express himself
t.khattab: Am I not better than this nobody who can hardly express himself
t.ghali: Or even am I (not) more charitable (i.e., better) than this (fellow), who is contemptile and almost cannot make (his meaning) evident
t.irving: Surely I am better than this wretch who can scarcely explain a thing
t.iwq: Or am I not better than this one who is insignificant and hardly makes himself clear

t_id.kemenag: Bukankah aku lebih baik dari orang yang hina ini dan yang hampir tidak dapat menjelaskan (perkataannya)?
t_id.sabiq: Lebih dari itu, Fir'aun bahkan berkata, "Aku bahkan lebih baik dari orang yang lemah dan hina-dina yang hampir saja tidak mampu menjelaskan dakwanya dengan bahasa yang fasih."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Bukankah) kalian telah melihat sesudah kesemuanya itu (aku lebih baik dari orang ini) dari Nabi Musa (yang dia adalah orang hina) lemah lagi hina (dan yang hampir tidak dapat berbicara dengan jelas) tidak dapat menjelaskan perkataannya, karena sewaktu kecil ia pernah memakan bara api, hingga lisannya pelan atau tidak fasih.
t_id.iwq: Bukankah aku lebih baik dari orang yang hina ini dan yang hampir tidak dapat menjelaskan (perkataannya)?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم» تبصرون، وحينئذ «أنا خير من هذا» أي موسى «الذي هو مهين» ضعيف حقير «و يكاد يُبين» يظهر كلامه للثغته بالجمرة التي تناولها في صغره
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ أَنَا۠ خَيۡرٞ مِّنۡ هَٰذَا ٱلَّذِي هُوَ مَهِينٞ وَلَا يَكَادُ يُبِينُ (مَهِينٌ: ضَعِيفٌ لَا عِزَّ لَهُ, وَلَا يَكَادُ يُبِينُ: لَا يَكَادُ يُفْصِحُ فِي كَلَامِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Bukankah) kalian telah melihat sesudah kesemuanya itu (aku lebih baik dari orang ini) dari Nabi Musa (yang dia adalah orang hina) lemah lagi hina (dan yang hampir tidak dapat berbicara dengan jelas) tidak dapat menjelaskan perkataannya, karena sewaktu kecil ia pernah memakan bara api, hingga lisannya pelan atau tidak fasih

ar: فَلَوۡلَاۤ أُلۡقِیَ عَلَیۡهِ أَسۡوِرَةࣱ مِّن ذَهَبٍ أَوۡ جَاۤءَ مَعَهُ ٱلۡمَلَـٰۤئِكَةُ مُقۡتَرِنِینَ
ar_simple: فَلَوْلَا أُلْقِيَ عَلَيْهِ أَسْوِرَةٌ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ أَوْ جَاءَ مَعَهُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ مُقْتَرِنِينَ
transliteration: falawlā ul'qiya ʿalayhi aswiratun min dhahabin aw jāa maʿahu l-malāikatu muq'tarinīna

t.sahih-int: Then why have there not been placed upon him bracelets of gold or come with him the angels in conjunction
t.asad: “And then - why have no golden armlets been bestowed on him? or why have no angels come together with him?”
t.yusuf-ali: Then why are not gold bracelets bestowed on him, or (why) come (not) with him angels accompanying him in procession
t.pickthall: Why, then, have armlets of gold not been set upon him, or angels sent along with him
t.arberry: Why then have bracelets of gold not been cast on him, or angels not come with him conjoined
t.hilali-khan: Why then are not golden bracelets bestowed on him, or angels sent along with him
t.itani: Why are bracelets of gold not dropped on him, or they angels came with him in procession?”
t.mubarakpuri: Why then are not golden bracelets bestowed on him, or angels sent along with him"
t.qarib: Why then are not golden bracelets placed on him, or the angels are accompanying him
t.wahiduddin: and why have not armlets of gold been bestowed on him and why is there not a train of angels accompanying him
t.shakir: But why have not bracelets of gold been put upon him, or why have there not come with him angels as companions
t.haleem: Why has he not been given any gold bracelets? Why have no angels come to accompany him?’
t.maariful: So why were the bracelets of gold not sent down to him, or (why) did the angels not come along with him as companions?”
t.khattab: Why then have no golden bracelets ˹of kingship˺ been granted to him or angels come with him as escorts!”
t.ghali: So, had armlets of gold been cast (i.e., bestoned on him) on him, or Angels had come with him in comradeship
t.irving: If he were only presented with gold bracelets or some angels came along hitched up with him!´
t.iwq: Then why have there not been placed upon him bracelets of gold or come with him the angels in conjunction

t_id.kemenag: Mengapa tidak dipakaikan kepadanya gelang dari emas atau malaikat datang bersama-sama dia untuk mengiringkannya?"
t_id.sabiq: Fir'aun berkata pula, seraya mengajak pengikutnya untuk mendustakan Mûsâ, "Mengapa Tuhan Mûsâ tidak menyematkan gelang emas kepadanya untuk dapat mengendalikan segala persoalan? Atau, mengapa Tuhannya tidak menurunkan bantuan kepadanya berupa malaikat yang menguatkan dakwaannya bahwa ia benar-benar membawa misi kerasulan?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mengapa tidak) kenapa tidak dipakaikan kepadanya jika memang ia orang yang benar di dalam pengakuannya (gelang dari emas) lafal Asawirah adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Aswiratun yang wazannya sama dengan lafal Aghribatun, dan lafal Aswiratun ini merupakan bentuk jamak pula dari lafal Siwarun. Maksud Firaun, mengapa tidak dipakaikan kepadanya gelang dari emas sebagaimana kebiasaan orang-orang yang diberi kekuasaan olehnya, yaitu orang tersebut diberi pakaian kebesaran yang terbuat dari emas dan pula dipakaikan kepadanya gelang emas sebagai tanda kedudukannya (atau malaikat datang bersama-sama dia mengiringkannya") datang berturut-turut kepadanya seraya menyatakan kebenaran kerasulannya.
t_id.iwq: Mengapa tidak dipakaikan kepadanya gelang dari emas atau malaikat datang bersama-sama dia untuk mengiringkannya?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلولا» هلا «ألقي عليه» إن كان صادقاً «أسورة من ذهب» جمع أسورة كأغربة جمع سوار كعادتهم فيمن يسودونه أن يلبسوه أسورة ذهب ويطوقونه طوق ذهب «أو جاء معه الملائكة مقترنين» متتابعين يشهدون بصدقه
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَوۡلَآ أُلۡقِيَ عَلَيۡهِ أَسۡوِرَةٞ مِّن ذَهَبٍ أَوۡ جَآءَ مَعَهُ ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ مُقۡتَرِنِينَ (مُقْتَرِنِينَ: مَقْرُونِينَ مَعَهُ يُصَدِّقُونَهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mengapa tidak) kenapa tidak dipakaikan kepadanya jika memang ia orang yang benar di dalam pengakuannya (gelang dari emas) lafal Asawirah adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Aswiratun yang wazannya sama dengan lafal Aghribatun, dan lafal Aswiratun ini merupakan bentuk jamak pula dari lafal Siwarun. Maksud Firaun, mengapa tidak dipakaikan kepadanya gelang dari emas sebagaimana kebiasaan orang-orang yang diberi kekuasaan olehnya, yaitu orang tersebut diberi pakaian kebesaran yang terbuat dari emas dan pula dipakaikan kepadanya gelang emas sebagai tanda kedudukannya (atau malaikat datang bersama-sama dia mengiringkannya") datang berturut-turut kepadanya seraya menyatakan kebenaran kerasulannya

ar: فَٱسۡتَخَفَّ قَوۡمَهُۥ فَأَطَاعُوهُۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُوا۟ قَوۡمࣰا فَـٰسِقِینَ
ar_simple: فَاسْتَخَفَّ قَوْمَهُ فَأَطَاعُوهُ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا قَوْمًا فَاسِقِينَ
transliteration: fa-is'takhaffa qawmahu fa-aṭāʿūhu innahum kānū qawman fāsiqīna

t.sahih-int: So he bluffed his people, and they obeyed him. Indeed, they were [themselves] a people defiantly disobedient [of Allah]
t.asad: Thus he incited his people to levity, and they obeyed him: for, behold, they were people depraved
t.yusuf-ali: Thus did he make fools of his people, and they obeyed him: truly were they a people rebellious (against Allah)
t.pickthall: Thus he persuaded his people to make light (of Moses), and they obeyed him. Lo! they were a wanton folk
t.arberry: So he made his people unsteady, and they obeyed him; surely they were an ungodly people
t.hilali-khan: Thus he [Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] befooled and misled his people, and they obeyed him. Verily, they were ever a people who were Fasiqun (rebellious, disobedient to Allah)
t.itani: Thus he fooled his people, and they obeyed him. They were wicked people.
t.mubarakpuri: Thus he fooled his people, and they obeyed him. Verily, they were ever a people who were rebellious.
t.qarib: He [Pharaoh] easily swayed his people, and they obeyed him. [To Us] they were disobedient people
t.wahiduddin: In this way he fooled his people and they obeyed him: they were a rebellious people
t.shakir: So he incited his people to levity and they obeyed him: surely they were a transgressing people
t.haleem: In this way he moved his people to accept and they obeyed him- they were perverse people
t.maariful: Thus he made fool of his people, and they obeyed him. Surely they were a sinful people
t.khattab: And so he fooled his people, and they obeyed him. They were truly a rebellious people
t.ghali: Then he induced levity (i.e., missed his people) on his people, so they obeyed him; surely they were an immoral people
t.irving: He sneered at his folk, yet they obeyed him; they were such immoral folk
t.iwq: So he bluffed his people, and they obeyed him. Indeed, they were themselves a people defiantly disobedient of Allah

t_id.kemenag: Maka Fir'aun mempengaruhi kaumnya (dengan perkataan itu) lalu mereka patuh kepadanya. Karena sesungguhnya mereka adalah kaum yang fasik.
t_id.sabiq: Dengan ucapannya itu, Fir'aun telah memprovokasi dan mempengaruhi pengikut-pengikutnya, sehingga mereka ikut terbawa dalam kesesatan. Sungguh mereka benar-benar kaum yang keluar dari agama Allah yang lurus.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Firaun mempengaruhi) berupaya menanamkan pengaruhnya kepada (kaumnya, lalu mereka patuh kepadanya) mematuhi apa yang dikehendaki oleh Firaun, yaitu mendustakan Musa (karena sesungguhnya mereka adalah kaum yang fasik.)
t_id.iwq: Maka Fir'aun mempengaruhi kaumnya (dengan perkataan itu) lalu mereka patuh kepadanya. Karena sesungguhnya mereka adalah kaum yang fasik.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فاستخف» استفز فرعون «قومه فأطاعوه» فيما يريد من تكذيب موسى «إنهم كانوا قوماً فاسقين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَٱسۡتَخَفَّ قَوۡمَهُۥ فَأَطَاعُوهُۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ قَوۡمٗا فَٰسِقِينَ (فَاسْتَخَفَّ قَوْمَهُ: اسْتَخَفَّ بِعُقُولِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Firaun mempengaruhi) berupaya menanamkan pengaruhnya kepada (kaumnya, lalu mereka patuh kepadanya) mematuhi apa yang dikehendaki oleh Firaun, yaitu mendustakan Musa (karena sesungguhnya mereka adalah kaum yang fasik)

ar: فَلَمَّاۤ ءَاسَفُونَا ٱنتَقَمۡنَا مِنۡهُمۡ فَأَغۡرَقۡنَـٰهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِینَ
ar_simple: فَلَمَّا آسَفُونَا انْتَقَمْنَا مِنْهُمْ فَأَغْرَقْنَاهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
transliteration: falammā āsafūnā intaqamnā min'hum fa-aghraqnāhum ajmaʿīna

t.sahih-int: And when they angered Us, We took retribution from them and drowned them all
t.asad: But when they continued to challenge Us, We inflicted Our retribution on them, and drowned them all
t.yusuf-ali: When at length they provoked Us, We exacted retribution from them, and We drowned them all
t.pickthall: So, when they angered Us, We punished them and drowned them every one
t.arberry: So, when they had angered Us, We took vengeance on them, and We drowned them all together
t.hilali-khan: So when they angered Us, We punished them, and drowned them all
t.itani: And when they provoked Our wrath, We took retribution from them, and We drowned them all.
t.mubarakpuri: So when they angered Us, We punished them, and drowned them all.
t.qarib: but when they continued to challenge Us, We inflicted Our punishment on them, and drowned them all
t.wahiduddin: Then when they provoked Our wrath, We took revenge on them and drowned every one of them
t.shakir: Then when they displeased Us, We inflicted a retribution on them, so We drowned them all together
t.haleem: When they provoked Us, We punished and drowned them all
t.maariful: So, when they provoked Our anger, We took vengeance on them, and drowned them all together
t.khattab: So when they enraged Us, We inflicted punishment upon them, drowning them all
t.ghali: Then as soon as they provoked Our anger, (Or: behaved foolishly towards Us) We took vengeance on them; so We drowned them all together
t.irving: When they provoked Us [sufficiently], We took revenge on them and let them all drown
t.iwq: And when they angered Us, We took retribution from them and drowned them all

t_id.kemenag: Maka tatkala mereka membuat Kami murka, Kami menghukum mereka lalu kami tenggelamkan mereka semuanya (di laut),
t_id.sabiq: Ketika mereka membuat Kami sangat murka karena telah melampaui batas kerusakan, Kami pun memberikan balasan dengan cara menenggelamkan mereka semua.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala mereka membuat Kami murka) (Kami menghukum mereka lalu Kami tenggelamkan mereka semuanya di laut.)
t_id.iwq: Maka tatkala mereka membuat Kami murka, Kami menghukum mereka lalu kami tenggelamkan mereka semuanya (di laut),

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلما آسفونا» أغضبونا «انتقمنا منهم فأغرقناهم أجمعين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَمَّآ ءَاسَفُونَا ٱنتَقَمۡنَا مِنۡهُمۡ فَأَغۡرَقۡنَٰهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ (آسَفُونَا: أَغْضَبُونَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala mereka membuat Kami murka) (Kami menghukum mereka lalu Kami tenggelamkan mereka semuanya di laut)

ar: فَجَعَلۡنَـٰهُمۡ سَلَفࣰا وَمَثَلࣰا لِّلۡءَاخِرِینَ
ar_simple: فَجَعَلْنَاهُمْ سَلَفًا وَمَثَلًا لِلْآخِرِينَ
transliteration: fajaʿalnāhum salafan wamathalan lil'ākhirīna

t.sahih-int: And We made them a precedent and an example for the later peoples
t.asad: and so We made them a thing of the past, and an example to those who would come after them
t.yusuf-ali: And We made them (a people) of the Past and an Example to later ages
t.pickthall: And We made them a thing past, and an example for those after (them)
t.arberry: and We made them a thing past, and We appointed them for an example to later folk
t.hilali-khan: And We made them a precedent (as a lesson for those coming after them), and an example to later generations
t.itani: Thus We made them a precedent and an example for the others.
t.mubarakpuri: And We made them a precedent, and an example to later generations.
t.qarib: We made them a precedent and an example for the others
t.wahiduddin: We made them a precedent, an example for later peoples
t.shakir: And We made them a precedent and example to the later generations
t.haleem: We made them a lesson and an example for later people
t.maariful: and made them a people of the past, and an example for the later generations
t.khattab: And We made them an example and a lesson for those after them
t.ghali: So We made them a bygone (precedent) and a similitude to the later (generations)
t.irving: We left them as has-beens and an example for later men
t.iwq: And We made them a precedent and an example for the later peoples

t_id.kemenag: dan Kami jadikan mereka sebagai pelajaran dan contoh bagi orang-orang yang kemudian.
t_id.sabiq: Fir'aun dan pengikutnya Kami jadikan sebagai pelajaran bagi orang-orang kafir setelahnya dalam menerima siksaan yang serupa dengan mereka. Selain itu, mereka juga Kami jadikan sebagai bahan pembicaraan luar biasa yang dapat menjadi pelajaran bagi seluruh umat manusia.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami jadikan mereka sebagai pelajaran) lafal Salafan merupakan bentuk jamak dari lafal salifun, wazannya sama dengan lafal Khadimun atau pelayan, yang jamaknya adalah Khadamun; yakni orang-orang terdahulu yang dijadikan sebagai pelajaran (dan contoh bagi orang-orang yang kemudian) sesudah mereka, di mana orang-orang yang sesudah mereka itu dapat mengambil contoh dari keadaan mereka, karena itu mereka tidak berani melakukan hal-hal serupa.
t_id.iwq: dan Kami jadikan mereka sebagai pelajaran dan contoh bagi orang-orang yang kemudian.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فجعلناهم سلفاً» جمع سالف كخادم وخدم أي سابقين غيره «ومثلا للآخرين» بعدهم يتمثلون بحالهم فلا يقدمون على مثل أفعالهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ سَلَفٗا وَمَثَلٗا لِّلۡأٓخِرِينَ (سَلَفًا: قُدْوَةً لِمَنْ يَعْمَلُ مِثْلَ عَمَلِهِمْ؛ فَيَسْتَحِقُّ العُقُوبَةَ, وَمَثَلًا: عِظَةً، وَعِبْرَةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami jadikan mereka sebagai pelajaran) lafal Salafan merupakan bentuk jamak dari lafal salifun, wazannya sama dengan lafal Khadimun atau pelayan, yang jamaknya adalah Khadamun; yakni orang-orang terdahulu yang dijadikan sebagai pelajaran (dan contoh bagi orang-orang yang kemudian) sesudah mereka, di mana orang-orang yang sesudah mereka itu dapat mengambil contoh dari keadaan mereka, karena itu mereka tidak berani melakukan hal-hal serupa

ar: ۞ وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ٱبۡنُ مَرۡیَمَ مَثَلًا إِذَا قَوۡمُكَ مِنۡهُ یَصِدُّونَ
ar_simple: ۞ وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ مَثَلًا إِذَا قَوْمُكَ مِنْهُ يَصِدُّونَ
transliteration: walammā ḍuriba ub'nu maryama mathalan idhā qawmuka min'hu yaṣiddūna

t.sahih-int: And when the son of Mary was presented as an example, immediately your people laughed aloud
t.asad: NOW WHENEVER [the nature of] the son of Mary is set forth as an example, [O Muhammad,] lo! thy people raise an outcry on this score
t.yusuf-ali: When (Jesus) the son of Mary is held up as an example, behold, thy people raise a clamour thereat (in ridicule)
t.pickthall: And when the son of Mary is quoted as an example, behold! the folk laugh out
t.arberry: And when the son of Mary is cited as an example, behold, thy people turn away from it
t.hilali-khan: And when the son of Maryam (Mary) is quoted as an example [i.e. 'Iesa (Jesus) is worshipped like their idols), behold! Your people cry aloud (laugh out at the example)
t.itani: And when the son of Mary was cited as an example, your people opposed.
t.mubarakpuri: And when the son of Maryam is quoted as an example, behold, your people cry aloud thereat.
t.qarib: And when the son of Mary was given as an example, immediately your people [Prophet] loudly objected
t.wahiduddin: When [Jesus] the son of Mary is held up as an example, your people raise an outcry on this
t.shakir: And when a description of the son of Marium is given, lo! your people raise a clamor thereat
t.haleem: When the son of Mary is cited as an example, your people [Prophet] laugh and jeer
t.maariful: When the example of the son of Maryam was cited, your people started at once shouting at it (in joy)
t.khattab: When the son of Mary was cited as an example ˹in argument˺, your people ˹O Prophet˺ broke into ˹joyful˺ applause
t.ghali: And as soon as the son of Maryam (levity (Son of Mary, ) is struck as a similitude, only then have your people clamored aloud thereat
t.irving: When Mary´s son is quoted as an example, why, your people resist him
t.iwq: And when the son of Mary was presented as an example, immediately your people laughed aloud

t_id.kemenag: Dan tatkala putra Maryam (Isa) dijadikan perumpamnaan tiba-tiba kaummu (Quraisy) bersorak karenanya.
t_id.sabiq: Tatkala Allah menjadikan 'Isâ putra Maryam sebagai permisalan yang diciptakan seperti Adam--yang diciptakan dari tanah, kemudian dikatakan kepadanya, "Jadilah," lalu ia pun tercipta, dengan begitu berarti ia hanyalah seorang makhluk yang memperoleh karunia kenabian, bukan Tuhan yang harus disembah--tiba- tiba kaummu menentangnya dan tidak sadar dengan permisalan itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tatkala dijadikan) dibuat (putra Maryam sebagai perumpamaan) yaitu ketika Allah swt. menurunkan firman-Nya, "Sesungguhnya kalian dan apa yang kalian sembah selain Allah adalah makanan neraka Jahanam.." (Q.S. Al Anbiya, 98). Seketika itu juga orang-orang musyrik mengatakan, "Kami rela bila ternyata tuhan-tuhan sesembahan kami bersama dengan Isa, karena ia pun menjadi sesembahan selain Allah pula (tiba-tiba kaummu) yakni mereka yang musyrik (terhadap perumpamaan itu) terhadap misal tersebut (menertawakannya) karena gembira mendengar perumpamaan itu.
t_id.iwq: Dan tatkala putra Maryam (Isa) dijadikan perumpamnaan tiba-tiba kaummu (Quraisy) bersorak karenanya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (ولما ضرب) جعل (ابن مريم مثلا) حين نزل قوله تعالى "" إنكم وما تعبدون من دون الله حصب جهنم "" فقال المشركون: رضينا أن تكون آلهتنا مع عيسى لأنه عبد من دون الله (إذا قومك) أي المشركون (منه) من المثل (يصدون) يضحكون فرحاً بما سمعوا
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ٱبۡنُ مَرۡيَمَ مَثَلًا إِذَا قَوۡمُكَ مِنۡهُ يَصِدُّونَ (يَصِدُّونَ: يَضَجُّونَ، وَيَصِيحُونَ، فَرَحًا، وَجَذَلًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tatkala dijadikan) dibuat (putra Maryam sebagai perumpamaan) yaitu ketika Allah swt. menurunkan firman-Nya, "Sesungguhnya kalian dan apa yang kalian sembah selain Allah adalah makanan neraka Jahanam.." (Q.S. Al Anbiya, 98). Seketika itu juga orang-orang musyrik mengatakan, "Kami rela bila ternyata tuhan-tuhan sesembahan kami bersama dengan Isa, karena ia pun menjadi sesembahan selain Allah pula (tiba-tiba kaummu) yakni mereka yang musyrik (terhadap perumpamaan itu) terhadap misal tersebut (menertawakannya) karena gembira mendengar perumpamaan itu

ar: وَقَالُوۤا۟ ءَأَ ٰلِهَتُنَا خَیۡرٌ أَمۡ هُوَۚ مَا ضَرَبُوهُ لَكَ إِلَّا جَدَلَۢاۚ بَلۡ هُمۡ قَوۡمٌ خَصِمُونَ
ar_simple: وَقَالُوا أَآلِهَتُنَا خَيْرٌ أَمْ هُوَ ۚ مَا ضَرَبُوهُ لَكَ إِلَّا جَدَلًا ۚ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ خَصِمُونَ
transliteration: waqālū aālihatunā khayrun am huwa mā ḍarabūhu laka illā jadalan bal hum qawmun khaṣimūna

t.sahih-int: And they said, "Are your gods better, or is he?" They did not present the comparison except for [mere] argument. But, [in fact], they are a people prone to dispute
t.asad: and say, “Which is better - our deities or he?” [But] it is only in the spirit of dispute that they put this comparison before thee: yea, they are contentious folk
t.yusuf-ali: And they say, "Are our gods best, or he?" This they set forth to thee, only by way of disputation: yea, they are a contentious people
t.pickthall: And say: Are our gods better, or is he? They raise not the objection save for argument. Nay! but they are a contentious folk
t.arberry: and say, 'What, are our gods better, or he?' They cite not him to thee, save to dispute;. nay, but they are a people contentious
t.hilali-khan: And say: "Are our aliha (gods) better or is he ['Iesa (Jesus)]?" They quoted not the above example except for argument. Nay! But they are a quarrelsome people. [(See VV. 21:97-101) - The Quran
t.itani: They said, “Are our gods better, or he?” They cited him only for argument. In fact, they are a quarrelsome people.
t.mubarakpuri: And say: "Are our gods better or is he" They quoted not the above example except for argument. Nay! But they are a quarrelsome people.
t.qarib: and they said, "who is better, our gods or him?" They cite him only for arguments sake. In fact, they are a confrontational people
t.wahiduddin: saying, "Are our gods better or him?" -- they cite him only to challenge you: they are a contentious people
t.shakir: And they say: Are our gods better, or is he? They do not set it forth to you save by way of disputation; nay, they are a contentious people
t.haleem: saying, ‘Are our gods better or him?’- they cite him only to challenge you: they are a contentious people
t.maariful: and said, “Are Our gods better or is he?” They did not cite it (the example) but for the sake of disputation. Rather, they are a quarrelsome people
t.khattab: They exclaimed, “Which is better: our gods or Jesus?” They cite him only to argue. In fact, they are a people prone to dispute
t.ghali: And they have said, "Are our gods more charitable, or rather he? In no way have they struck (the similitude) to you except in dispute; no indeed, (but) they are a people, (who are) constant adversaries
t.irving: They say: "Are our gods better, or is he?" They quote him to you only for the sake of argument; indeed they are such quarrelsome folk
t.iwq: And they said, "Are your gods better, or is he?" They did not present the comparison except for mere argument. But, in fact, they are a people prone to dispute

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka berkata: "Manakah yang lebih baik tuhan-tuhan kami atau dia (Isa)?" Mereka tidak memberikan perumpamaan itu kepadamu melainkan dengan maksud membantah saja, sebenarnya mereka adalah kaum yang suka bertengkar.
t_id.sabiq: Orang-orang kafir berkata, "Manakah yang lebih baik, tuhan-tuhan kami atau Tuhan 'Isâ? Kalau 'Isâ masuk neraka, biarlah kami dan tuhan-tuhan kami ikut bersamanya." Mereka tidak memberikan perumpamaan itu kepadamu selain untuk bertengkar dan mencari kemenangan dalam berdebat, bukan untuk mencari kebenaran. Bahkan mereka adalah kaum yang suka dan mudah bertengkar.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata, 'Manakah yang lebih baik tuhan-tuhan kami atau dia?') yakni nabi Isa, maka karenanya kami rela tuhan-tuhan kami bersama dia (Mereka tidak memberikan perumpamaan itu) atau misal tersebut (kepadamu melainkan dengan maksud membantah saja) atau menyanggah kamu dengan cara yang batil, karena mereka telah mengetahui, bahwa berhala-berhala yang tidak berakal itu tidak akan dapat menyamai Nabi Isa a.s. (sebenarnya mereka adalah kaum yang suka bertengkar") sangat gemar bertengkar.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka berkata: "Manakah yang lebih baik tuhan-tuhan kami atau dia (Isa)?" Mereka tidak memberikan perumpamaan itu kepadamu melainkan dengan maksud membantah saja, sebenarnya mereka adalah kaum yang suka bertengkar.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقالوا أألهتنا خير أم هو» أي عيسى فنرضى أن تكون آلهتنا معه «ما ضربوه» أي المثل «لك إلا جدلا» خصومة بالباطل لعلمهم أن ما لغير العاقل فلا يتناول عيسى عليه السلام «بل هم قوم خصمون» شديدو الخصومة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالُوٓاْ ءَأَٰلِهَتُنَا خَيۡرٌ أَمۡ هُوَۚ مَا ضَرَبُوهُ لَكَ إِلَّا جَدَلَۢاۚ بَلۡ هُمۡ قَوۡمٌ خَصِمُونَ (خَصِمُونَ: شَدِيدُو الخُصُومَةِ بِالبَاطِلِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata, 'Manakah yang lebih baik tuhan-tuhan kami atau dia?') yakni nabi Isa, maka karenanya kami rela tuhan-tuhan kami bersama dia (Mereka tidak memberikan perumpamaan itu) atau misal tersebut (kepadamu melainkan dengan maksud membantah saja) atau menyanggah kamu dengan cara yang batil, karena mereka telah mengetahui, bahwa berhala-berhala yang tidak berakal itu tidak akan dapat menyamai Nabi Isa a.s. (sebenarnya mereka adalah kaum yang suka bertengkar") sangat gemar bertengkar

ar: إِنۡ هُوَ إِلَّا عَبۡدٌ أَنۡعَمۡنَا عَلَیۡهِ وَجَعَلۡنَـٰهُ مَثَلࣰا لِّبَنِیۤ إِسۡرَ ٰۤءِیلَ
ar_simple: إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا عَبْدٌ أَنْعَمْنَا عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلْنَاهُ مَثَلًا لِبَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ
transliteration: in huwa illā ʿabdun anʿamnā ʿalayhi wajaʿalnāhu mathalan libanī is'rāīla

t.sahih-int: Jesus was not but a servant upon whom We bestowed favor, and We made him an example for the Children of Israel
t.asad: [As for Jesus,] he was nothing but [a human being -] a servant [of Ours] whom We had graced [with prophethood], and whom We made an example for the children of Israel
t.yusuf-ali: He was no more than a servant: We granted Our favour to him, and We made him an example to the Children of Israel
t.pickthall: He is nothing but a slave on whom We bestowed favour, and We made him a pattern for the Children of Israel
t.arberry: He is only a servant We blessed, and We made him to be an example to the Children of Israel
t.hilali-khan: He ['Iesa (Jesus)] was not more than a slave. We granted Our Favour to him, and We made him an example to the Children of Israel (i.e. his creation without a father)
t.itani: He was just a servant whom We blessed, and We made him an example for the Children of Israel.
t.mubarakpuri: He was not more than a servant. We granted Our favor to him, and We made him an example for the Children of Israel.
t.qarib: [Jesus] was a servant whom We blessed, and We made him an example for the Children of Israel
t.wahiduddin: but he was only a servant We favoured and made an example for the Children of Israel
t.shakir: He was naught but a servant on whom We bestowed favor, and We made him an example for the children of Israel
t.haleem: but he is only a servant We favoured and made an example for the Children of Israel
t.maariful: He (‘Īsā) is no more than a servant (of Allah) whom We favoured and made an example for the children of Isrā’īl
t.khattab: He was only a servant We showed favour to, and made as an example for the Children of Israel
t.ghali: Decidedly he is nothing except a bondman We favored, and We made him to be a similitude to the Seeds (Or: sons) of Israel
t.irving: He was only a servant whom We favored and offered as an example to the Children of Israel
t.iwq: Jesus was not but a servant upon whom We bestowed favor, and We made him an example for the Children of Israel

t_id.kemenag: Isa tidak lain hanyalah seorang hamba yang Kami berikan kepadanya nikmat (kenabian) dan Kami jadikan dia sebagai tanda bukti (kekuasaan Allah) untuk Bani lsrail
t_id.sabiq: 'Isâ hanyalah seorang hamba yang Kami beri nikmat kenabian. Ia Kami jadikan sebagai pelajaran yang luar biasa, seperti perumpamaan--karena diciptakan tanpa bapak--bagi Banû Isrâ'îl yang membuktikan kesempurnaan kekuasaan Kami.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Bukankah) tidak lain (dia) yakni Nabi Isa itu (hanyalah seorang hamba yang Kami berikan kepadanya nikmat) kenabian (dan Kami jadikan dia) yaitu kelahirannya dengan tanpa ayah (sebagai perumpamaan untuk Bani Israel) maksudnya, sebagai bukti yang menunjukkan akan kekuasaan Allah swt. yang mampu menciptakan apa yang dikehendaki-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Isa tidak lain hanyalah seorang hamba yang Kami berikan kepadanya nikmat (kenabian) dan Kami jadikan dia sebagai tanda bukti (kekuasaan Allah) untuk Bani lsrail

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن» ما «هو» عيسى «إلا عبد أنعمنا عليه» بالنبوة «وجعلناه» بوجوده من غير أب «مثلا لبني إسرائيل» أي كالمثل لغرابته يستدل به على قدرة الله تعالى على ما يشاء
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنۡ هُوَ إِلَّا عَبۡدٌ أَنۡعَمۡنَا عَلَيۡهِ وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُ مَثَلٗا لِّبَنِيٓ إِسۡرَـٰٓءِيلَ (مَثَلًا: عِبْرَةً، وَآيَةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Bukankah) tidak lain (dia) yakni Nabi Isa itu (hanyalah seorang hamba yang Kami berikan kepadanya nikmat) kenabian (dan Kami jadikan dia) yaitu kelahirannya dengan tanpa ayah (sebagai perumpamaan untuk Bani Israel) maksudnya, sebagai bukti yang menunjukkan akan kekuasaan Allah swt. yang mampu menciptakan apa yang dikehendaki-Nya

ar: وَلَوۡ نَشَاۤءُ لَجَعَلۡنَا مِنكُم مَّلَـٰۤئِكَةࣰ فِی ٱلۡأَرۡضِ یَخۡلُفُونَ
ar_simple: وَلَوْ نَشَاءُ لَجَعَلْنَا مِنْكُمْ مَلَائِكَةً فِي الْأَرْضِ يَخْلُفُونَ
transliteration: walaw nashāu lajaʿalnā minkum malāikatan fī l-arḍi yakhlufūna

t.sahih-int: And if We willed, We could have made [instead] of you angels succeeding [one another] on the earth
t.asad: And had We so willed, [O you who worship angels,] We could indeed have made you into angels succeeding one another on earth
t.yusuf-ali: And if it were Our Will, We could make angels from amongst you, succeeding each other on the earth
t.pickthall: And had We willed We could have set among you angels to be viceroys in the earth
t.arberry: Had We willed, We would have appointed angels among you to be successors in the earth
t.hilali-khan: And if it were Our Will, We would have [destroyed you (mankind) all, and] made angels to replace you on the earth. [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol: 25, Page]
t.itani: Had We willed, We would have made of you angels to be successors on earth.
t.mubarakpuri: And if it were Our will, We would have made angels to replace you on the earth.
t.qarib: Had We so willed, We could have made you into angels succeeding one another on earth
t.wahiduddin: if We had so wished, We could have appointed angels in exchange for you to succeed you on the earth
t.shakir: And if We please, We could make among you angels to be successors in the land
t.haleem: if it had been Our will, We could have made you angels, succeeding one another on earth
t.maariful: And if We will, We may create angels from you who succeed you on the earth
t.khattab: Had We willed, We could have easily replaced you ˹all˺ with angels, succeeding one another on earth
t.ghali: And if We had (so) decided, We would indeed have made from among you Angels to be successors in the earth
t.irving: If We had so wished, We might have made angels out of some of you who would replace you on earth
t.iwq: And if We willed, We could have made instead of you angels succeeding one another on the earth

t_id.kemenag: Dan kalau Kami kehendaki benar-benar Kami jadikan sebagai gantimu di muka bumi malaikat-malaikat yang turun temurun.
t_id.sabiq: Jika Kami berkehendak, tentu Kami akan mengubah sebagian kalian menjadi malaikat yang akan menggantikan kalian seperti anak menggantikan orangtuanya, supaya kalian tahu bahwa malaikat itu tunduk kepada kekuasan Allah. Lalu, dari mana malaikat mempunyai hak untuk menjadi tuhan?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan kalau Kami kehendaki benar-benar Kami jadikan sebagai ganti kalian) untuk mengganti kalian (di muka bumi malaikat-malaikat yang turun temurun) misalnya, kalian Kami binasakan terlebih dahulu, lalu Kami jadikan malaikat sebagai ganti kalian.
t_id.iwq: Dan kalau Kami kehendaki benar-benar Kami jadikan sebagai gantimu di muka bumi malaikat-malaikat yang turun temurun.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولو نشاء لجعلنا منكم» بدلكم «ملائكة في الأرض يخلفون» بأن نهلككم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَوۡ نَشَآءُ لَجَعَلۡنَا مِنكُم مَّلَـٰٓئِكَةٗ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ يَخۡلُفُونَ (لَجَعَلْنَا مِنكُم: لَجَعَلْنَا بَدَلَكُمْ, يَخْلُفُونَ: يَخْلُفُ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا، بَدَلًا مِنْ بَنِي آدَمَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan kalau Kami kehendaki benar-benar Kami jadikan sebagai ganti kalian) untuk mengganti kalian (di muka bumi malaikat-malaikat yang turun temurun) misalnya, kalian Kami binasakan terlebih dahulu, lalu Kami jadikan malaikat sebagai ganti kalian

ar: وَإِنَّهُۥ لَعِلۡمࣱ لِّلسَّاعَةِ فَلَا تَمۡتَرُنَّ بِهَا وَٱتَّبِعُونِۚ هَـٰذَا صِرَ ٰطࣱ مُّسۡتَقِیمࣱ
ar_simple: وَإِنَّهُ لَعِلْمٌ لِلسَّاعَةِ فَلَا تَمْتَرُنَّ بِهَا وَاتَّبِعُونِ ۚ هَٰذَا صِرَاطٌ مُسْتَقِيمٌ
transliteration: wa-innahu laʿil'mun lilssāʿati falā tamtarunna bihā wa-ittabiʿūni hādhā ṣirāṭun mus'taqīmun

t.sahih-int: And indeed, Jesus will be [a sign for] knowledge of the Hour, so be not in doubt of it, and follow Me. This is a straight path
t.asad: AND, BEHOLD, this [divine writ] is indeed a means to know [that] the Last Hour [is bound to come]; hence, have no doubt whatever about it, but follow Me: this [alone] is a straight way
t.yusuf-ali: And (Jesus) shall be a Sign (for the coming of) the Hour (of Judgment): therefore have no doubt about the (Hour), but follow ye Me: this is a Straight Way
t.pickthall: And lo! verily there is knowledge of the Hour. So doubt ye not concerning it, but follow Me. This is the right path
t.arberry: It is knowledge of the Hour; doubt not concerning it, and follow me. This is a straight path
t.hilali-khan: And he ['Iesa (Jesus), son of Maryam (Mary)] shall be a known sign for (the coming of) the Hour (Day of Resurrection) [i.e. 'Iesa's (Jesus) descent on the earth]. Therefore have no doubt concerning it (i.e. the Day of Resurrection). And follow Me (Allah) (i.e. be obedient to Allah and do what He orders you to do, O mankind)! This is the Straight Path (of Islamic Monotheism, leading to Allah and to His Paradise)
t.itani: He is a portent of the Hour, so have no doubt about it, and follow Me. This is a straight way.
t.mubarakpuri: And he shall be a known sign for (the coming of) the Hour. Therefore have no doubt concerning it. And follow Me! This is the straight path.
t.qarib: He [Jesus] is a [sign for] knowledge of the Hour, so have no doubt about it, and follow Me. This is a straight path
t.wahiduddin: He is a sign of the Hour. Have no doubt about it. But follow me. This is a straight path
t.shakir: And most surely it is a knowledge of the hour, therefore have no doubt about it and follow me: this is the right path
t.haleem: This [Quran] gives knowledge of the Hour: do not doubt it. Follow Me for this is the right path
t.maariful: And he (‘Īsā) is a source of knowledge of the Hour (the Day of Judgment); so do not be in doubt about it, and follow me. This is the straight way
t.khattab: And his ˹second˺ coming is truly a sign for the Hour. So have no doubt about it, and follow me. This is the Straight Path
t.ghali: And surely it is indeed knowledge of the Hour; so definitely do not wrangle about it, and closely follow Me. This is a straight Path
t.irving: It will be because of knowledge about the Hour. Do not puzzle over it and follow Me; this is a Straight Road
t.iwq: And indeed, Jesus will be a sign for knowledge of the Hour, so be not in doubt of it, and follow Me. This is a straight path

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya Isa itu benar-benar memberikan pengetahuan tentang hari kiamat. Karena itu janganlah kamu ragu-ragu tentang kiamat itu dan ikutilah Aku. Inilah jalan yang lurus.
t_id.sabiq: Penciptaan 'Isâ tanpa bapak dan kemampuannya menyembuhkan penyakit buta dan sopak benar-benar merupakan bukti bahwa hari kiamat akan datang. Oleh karena itu, janganlah sekali-kali kalian meragukannya. Ikutilah petunjuk dan rasul-Ku. Apa yang Aku serukan kepada kalian ini adalah jalan lurus yang mengantarkan kalian kepada keselamatan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya dia) Nabi Isa itu (benar-benar merupakan pengetahuan tentang hari kiamat) artinya, dengan diturunkannya dia maka diketahuilah dekatnya hari kiamat. (Karena itu janganlah kalian ragu-ragu tentang kiamat itu) atau janganlah kalian meragukannya. Lafal Tamtarunna asalnya Tamtarunanna, kemudian dibuang daripadanya Nun alamat rafa' karena dijazmkan, dan dibuang pula daripadanya Wawu Dhamir jamak tetapi bukan karena Illat bertemunya dua huruf yang disukunkan, sehingga jadilah Tamtarunna. (Dan) katakanlah kepada mereka, ("Ikutilah aku) yakni ajaran tauhid ini. (Inilah) apa yang kuperintahkan kalian menjalankannya (jalan) atau tuntunan (yang lurus.)
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya Isa itu benar-benar memberikan pengetahuan tentang hari kiamat. Karena itu janganlah kamu ragu-ragu tentang kiamat itu dan ikutilah Aku. Inilah jalan yang lurus.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإنه» أي عيسى «لعلم للساعة» تعلم بنزوله «فلا تمترن بها» أي تشكن فيها، حذف منه نون الرفع للجزم، وواو الضمير لالتقاء الساكنين «و» قل لهم «اتبعون» على التوحيد «هذا» الذي آمركم به «صراط» طريق «مستقيم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِنَّهُۥ لَعِلۡمٞ لِّلسَّاعَةِ فَلَا تَمۡتَرُنَّ بِهَا وَٱتَّبِعُونِۚ هَٰذَا صِرَٰطٞ مُّسۡتَقِيمٞ (لَعِلْمٌ لِّلسَّاعَةِ: إِنَّ نُزُولَ عِيسَى - عليه السلام - لَدَلِيلٌ عَلَى قُرْبِ وُقُوعِ السَّاعَةِ, فَلَا تَمْتَرُنَّ: لَا تَشُكُّوا, صِرَاطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ: طَرِيقٌ قَوِيمٌ إِلَى الجَنَّةِ لَا عِوَجَ فِيهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya dia) Nabi Isa itu (benar-benar merupakan pengetahuan tentang hari kiamat) artinya, dengan diturunkannya dia maka diketahuilah dekatnya hari kiamat. (Karena itu janganlah kalian ragu-ragu tentang kiamat itu) atau janganlah kalian meragukannya. Lafal Tamtarunna asalnya Tamtarunanna, kemudian dibuang daripadanya Nun alamat rafa' karena dijazmkan, dan dibuang pula daripadanya Wawu Dhamir jamak tetapi bukan karena Illat bertemunya dua huruf yang disukunkan, sehingga jadilah Tamtarunna. (Dan) katakanlah kepada mereka, ("Ikutilah aku) yakni ajaran tauhid ini. (Inilah) apa yang kuperintahkan kalian menjalankannya (jalan) atau tuntunan (yang lurus)

ar: وَلَا یَصُدَّنَّكُمُ ٱلشَّیۡطَـٰنُۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمۡ عَدُوࣱّ مُّبِینࣱ
ar_simple: وَلَا يَصُدَّنَّكُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ ۖ إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُبِينٌ
transliteration: walā yaṣuddannakumu l-shayṭānu innahu lakum ʿaduwwun mubīnun

t.sahih-int: And never let Satan avert you. Indeed, he is to you a clear enemy
t.asad: And let not Satan bars you [from it] - for, verily, he is your open foe
t.yusuf-ali: Let not the Evil One hinder you: for he is to you an enemy avowed
t.pickthall: And let not Satan turn you aside. Lo! he is an open enemy for you
t.arberry: Let not Satan bar you; he is for you a manifest foe
t.hilali-khan: And let not Shaitan (Satan) hinder you (from the right religion, i.e. Islamic Monotheism), Verily, he (Satan) to you is a plain enemy
t.itani: And let not Satan divert you. He is an open enemy to you.
t.mubarakpuri: And let not Shaytan hinder you. Verily, he (Shaytan) to you is a plain enemy.
t.qarib: Never let Satan divert you. He is a clear enemy to you all
t.wahiduddin: do not let Satan bar your way. He is truly your sworn enemy
t.shakir: And let not the Shaitan prevent you; surely he is your j open enemy
t.haleem: do not let Satan hinder you, for he is your sworn enemy
t.maariful: And let not the ShaiTān (Satan) prevent you (from following this way). He is surely an open enemy for you
t.khattab: And do not let Satan hinder you, ˹for˺ he is certainly your sworn enemy
t.ghali: And definitely do not let Shaytan (The) ever-vicious (one), i.e., the Devil) bar you; (i.e., the believers) surely he is for you an evident enemy
t.irving: Do not let Satan hamper you; he is an obvious enemy of yours
t.iwq: And never let Satan avert you. Indeed, he is to you a clear enemy

t_id.kemenag: Dan janganlah kamu sekali-kali dipalingkan oleh syaitan; sesungguhnya syaitan itu musuh yang nyata bagimu.
t_id.sabiq: Janganlah kalian dipalingkan oleh setan untuk tidak mengikuti jalan-Ku yang lurus. Bagi kalian, setan benar-benar musuh yang nyata.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan janganlah kalian sekali-kali dipalingkan) dapat dipalingkan dari agama Allah (oleh setan; sesungguhnya setan itu musuh yang nyata bagi kalian") nyata permusuhannya.
t_id.iwq: Dan janganlah kamu sekali-kali dipalingkan oleh syaitan; sesungguhnya syaitan itu musuh yang nyata bagimu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولا يصدنكم» يصرفنكم عن دين الله «الشيطان إنه لكم عدو مبين» بيِّن العداوة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَا يَصُدَّنَّكُمُ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنُۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمۡ عَدُوّٞ مُّبِينٞ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan janganlah kalian sekali-kali dipalingkan) dapat dipalingkan dari agama Allah (oleh setan; sesungguhnya setan itu musuh yang nyata bagi kalian") nyata permusuhannya

ar: وَلَمَّا جَاۤءَ عِیسَىٰ بِٱلۡبَیِّنَـٰتِ قَالَ قَدۡ جِئۡتُكُم بِٱلۡحِكۡمَةِ وَلِأُبَیِّنَ لَكُم بَعۡضَ ٱلَّذِی تَخۡتَلِفُونَ فِیهِۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِیعُونِ
ar_simple: وَلَمَّا جَاءَ عِيسَىٰ بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ قَالَ قَدْ جِئْتُكُمْ بِالْحِكْمَةِ وَلِأُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ بَعْضَ الَّذِي تَخْتَلِفُونَ فِيهِ ۖ فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
transliteration: walammā jāa ʿīsā bil-bayināti qāla qad ji'tukum bil-ḥik'mati wali-ubayyina lakum baʿḍa alladhī takhtalifūna fīhi fa-ittaqū l-laha wa-aṭīʿūni

t.sahih-int: And when Jesus brought clear proofs, he said, "I have come to you with wisdom and to make clear to you some of that over which you differ, so fear Allah and obey me
t.asad: NOW WHEN Jesus came [to his people] with all evidence of the truth, he said: “I have now come unto you with wisdom, and to make clear Unto you some of that on which you are at variance: hence, be conscious of God, and pay heed unto me
t.yusuf-ali: When Jesus came with Clear Signs, he said: "Now have I come to you with Wisdom, and in order to make clear to you some of the (points) on which ye dispute: therefore fear Allah and obey me
t.pickthall: When Jesus came with clear proofs (of Allah's Sovereignty), he said: I have come unto you with wisdom, and to make plain some of that concerning which ye differ. So keep your duty to Allah, and obey me
t.arberry: And when Jesus came with the clear signs he said, 'I have come to you with wisdom, and that I may make clear to you some of that whereon you are at variance; so fear you God and obey you me
t.hilali-khan: And when 'Iesa (Jesus) came with (Our) clear Proofs, he said: "I have come to you with Al-Hikmah (Prophethood), and in order to make clear to you some of the (points) in which you differ, therefore fear Allah and obey me
t.itani: When Jesus came with the clarifications, he said, “I have come to you with wisdom, and to clarify for you some of what you differ about. So fear God, and obey me.
t.mubarakpuri: And when `Isa came with clear proofs, he said: "I have come to you with Al-Hikmah, and in order to make clear to you some of that in which you differ. Therefore have Taqwa of Allah and obey me."
t.qarib: When Jesus came with clear evidence, he said, "I have brought you Wisdom and to clarify for you some of what you differ about. Be always mindful of God and obey me
t.wahiduddin: When Jesus came with clear signs, he said, "Now I have come to you with wisdom, in order to make clear to you some of the things about which you dispute: therefore fear God and obey me
t.shakir: And when Isa came with clear arguments he said: I have come to you indeed with wisdom, and that I may make clear to you part of what you differ in; so be careful of (your duty to) Allah and obey me
t.haleem: When Jesus came with clear signs he said, ‘I have brought you wisdom; I have come to clear up some of your differences for you. Be mindful of God and obey me
t.maariful: When ‘Īsā came with clear proofs, he said, “I have come to you with wisdom, and to explain to you some of those matters in which you differ. So, fear Allah, and obey me
t.khattab: When Jesus came with clear proofs, he declared, “I have come to you with wisdom, and to clarify to you some of what you differ about. So fear Allah, and obey me
t.ghali: And as soon as Isa (Jesus) came with the supreme evidence (s), he said, "I have readily come to you with (the) Wisdom, and that I may make evident to you some of that concerning which you differ; so be pious to Allah, and obey me
t.irving: When Jesus came with explanations, he said: "I have brought you wisdom, so that I may explain something to you about what you have been differing over. So heed God and obey me
t.iwq: And when Jesus brought clear proofs, he said, "I have come to you with wisdom and to make clear to you some of that over which you differ, so fear Allah and obey me

t_id.kemenag: Dan tatkala Isa datang membawa keterangan dia berkata: "Sesungguhnya aku datang kepadamu dengan membawa hikmat dan untuk menjelaskan kepadamu sebagian dari apa yang kamu berselisih tentangnya, maka bertakwalah kepada Allah dan taatlah (kepada)ku".
