t_id.sabiq: Ketika 'Isâ diutus kepada Banû Isrâ'îl dengan membawa mukjizat dan ayat-ayat yang jelas, ia berkata, "Sungguh, aku datang kepada kalian dengan membawa syariat yang penuh hikmah, yang mengajak kalian kepada pengesaan Tuhan. Aku datang untuk menjelaskan sebagian perkara agama yang kalian perselisihkan, sehingga kalian semua berada kebenaran. Dari itu, takutlah kepada siksa Allah dan patuhilah apa yang aku serukan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tatkala Isa datang dengan membawa keterangan-keterangan) mukjizat-mukjizat dan syariat-syariat (dia berkata, "Sesungguhnya aku datang kepada kalian dengan membawa hikmah) kenabian dan syariat Injil (dan untuk menjelaskan kepada kalian sebagian dari apa yang kalian berselisih tentangnya) yakni tentang hukum-hukum Taurat, yaitu menyangkut masalah agama dan masalah-masalah lainnya, Nabi Isa menjelaskan kepada mereka perkara agama yang sebenarnya (maka bertakwalah kepada Allah dan taatlah kepadaku.")
t_id.iwq: Dan tatkala Isa datang membawa keterangan dia berkata: "Sesungguhnya aku datang kepadamu dengan membawa hikmat dan untuk menjelaskan kepadamu sebagian dari apa yang kamu berselisih tentangnya, maka bertakwalah kepada Allah dan taatlah (kepada)ku".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولما جاء عيسى بالبينات» بالمعجزات والشرائع «قال قد جئتكم بالحكمة» بالنبوة وشرائع الإنجيل «ولأبيِّن لكم بعض الذي تختلفون فيه» من أحكام التوراة من أمر الدين وغيره فبيَّن لهم أمر الدين «فاتقوا الله وأطيعون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَمَّا جَآءَ عِيسَىٰ بِٱلۡبَيِّنَٰتِ قَالَ قَدۡ جِئۡتُكُم بِٱلۡحِكۡمَةِ وَلِأُبَيِّنَ لَكُم بَعۡضَ ٱلَّذِي تَخۡتَلِفُونَ فِيهِۖ فَٱتَّقُواْ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (بِالْحِكْمَةِ: بِالنُّبُوَّةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tatkala Isa datang dengan membawa keterangan-keterangan) mukjizat-mukjizat dan syariat-syariat (dia berkata, "Sesungguhnya aku datang kepada kalian dengan membawa hikmah) kenabian dan syariat Injil (dan untuk menjelaskan kepada kalian sebagian dari apa yang kalian berselisih tentangnya) yakni tentang hukum-hukum Taurat, yaitu menyangkut masalah agama dan masalah-masalah lainnya, Nabi Isa menjelaskan kepada mereka perkara agama yang sebenarnya (maka bertakwalah kepada Allah dan taatlah kepadaku)

ar: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ رَبِّی وَرَبُّكُمۡ فَٱعۡبُدُوهُۚ هَـٰذَا صِرَ ٰطࣱ مُّسۡتَقِیمࣱ
ar_simple: إِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمْ فَاعْبُدُوهُ ۚ هَٰذَا صِرَاطٌ مُسْتَقِيمٌ
transliteration: inna l-laha huwa rabbī warabbukum fa-uʿ'budūhu hādhā ṣirāṭun mus'taqīmun

t.sahih-int: Indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path
t.asad: “Verily, God is my Sustainer as well as your Sustainer; so worship [none but] Him: this [alone] is a straight way!”
t.yusuf-ali: For Allah, He is my Lord and your Lord: so worship ye Him: this is a Straight Way
t.pickthall: Lo! Allah, He is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him. This is a right path
t.arberry: Assuredly God is my Lord and your Lord; therefore serve Him; this is a straight path
t.hilali-khan: Verily, Allah! He is my Lord (God) and your Lord (God). So worship Him (Alone). This is the (only) Straight Path (i.e. Allah's religion of true Islamic Monotheism)
t.itani: God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him—this is a straight path.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Verily, Allah! He is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him. This is the straight path."
t.qarib: God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path
t.wahiduddin: For God, He is my Lord and your Lord: so worship Him: that is a straight path
t.shakir: Surely Allah is my Lord and your Lord, therefore serve Him; this is the right path
t.haleem: God is my Lord and your Lord. Serve Him: this is the straight path.’
t.maariful: Surely, it is Allah who is my Lord and your Lord; so worship Him. This is the straight way.”
t.khattab: Surely Allah ˹alone˺ is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him ˹alone˺. This is the Straight Path.”
t.ghali: Surely Allah, Ever He, is my Lord and your Lord; so worship Him. This is a straight Path
t.irving: God is my Lord and your Lord, so serve Him. This is a Straight Road
t.iwq: Indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Allah Dialah Tuhanku dan Tuhan kamu maka sembahlah Dia, ini adalah jalan yang lurus.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya hanya Allahlah Penciptaku dan Pencipta kalian. Maka sembahlah Dia, bukan yang lain dan peliharalah syariat-Nya. Apa yang aku serukan kepada kalian ini adalah jalan lurus yang mengantarkan kalian kepada keselamatan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Allah Dialah Rabbku dan Rabb kalian, maka sembahlah Dia, ini adalah jalan) tuntunan (yang lurus.)
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Allah Dialah Tuhanku dan Tuhan kamu maka sembahlah Dia, ini adalah jalan yang lurus.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن الله هو ربي وربكم فاعبدوه هذا صراط» طريق «مستقيم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمۡ فَٱعۡبُدُوهُۚ هَٰذَا صِرَٰطٞ مُّسۡتَقِيمٞ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Allah Dialah Rabbku dan Rabb kalian, maka sembahlah Dia, ini adalah jalan) tuntunan (yang lurus)

ar: فَٱخۡتَلَفَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابُ مِنۢ بَیۡنِهِمۡۖ فَوَیۡلࣱ لِّلَّذِینَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنۡ عَذَابِ یَوۡمٍ أَلِیمٍ
ar_simple: فَاخْتَلَفَ الْأَحْزَابُ مِنْ بَيْنِهِمْ ۖ فَوَيْلٌ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْ عَذَابِ يَوْمٍ أَلِيمٍ
transliteration: fa-ikh'talafa l-aḥzābu min baynihim fawaylun lilladhīna ẓalamū min ʿadhābi yawmin alīmin

t.sahih-int: But the denominations from among them differed [and separated], so woe to those who have wronged from the punishment of a painful Day
t.asad: But factions from among those [who came after Jesus] began to hold divergent views: woe, then, unto those who are bent on evildoing - [woe] for the suffering [that will befall them] on a grievous Day
t.yusuf-ali: But sects from among themselves fell into disagreement: then woe to the wrong-doers, from the Penalty of a Grievous Day
t.pickthall: But the factions among them differed. Then woe unto those who do wrong from the doom of a painful day
t.arberry: But the parties among them fell into variance; so woe unto those who did evil, because of the chastisement of a painful day
t.hilali-khan: But the sects from among themselves differed. So woe to those who do wrong (by ascribing things to 'Iesa (Jesus) that are not true) from the torment of a painful Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection)
t.itani: But the factions differed among themselves. So woe to the wrongdoers from the suffering of a painful Day.
t.mubarakpuri: But the sects from among themselves differed. So woe to those who do wrong from the torment of a painful Day!
t.qarib: But the factions differed among themselves. How terrible is the punishment of a painful Day to the unjust
t.wahiduddin: The various factions among them differed -- woe then to those who did wrong: they will suffer the punishment of a painful Day
t.shakir: But parties from among them differed, so woe to those who were unjust because of the chastisement of a painful day
t.haleem: Yet still the different factions among them disagreed- woe to the evildoers: they will suffer the torment of a grievous day
t.maariful: Then different groups, out of them, fell into disagreement. Woe to the wrongdoers because of the punishment of a painful day
t.khattab: Yet their ˹various˺ groups have differed among themselves ˹about him˺, so woe to the wrongdoers when they face the torment of a painful Day
t.ghali: Yet the (allied) parties differed among themselves; so woe to the ones who did injustice from the torment of a painful Day
t.irving: Factions have disagreed among themselves. It will be too bad for the ones who have done wrong as [they face] torment on a painful day
t.iwq: But the denominations from among them differed and separated, so woe to those who have wronged from the punishment of a painful Day

t_id.kemenag: Maka berselisihlah golongan-golongan (yang terdapat) di antara mereka, lalu kecelakaan yang besarlah bagi orang-orang yang zalim yakni siksaan hari yang pedih (kiamat).
t_id.sabiq: Tetapi, orang-orang Nasrani kemudian berselisih menjadi beberapa sekte mengenai pribadi 'Isâ, setelah 'Isâ tiada. Maka, celakalah orang-orang yang zalim itu karena disiksa dengan sikaan yang amat pedih pada hari kiamat, akibat keingkaran yang mereka katakan tentang 'Isâ.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka berselisihlah golongan-golongan di antara mereka) tentang perkara Nabi Isa ini, apakah dia anak Allah atau Allah, atau tuhan yang ketiga (maka kecelakaan yang besarlah) lafal Al Wail menunjukkan kalimat azab (bagi orang-orang yang lalim) bagi orang-orang kafir, karena perkataan yang mereka ucapkan mengenai Nabi Isa (yaitu siksaan hari yang pedih) atau azab yang menyakitkan.
t_id.iwq: Maka berselisihlah golongan-golongan (yang terdapat) di antara mereka, lalu kecelakaan yang besarlah bagi orang-orang yang zalim yakni siksaan hari yang pedih (kiamat).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فاختلف الأحزاب من بينهم» في عيسى أهو الله أو ابن الله أو ثالث ثلاثة «فويلٌ» كلمة عذاب «للذين ظلموا» كفروا بما قالوه في عيسى «من عذاب يوم أليم» مؤلم
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَٱخۡتَلَفَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابُ مِنۢ بَيۡنِهِمۡۖ فَوَيۡلٞ لِّلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ مِنۡ عَذَابِ يَوۡمٍ أَلِيمٍ (الْأَحْزَابُ: الفِرَقُ, فَوَيْلٌ: هَلَاكٌ، وَدَمَارٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka berselisihlah golongan-golongan di antara mereka) tentang perkara Nabi Isa ini, apakah dia anak Allah atau Allah, atau tuhan yang ketiga (maka kecelakaan yang besarlah) lafal Al Wail menunjukkan kalimat azab (bagi orang-orang yang lalim) bagi orang-orang kafir, karena perkataan yang mereka ucapkan mengenai Nabi Isa (yaitu siksaan hari yang pedih) atau azab yang menyakitkan

ar: هَلۡ یَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا ٱلسَّاعَةَ أَن تَأۡتِیَهُم بَغۡتَةࣰ وَهُمۡ لَا یَشۡعُرُونَ
ar_simple: هَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا السَّاعَةَ أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمْ بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
transliteration: hal yanẓurūna illā l-sāʿata an tatiyahum baghtatan wahum lā yashʿurūna

t.sahih-int: Are they waiting except for the Hour to come upon them suddenly while they perceive not
t.asad: ARE THEY [who are lost in sin] but waiting for the Last Hour - [waiting] that it come upon them of a sudden, without their being aware [of its approach]
t.yusuf-ali: Do they only wait for the Hour - that it should come on them all of a sudden, while they perceive not
t.pickthall: Await they aught save the Hour, that it shall come upon them suddenly, when they know not
t.arberry: Are they looking for aught but the Hour, that it shall come upon them suddenly, when they are not aware
t.hilali-khan: Do they only wait for the Hour that it shall come upon them suddenly, while they perceive not
t.itani: Are they only waiting for the Hour to come upon them suddenly, while they are unaware?
t.mubarakpuri: Do they only wait for the Hour that it shall come upon them suddenly while they perceive not
t.qarib: What are they waiting for except for the Hour to come suddenly upon them when they are not expecting it
t.wahiduddin: Are they merely waiting for the Hour, which will come upon them suddenly and take them unawares
t.shakir: Do they wait for aught but the hour, that it should come! upon them all of a sudden while they do not perceive
t.haleem: what are they waiting for but the Hour, which will come upon them suddenly and take them unawares
t.maariful: They are waiting for nothing but for the Hour to come upon them suddenly, while they would not even imagine (it)
t.khattab: Are they waiting for the Hour to take them by surprise when they least expect ˹it˺
t.ghali: Are they looking for (anything) except the Hour, that it will come to them suddenly, when they are not aware
t.irving: Are they merely waiting for the Hour to come upon them suddenly while they do not even notice it
t.iwq: Are they waiting except for the Hour to come upon them suddenly while they perceive not

t_id.kemenag: Mereka tidak menunggu kecuali kedatangan hari kiamat kepada mereka dengan tiba-tiba sedang mereka tidak menyadarinya.
t_id.sabiq: Orang-orang kafir hanya menanti datangnya hari kiamat secara tiba-tiba, setelah mereka menolak untuk beriman, sementara mereka lengah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka tidak menunggu) orang-orang kafir Mekah tidak menunggu-nunggu (kecuali kedatangan hari kiamat kepada mereka) lafal An Ta-tiyahum menjadi Badal dari lafal As Sa'ah (dengan tiba-tiba) atau sekonyong-konyong (sedangkan mereka tidak menyadarinya) tidak menyadari kedatangannya sebelum itu.
t_id.iwq: Mereka tidak menunggu kecuali kedatangan hari kiamat kepada mereka dengan tiba-tiba sedang mereka tidak menyadarinya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «هل ينظرون» أي كفار مكة، أي ما ينتظرون «إلا الساعة أن تأتيهم» بدل من الساعة «بغتةً» فجأة «وهم لا يشعرون» بوقت مجيئها قبله
tafsir.siraj-ar: هَلۡ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا ٱلسَّاعَةَ أَن تَأۡتِيَهُم بَغۡتَةٗ وَهُمۡ لَا يَشۡعُرُونَ (يَنظُرُونَ: يَنْتَظِرُونَ, بَغْتَةً: فَجْأَةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka tidak menunggu) orang-orang kafir Mekah tidak menunggu-nunggu (kecuali kedatangan hari kiamat kepada mereka) lafal An Ta-tiyahum menjadi Badal dari lafal As Sa'ah (dengan tiba-tiba) atau sekonyong-konyong (sedangkan mereka tidak menyadarinya) tidak menyadari kedatangannya sebelum itu

ar: ٱلۡأَخِلَّاۤءُ یَوۡمَئِذِۭ بَعۡضُهُمۡ لِبَعۡضٍ عَدُوٌّ إِلَّا ٱلۡمُتَّقِینَ
ar_simple: الْأَخِلَّاءُ يَوْمَئِذٍ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ إِلَّا الْمُتَّقِينَ
transliteration: al-akhilāu yawma-idhin baʿḍuhum libaʿḍin ʿaduwwun illā l-mutaqīna

t.sahih-int: Close friends, that Day, will be enemies to each other, except for the righteous
t.asad: On that Day, [erstwhile] friends will be foes unto one another - [all] save the God-conscious
t.yusuf-ali: Friends on that day will be foes, one to another,- except the Righteous
t.pickthall: Friends on that day will be foes one to another, save those who kept their duty (to Allah)
t.arberry: Friends on that day shall be foes to one another, but the god-fearing
t.hilali-khan: Friends on that Day will be foes one to another except Al-Muttaqun (pious - see V)
t.itani: On that Day, friends will be enemies of one another, except for the righteous.
t.mubarakpuri: Friends on that Day will be foes one to another except those who have Taqwa.
t.qarib: On that Day, close friends will be enemies to each other, except for those who are mindful of God
t.wahiduddin: On that Day, friends will become each other's enemies, except the righteous
t.shakir: The friends shall on that day be enemies one to another, except those who guard (against evil)
t.haleem: On that Day, friends will become each other’s enemies. Not so the righteous
t.maariful: Friends, on that day, will become enemies to one another, except the God-fearing
t.khattab: Close friends will be enemies to one another on that Day, except the righteous
t.ghali: Close fellows upon that Day will be enemies to one another, (Literally: some of them to some (others) except the pious
t.irving: On that day even bosom friends will become one another´s enemies, unless they have done their duty
t.iwq: Close friends, that Day, will be enemies to each other, except for the righteous

t_id.kemenag: Teman-teman akrab pada hari itu sebagiannya menjadi musuh bagi sebagian yang lain kecuali orang-orang yang bertakwa.
t_id.sabiq: Teman-teman yang disatukan oleh kebatilan di dunia, pada hari kiamat yang datangnya tiba-tiba itu, akan saling bermusuhan. Pada hari itu, semua hubungan cinta kasih akan terputus kecuali cinta kasih orang-orang yang ketika berada di dunia takut kepada siksa Allah dan bersatu dalam menaati perintah Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Teman-teman akrab) dalam hal maksiat sewaktu di dunia (pada hari itu) pada hari kiamat itu lafal Yaumaidzin berta'alluq kepada firman selanjutnya (sebagian dari mereka menjadi musuh bagi sebagian yang lain kecuali orang-orang yang bertakwa) terkecuali orang-orang yang saling kasih mengasihi di dalam ketaatan kepada Allah swt., mereka itulah yang sebenarnya berteman, kemudian dikatakan kepada mereka yang bertakwa itu,
t_id.iwq: Teman-teman akrab pada hari itu sebagiannya menjadi musuh bagi sebagian yang lain kecuali orang-orang yang bertakwa.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «الأخلاءُ» على المعصية في الدنيا «يومئذ» يوم القيامة متعلق بقوله «بعضهم لبعض عدو إلا المتقين» المتحابين في الله على طاعته فإنهم أصدقاء ويقال لهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱلۡأَخِلَّآءُ يَوۡمَئِذِۭ بَعۡضُهُمۡ لِبَعۡضٍ عَدُوٌّ إِلَّا ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ (الْأَخِلَّاءُ: الأَصْدِقَاءُ، وَالأَحْبَابُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Teman-teman akrab) dalam hal maksiat sewaktu di dunia (pada hari itu) pada hari kiamat itu lafal Yaumaidzin berta'alluq kepada firman selanjutnya (sebagian dari mereka menjadi musuh bagi sebagian yang lain kecuali orang-orang yang bertakwa) terkecuali orang-orang yang saling kasih mengasihi di dalam ketaatan kepada Allah swt., mereka itulah yang sebenarnya berteman, kemudian dikatakan kepada mereka yang bertakwa itu

ar: یَـٰعِبَادِ لَا خَوۡفٌ عَلَیۡكُمُ ٱلۡیَوۡمَ وَلَاۤ أَنتُمۡ تَحۡزَنُونَ
ar_simple: يَا عِبَادِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَوْمَ وَلَا أَنْتُمْ تَحْزَنُونَ
transliteration: yāʿibādi lā khawfun ʿalaykumu l-yawma walā antum taḥzanūna

t.sahih-int: [To whom Allah will say], "O My servants, no fear will there be concerning you this Day, nor will you grieve
t.asad: [And God will say:] “O you servants of Mine! No fear need you have today, and neither shall you grieve –
t.yusuf-ali: My devotees! no fear shall be on you that Day, nor shall ye grieve
t.pickthall: O My slaves! For you there is no fear this day, nor is it ye who grieve
t.arberry: O My servants, today no fear is on you, neither do you sorrow
t.hilali-khan: (It will be said to the true believers of Islamic Monotheism): My worshippers! No fear shall be on you this Day, nor shall you grieve
t.itani: O My servants, you have nothing to fear on that Day, nor will you grieve.
t.mubarakpuri: My servants! No fear shall be on you this Day, nor shall you grieve,
t.qarib: My worshipers, you have nothing to fear on that Day, nor will you grieve
t.wahiduddin: O My servants, you need not fear this Day, nor shall you grieve
t.shakir: O My servants! there is no fear for you this day, nor shall you grieve
t.haleem: ‘My servants, there is no fear for you today, nor shall you grieve’––
t.maariful: (to whom it will be said) “O my servants, there is no fear for you today, nor will you grieve
t.khattab: ˹who will be told,˺ “O My servants! There is no fear for you Today, nor will you grieve—
t.ghali: O My bondmen, today no fear is on you, nor is it you who grieve
t.irving: My servants, you have no need to fear today nor should you feel saddened
t.iwq: To whom Allah will say, "O My servants, no fear will there be concerning you this Day, nor will you grieve

t_id.kemenag: "Hai hamba-hamba-Ku, tiada kekhawatiran terhadapmu pada hari ini dan tidak pula kamu bersedih hati.
t_id.sabiq: Allah akan memanggil orang-orang yang bertakwa, sebagai penghargaan atas mereka, "Wahai hamba- hambaku, hari ini kalian tidak perlu takut lagi kepada siksaan dan juga tidak perlu bersedih, karena kalian telah terbebas dari azab. Dan Allah menjamin kalian dengan pahala."
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Hai hamba-hamba-Ku, tiada kekuatiran terhadap kalian pada hari ini dan tidak pula kalian bersedih hati.")
t_id.iwq: "Hai hamba-hamba-Ku, tiada kekhawatiran terhadapmu pada hari ini dan tidak pula kamu bersedih hati.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يا عبادِ لا خوف عليكم اليوم ولا أنتم تحزنون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَٰعِبَادِ لَا خَوۡفٌ عَلَيۡكُمُ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ وَلَآ أَنتُمۡ تَحۡزَنُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Hai hamba-hamba-Ku, tiada kekuatiran terhadap kalian pada hari ini dan tidak pula kalian bersedih hati

ar: ٱلَّذِینَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِءَایَـٰتِنَا وَكَانُوا۟ مُسۡلِمِینَ
ar_simple: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَكَانُوا مُسْلِمِينَ
transliteration: alladhīna āmanū biāyātinā wakānū mus'limīna

t.sahih-int: [You] who believed in Our verses and were Muslims
t.asad: [O you] who have attained to faith in Our messages and have surrendered your own selves unto Us
t.yusuf-ali: (Being) those who have believed in Our Signs and bowed (their wills to Ours) in Islam
t.pickthall: (Ye) who believed Our revelations and were self-surrendered
t.arberry: even those who believed in Our signs, and had surrendered themselves
t.hilali-khan: (You) who believed in Our Ayat (proofs, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) and were Muslims (i.e. who submit totally to Allah's Will, and believe in the Oneness of Allah - Islamic Monotheism)
t.itani: Those who believed in Our revelations, and were submissive.
t.mubarakpuri: (You) who believed in Our Ayat and were Muslims.
t.qarib: those who believed in Our revelations and were submissive
t.wahiduddin: those who believed in Our revelations and surrendered themselves to Us
t.shakir: Those who believed in Our communications and were submissive
t.haleem: those who believed in Our revelations and devoted themselves to Us
t.maariful: O those (servants) who believed in Our signs, and remained obedient
t.khattab: ˹those˺ who believed in Our signs and ˹fully˺ submitted ˹to Us˺
t.ghali: The ones who believed in Our signs and were Muslims
t.irving: [tell] those who believe in Our signs and are committed to [live in] peace
t.iwq: You who believed in Our verses and were Muslims

t_id.kemenag: (Yaitu) orang-orang yang beriman kepada ayat-ayat Kami dan adalah mereka dahulu orang-orang yang berserah diri.
t_id.sabiq: Orang-orang yang membenarkan ayat-ayat Allah, menaati-Nya dan selalu tunduk dan patuh-Nya,
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu orang-orang yang beriman) lafal ayat ini menjadi Na'at atau sifat bagi lafal 'Ibaadi' pada ayat di atas (kepada ayat-ayat Kami) yakni Alquran (dan adalah mereka dahulu orang-orang yang berserah diri.)
t_id.iwq: (Yaitu) orang-orang yang beriman kepada ayat-ayat Kami dan adalah mereka dahulu orang-orang yang berserah diri.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «الذين آمنوا» نعت لعبادي «بآياتنا» القرآن «وكانوا مسلمين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا وَكَانُواْ مُسۡلِمِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu orang-orang yang beriman) lafal ayat ini menjadi Na'at atau sifat bagi lafal 'Ibaadi' pada ayat di atas (kepada ayat-ayat Kami) yakni Alquran (dan adalah mereka dahulu orang-orang yang berserah diri)

ar: ٱدۡخُلُوا۟ ٱلۡجَنَّةَ أَنتُمۡ وَأَزۡوَ ٰجُكُمۡ تُحۡبَرُونَ
ar_simple: ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ أَنْتُمْ وَأَزْوَاجُكُمْ تُحْبَرُونَ
transliteration: ud'khulū l-janata antum wa-azwājukum tuḥ'barūna

t.sahih-int: Enter Paradise, you and your kinds, delighted
t.asad: Enter paradise, you and your spouses, with happiness blest!”
t.yusuf-ali: Enter ye the Garden, ye and your wives, in (beauty and) rejoicing
t.pickthall: Enter the Garden, ye and your wives, to be made glad
t.arberry: Enter Paradise, you and your wives, walking with joy
t.hilali-khan: Enter Paradise, you and your wives, in happiness
t.itani: Enter the Garden, you and your spouses, Joyfully.
t.mubarakpuri: Enter Paradise, you and your wives, in happiness.
t.qarib: Enter the Garden, you and your spouses, filled with Joy
t.wahiduddin: Enter the Garden rejoicing, both you and your spouses
t.shakir: Enter the garden, you and your wives; you shall be made happy
t.haleem: ‘Enter Paradise, you and your spouses: you will be filled with joy.’
t.maariful: Enter the Paradise, you and your spouses, showered with bliss.”
t.khattab: Enter Paradise, you and your spouses, rejoicing.”
t.ghali: Enter the Garden, you and your spouses, to be made jubilant
t.irving: Enter the Garden rejoicing, both you and your spouses
t.iwq: Enter Paradise, you and your kinds, delighted

t_id.kemenag: Masuklah kamu ke dalam surga, kamu dan isteri-isteri kamu digembirakan".
t_id.sabiq: pada hari kiamat akan dikatakan kepada mereka, sebagai penghormatan, "Masuklah kalian bersama pasangan-pasangan kalian ke dalam surga. Di dalamnya, kalian akan merasa sangat bahagia yang tanda- tandanya tampak pada wajah-wajah kalian."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Masuklah kalian ke dalam surga, kalian) lafal Antum berkedudukan menjadi Mubtada (dan pasangan-pasangan kalian) yakni istri-istri kalian (digembirakan) dibahagiakan dan dimuliakan, lafal Tuhbaruuna menjadi Khabar dari Mubtada.
t_id.iwq: Masuklah kamu ke dalam surga, kamu dan isteri-isteri kamu digembirakan".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ادخلوا الجنة أنتم» مبتدأ «وأزواجكم» زوجاتكم «تحبرون» تسرون وتكرمون، خبر المبتدأ
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱدۡخُلُواْ ٱلۡجَنَّةَ أَنتُمۡ وَأَزۡوَٰجُكُمۡ تُحۡبَرُونَ (تُحْبَرُونَ: تُنْعَّمُونَ، وَتُسَرُّونَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Masuklah kalian ke dalam surga, kalian) lafal Antum berkedudukan menjadi Mubtada (dan pasangan-pasangan kalian) yakni istri-istri kalian (digembirakan) dibahagiakan dan dimuliakan, lafal Tuhbaruuna menjadi Khabar dari Mubtada

ar: یُطَافُ عَلَیۡهِم بِصِحَافࣲ مِّن ذَهَبࣲ وَأَكۡوَابࣲۖ وَفِیهَا مَا تَشۡتَهِیهِ ٱلۡأَنفُسُ وَتَلَذُّ ٱلۡأَعۡیُنُۖ وَأَنتُمۡ فِیهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ
ar_simple: يُطَافُ عَلَيْهِمْ بِصِحَافٍ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ وَأَكْوَابٍ ۖ وَفِيهَا مَا تَشْتَهِيهِ الْأَنْفُسُ وَتَلَذُّ الْأَعْيُنُ ۖ وَأَنْتُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
transliteration: yuṭāfu ʿalayhim biṣiḥāfin min dhahabin wa-akwābin wafīhā mā tashtahīhi l-anfusu wataladhu l-aʿyunu wa-antum fīhā khālidūna

t.sahih-int: Circulated among them will be plates and vessels of gold. And therein is whatever the souls desire and [what] delights the eyes, and you will abide therein eternally
t.asad: [And there] they will be waited upon with trays and goblets of gold; and there will be found all that the souls might desire, and [all that] the eyes might delight in. And therein shall you abide, [O you who believe]
t.yusuf-ali: To them will be passed round, dishes and goblets of gold: there will be there all that the souls could desire, all that their eyes could delight in: and ye shall abide therein (for eye)
t.pickthall: Therein are brought round for them trays of gold and goblets, and therein is all that souls desire and eyes find sweet. And ye are immortal therein
t.arberry: There shall be passed around them platters of gold, and cups, therein being whatever the souls desire, and the eyes delight in. 'And therein, you shall dwell forever
t.hilali-khan: Trays of gold and cups will be passed round them, (there will be) therein all that the one's inner-selves could desire, all that the eyes could delight in, and you will abide therein forever
t.itani: They will be served around with trays of gold, and cups. Therein is whatever the souls desire and delights the eyes. Therein you will stay forever.
t.mubarakpuri: Trays of gold and cups will be passed round them; (there will be) therein all that their souls desire, and all that eyes could delight in and you will abide therein forever.
t.qarib: Passed among them will be plates and cups of gold. They will have whatever their soul desire and whatever delights their eyes. There you will remain forever
t.wahiduddin: Dishes and goblets of gold will be passed around them with all that their souls desire and their eyes delight in. "There you will remain forever
t.shakir: There shall be sent round to them golden bowls and drinking-cups and therein shall be what their souls yearn after and (wherein) the eyes shall delight, and you shall abide therein
t.haleem: Dishes and goblets of gold will be passed around them with all that their souls desire and their eyes delight in. ‘There you will remain
t.maariful: Circulated among them will be the bowls of gold, and glasses. And there will be whatever souls desire and that which eyes enjoy. “And you will be living in it for ever
t.khattab: Golden trays and cups will be passed around to them. There will be whatever the souls desire and the eyes delight in. And you will be there forever
t.ghali: (There) will be passed round them platters of gold and goblets, and therein are whatever the selves crave for, and the eyes (find) delicious. "And you are therein eternally (abiding)
t.irving: Gold platters and cups will be passed around among them containing anything (their) souls may hanker for and (their) eyes delight in. You will live there forever
t.iwq: Circulated among them will be plates and vessels of gold. And therein is whatever the souls desire and what delights the eyes, and you will abide therein eternally

t_id.kemenag: Diedarkan kepada mereka piring-piring dari emas, dan piala-piala dan di dalam surga itu terdapat segala apa yang diingini oleh hati dan sedap (dipandang) mata dan kamu kekal di dalamnya".
t_id.sabiq: Setelah mereka masuk surga, kepada mereka diedarkan piring dan gelas dari emas. Di dalamnya terdapat beraneka ragam makanan dan minuman. Mereka, di dalam surga, akan memperoleh apa saja yang membangkitkan selera dan menyedapkan pandangan. Kepada mereka dikatakan, sebagai penyempurnaan kebahagiaan, "Kalian hidup selamanya dalam kenikmatan ini."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Diedarkan kepada mereka piring-piring) yang besar-besar (dari emas, gelas-gelas) tempat untuk minum yang tidak ada pengikatnya hingga si peminum dapat meminum dari sebelah mana saja; lafal Akwaabun adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Kuubun (dan di dalam surga itu terdapat segala apa yang diingini oleh hati) untuk dinikmati kelezatannya (dan sedap dipandang mata) artinya, sangat menyejukkan bila dipandang (dan kalian kekal di dalamnya.)
t_id.iwq: Diedarkan kepada mereka piring-piring dari emas, dan piala-piala dan di dalam surga itu terdapat segala apa yang diingini oleh hati dan sedap (dipandang) mata dan kamu kekal di dalamnya".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يطاف عليهم بصحاف» بقصاع «من ذهب وأكواب» جمع كوب وهو إناء لا عروة له ليشرب الشارب من حيث شاء «وفيها ما تشتهيه الأنفس» تلذذاً «وتلذ الأعين» نظراً «وأنتم فيها خالدون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: يُطَافُ عَلَيۡهِم بِصِحَافٖ مِّن ذَهَبٖ وَأَكۡوَابٖۖ وَفِيهَا مَا تَشۡتَهِيهِ ٱلۡأَنفُسُ وَتَلَذُّ ٱلۡأَعۡيُنُۖ وَأَنتُمۡ فِيهَا خَٰلِدُونَ (بِصِحَافٍ: بِأَوَانٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Diedarkan kepada mereka piring-piring) yang besar-besar (dari emas, gelas-gelas) tempat untuk minum yang tidak ada pengikatnya hingga si peminum dapat meminum dari sebelah mana saja; lafal Akwaabun adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Kuubun (dan di dalam surga itu terdapat segala apa yang diingini oleh hati) untuk dinikmati kelezatannya (dan sedap dipandang mata) artinya, sangat menyejukkan bila dipandang (dan kalian kekal di dalamnya)

ar: وَتِلۡكَ ٱلۡجَنَّةُ ٱلَّتِیۤ أُورِثۡتُمُوهَا بِمَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ
ar_simple: وَتِلْكَ الْجَنَّةُ الَّتِي أُورِثْتُمُوهَا بِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
transliteration: watil'ka l-janatu allatī ūrith'tumūhā bimā kuntum taʿmalūna

t.sahih-int: And that is Paradise which you are made to inherit for what you used to do
t.asad: for such will be the paradise which you shall have inherited by virtue of your past deeds
t.yusuf-ali: Such will be the Garden of which ye are made heirs for your (good) deeds (in life)
t.pickthall: This is the Garden which ye are made to inherit because of what ye used to do
t.arberry: This is the Paradise that you have been given for an inheritance for the things that you were doing
t.hilali-khan: This is the Paradise which you have been made to inherit because of your deeds which you used to do (in the life of the world)
t.itani: Such is the Garden you are made to inherit, because of what you used to do.
t.mubarakpuri: This is the Paradise, which you have been made to inherit because of your deeds that you used to do.
t.qarib: That is the Garden which you are made to inherit because of what you have done
t.wahiduddin: this is the Garden which you will inherit by virtue of your past deeds
t.shakir: And this is the garden which you are given as an inheritance on account of what you did
t.haleem: this is the Garden you are given as your own, because of what you used to do
t.maariful: That is the Paradise you are made to inherit because of what you used to do
t.khattab: That is the Paradise which you will be awarded for what you used to do
t.ghali: And that is the Garden which you are made to inherit for whatever you were doing
t.irving: Such is the Garden which you will inherit because of what you have been doing
t.iwq: And that is Paradise which you are made to inherit for what you used to do

t_id.kemenag: Dan itulah surga yang diwariskan kepada kamu disebabkan amal-amal yang dahulu kamu kerjakan.
t_id.sabiq: Dikatakan pula, sebagai penyempurnaan kenikmatan itu, "Itulah surga yang kalian dapati berkat amal saleh yang dahulu kalian kerjakan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan itulah surga yang diwariskan kepada kalian disebabkan amal-amal yang dahulu kalian kerjakan.)
t_id.iwq: Dan itulah surga yang diwariskan kepada kamu disebabkan amal-amal yang dahulu kamu kerjakan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وتلك الجنة التي أورثتموها بما كنتم تعملون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَتِلۡكَ ٱلۡجَنَّةُ ٱلَّتِيٓ أُورِثۡتُمُوهَا بِمَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dan itulah surga yang diwariskan kepada kalian disebabkan amal-amal yang dahulu kalian kerjakan

ar: لَكُمۡ فِیهَا فَـٰكِهَةࣱ كَثِیرَةࣱ مِّنۡهَا تَأۡكُلُونَ
ar_simple: لَكُمْ فِيهَا فَاكِهَةٌ كَثِيرَةٌ مِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ
transliteration: lakum fīhā fākihatun kathīratun min'hā takulūna

t.sahih-int: For you therein is much fruit from which you will eat
t.asad: fruits [of those deeds] shall you have in abundance, [and] thereof shall you partake
t.yusuf-ali: Ye shall have therein abundance of fruit, from which ye shall have satisfaction
t.pickthall: Therein for you is fruit in plenty whence to eat
t.arberry: Therein you have abundant fruits, whereof you may eat
t.hilali-khan: Therein for you will be fruits in plenty, of which you will eat (as you desire)
t.itani: Therein you will have abundant fruit, from which you eat.
t.mubarakpuri: Therein for you will be fruits in plenty, of which you will eat (as you desire).
t.qarib: There will be much fruit for you to eat
t.wahiduddin: and there is abundant fruit in it for you to eat
t.shakir: For you therein are many fruits of which you shall eat
t.haleem: and there is abundant fruit in it for you to eat.’
t.maariful: For you there are fruits in abundance from which you will eat.”
t.khattab: There you will have abundant fruit to eat from
t.ghali: Therein you have many fruits, whereof you eat
t.irving: You will have plenty of fruit to eat in it
t.iwq: For you therein is much fruit from which you will eat

t_id.kemenag: Di dalam surga itu ada buah-buahan yang banyak untukmu yang sebahagiannya kamu makan.
t_id.sabiq: Di surga kalian akan memperoleh segala jenis buah-buahan dengan berbagai macam ukuran yang dapat kalian nikmati."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Di dalam surga itu ada buah-buahan yang banyak untuk kalian yang sebagiannya) sebagian daripadanya (kalian makan) dan setiap apa yang telah dimakan secara langsung mendapat penggantinya yang baru.
t_id.iwq: Di dalam surga itu ada buah-buahan yang banyak untukmu yang sebahagiannya kamu makan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «لكم فيها فاكهة كثيرة منها» أي بعضها «تأكلون» وكل ما يؤكل يخلف بدله
tafsir.siraj-ar: لَكُمۡ فِيهَا فَٰكِهَةٞ كَثِيرَةٞ مِّنۡهَا تَأۡكُلُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Di dalam surga itu ada buah-buahan yang banyak untuk kalian yang sebagiannya) sebagian daripadanya (kalian makan) dan setiap apa yang telah dimakan secara langsung mendapat penggantinya yang baru

ar: إِنَّ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِینَ فِی عَذَابِ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدُونَ
ar_simple: إِنَّ الْمُجْرِمِينَ فِي عَذَابِ جَهَنَّمَ خَالِدُونَ
transliteration: inna l-muj'rimīna fī ʿadhābi jahannama khālidūna

t.sahih-int: Indeed, the criminals will be in the punishment of Hell, abiding eternally
t.asad: [But,] behold, they who are lost in sin shall abide in the suffering of hell
t.yusuf-ali: The sinners will be in the Punishment of Hell, to dwell therein (for aye)
t.pickthall: Lo! the guilty are immortal in hell's torment
t.arberry: But the evildoers dwell forever in the chastisement of Gehenna
t.hilali-khan: Verily, the Mujrimun (criminals, sinners, disbelievers, etc.) will be in the torment of Hell to abide therein forever
t.itani: As for the sinners, they will be in the torment of Hell forever.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, the criminals will be in the torment of Hell to abide therein forever.
t.qarib: Those who force others to reject Our messages will be in the punishment of Hell forever
t.wahiduddin: As for the evil-doers, they shall endure forever the torment of Hell
t.shakir: Surely the guilty shall abide in the chastisement of hell
t.haleem: But the evildoers will remain in Hell’s punishment
t.maariful: The sinners (on the other hand) will be in the punishment of Jahannam (Hell), living there forever
t.khattab: Indeed, the wicked will be in the torment of Hell forever
t.ghali: Surely the criminals are in the torment of Hell eternally (abiding)
t.irving: Yet criminals will remain in Hell´s torment
t.iwq: Indeed, the criminals will be in the punishment of Hell, abiding eternally

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang berdosa kekal di dalam azab neraka Jahannam.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang berdosa dengan berbuat kekafiran akan hidup selama-lamanya di dalam siksa neraka jahanam.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang berdosa kekal di dalam azab neraka Jahanam.)
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang berdosa kekal di dalam azab neraka neraka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن المجرمين في عذاب جهنم خالدون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ فِي عَذَابِ جَهَنَّمَ خَٰلِدُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang berdosa kekal di dalam azab neraka Jahanam

ar: لَا یُفَتَّرُ عَنۡهُمۡ وَهُمۡ فِیهِ مُبۡلِسُونَ
ar_simple: لَا يُفَتَّرُ عَنْهُمْ وَهُمْ فِيهِ مُبْلِسُونَ
transliteration: lā yufattaru ʿanhum wahum fīhi mub'lisūna

t.sahih-int: It will not be allowed to subside for them, and they, therein, are in despair
t.asad: it will not be lightened for them; and therein they will be lost in hopeless despair
t.yusuf-ali: Nowise will the (Punishment) be lightened for them, and in despair will they be there overwhelmed
t.pickthall: It is not relaxed for them, and they despair therein
t.arberry: that is not abated for them and therein they are sore confounded
t.hilali-khan: (The torment) will not be lightened for them, and they will be plunged into destruction with deep regrets, sorrows and in despair therein
t.itani: It will never be eased for them. In it, they will be devastated.
t.mubarakpuri: (The torment) will not be lightened for them, and they will be plunged into destruction with deep regrets, sorrows and in despair therein.
t.qarib: It will not be allowed to be eased for them. In it, they will be crushed by despair
t.wahiduddin: from which there is no relief: they will remain there in utter despair
t.shakir: It shall not be abated from them and they shall therein be despairing
t.haleem: from which there is no relief: they will remain in utter despair
t.maariful: It will not be lightened for them, and they will be there, devoid of all hopes
t.khattab: It will never be lightened for them, and there they will be overwhelmed with despair
t.ghali: It is not mitigated for them, and therein they will be dumbfounded
t.irving: it will not be eased for them and they will feel confounded in it
t.iwq: It will not be allowed to subside for them, and they, therein, are in despair

t_id.kemenag: Tidak diringankan azab itu dari mereka dan mereka di dalamnya berputus asa.
t_id.sabiq: Siksaan itu tidak diringankan dan dihentikan dari orang-orang yang berdosa. Mereka putus asa dari keselamatan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tidak dihenti-hentikan) maksudnya, tidak diringankan (azab itu dari mereka sedangkan mereka di dalamnya berputus asa) yakni dalam keadaan diam berputus asa.
t_id.iwq: Tidak diringankan azab itu dari mereka dan mereka di dalamnya berputus asa.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «لا يُفَتَّرُ» يخفف «عنهم وهم فيه مبلسون» ساكتون سكوت يأس
tafsir.siraj-ar: لَا يُفَتَّرُ عَنۡهُمۡ وَهُمۡ فِيهِ مُبۡلِسُونَ (لَا يُفَتَّرُ عَنْهُمْ: لَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمْ, مُبْلِسُونَ: آيِسُونَ مِنْ رَحْمَةِ اللهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tidak dihenti-hentikan) maksudnya, tidak diringankan (azab itu dari mereka sedangkan mereka di dalamnya berputus asa) yakni dalam keadaan diam berputus asa

ar: وَمَا ظَلَمۡنَـٰهُمۡ وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِینَ
ar_simple: وَمَا ظَلَمْنَاهُمْ وَلَٰكِنْ كَانُوا هُمُ الظَّالِمِينَ
transliteration: wamā ẓalamnāhum walākin kānū humu l-ẓālimīna

t.sahih-int: And We did not wrong them, but it was they who were the wrongdoers
t.asad: And it is not We who will be doing wrong unto them, but it is they who will have wronged themselves
t.yusuf-ali: Nowise shall We be unjust to them: but it is they who have been unjust themselves
t.pickthall: We wronged them not, but they it was who did the wrong
t.arberry: We never wronged them, but they themselves did the wrong
t.hilali-khan: We wronged them not, but they were the Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers, etc)
t.itani: We did them no injustice, but it was they who were the unjust.
t.mubarakpuri: We wronged them not, but they were the wrongdoers.
t.qarib: We did not wrong them, but it was they who were the unjust
t.wahiduddin: We have not wronged them; it was they who were the wrongdoers
t.shakir: And We are not unjust to them, but they themselves were unjust
t.haleem: We never wronged them; they were the ones who did wrong
t.maariful: We did not do injustice to them, rather, they themselves were the unjust
t.khattab: We did not wrong them, but it was they who were the wrongdoers
t.ghali: And in no way did We do injustice to them, but it is they who were the unjust
t.irving: Still We will not harm them but rather they have already harmed themselves
t.iwq: And We did not wrong them, but it was they who were those who do wrong

t_id.kemenag: Dan tidaklah Kami menganiaya mereka tetapi merekalah yang menganiaya diri mereka sendiri.
t_id.sabiq: Kami, dengan memberikan siksaan itu, tidak menzalimi mereka. Merekalah yang yang menzalimi diri sendiri, karena mereka lebih memilih kesesatan daripada petunjuk.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidaklah Kami menganiaya mereka tetapi merekalah yang menganiaya diri mereka sendiri.)
t_id.iwq: Dan tidaklah Kami menganiaya mereka tetapi merekalah yang menganiaya diri mereka sendiri.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وما ظلمناهم ولكن كانوا هم الظالمين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَا ظَلَمۡنَٰهُمۡ وَلَٰكِن كَانُواْ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dan tidaklah Kami menganiaya mereka tetapi merekalah yang menganiaya diri mereka sendiri

ar: وَنَادَوۡا۟ یَـٰمَـٰلِكُ لِیَقۡضِ عَلَیۡنَا رَبُّكَۖ قَالَ إِنَّكُم مَّـٰكِثُونَ
ar_simple: وَنَادَوْا يَا مَالِكُ لِيَقْضِ عَلَيْنَا رَبُّكَ ۖ قَالَ إِنَّكُمْ مَاكِثُونَ
transliteration: wanādaw yāmāliku liyaqḍi ʿalaynā rabbuka qāla innakum mākithūna

t.sahih-int: And they will call, "O Malik, let your Lord put an end to us!" He will say, "Indeed, you will remain
t.asad: And they will cry: “O thou [angel] who rulest [over hell]! Let thy Sustainer put an end to us!” - whereupon] he will reply: “Verily, you must live on [in this state]
t.yusuf-ali: They will cry: "O Malik! would that thy Lord put an end to us!" He will say, "Nay, but ye shall abide
t.pickthall: And they cry: O master! Let thy Lord make an end of us. He saith: Lo! here ye must remain
t.arberry: And they shall call, 'O
t.hilali-khan: And they will cry: "O Malik (Keeper of Hell)! Let your Lord make an end of us." He will say: "Verily you shall abide forever
t.itani: And they will cry, “O Malek, let your Lord finish us off.” He will say, “You are staying.”
t.mubarakpuri: And they will cry: "O Malik (keeper of Hell)! Let your Lord make an end of us." He will say: "Verily, you shall abide forever."
t.qarib: They will call, "Malik, let your Lord finish us off!" He will say, "You are staying
t.wahiduddin: They will cry, "Master, if only your Lord would put an end to us!" But he [the angel] will answer, "No! You are here to stay
t.shakir: And they shall call out: O Malik! let your Lord make an end of us. He shall say: Surely you shall tarry
t.haleem: They will cry, ‘Malik, if only your Lord would finish us off,’ but he will answer, ‘No! You are here to stay.’
t.maariful: And they will call (Mālik, the keeper of the Hell,) “O Mālik, let your Lord do away with us.” He will say, “You have to stay on.”
t.khattab: They will cry, “O Mâlik! Let your Lord finish us off.” He will answer, “You are definitely here to stay.”
t.ghali: And they will call out, "O Malik, (keeper of Hell) let your Lord decree upon us!" He will say, "Surely you are staying
t.irving: They will cry out: "Master, have your Lord put an end to us!" He will say: "You must stay on here
t.iwq: And they will call, "O Malik, let your Lord put an end to us!" He will say, "Indeed, you will remain

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berseru: "Hai Malik biarlah Tuhanmu membunuh kami saja". Dia menjawab: "Kamu akan tetap tinggal (di neraka ini)".
t_id.sabiq: Ketika orang-orang yang berdosa itu merasa putus asa untuk mendapatkan keringanan dari siksaan yang pedih, mereka berseru kepada Mâlik, malaikat penjaga neraka, dengan mengatakan, "Mohonlah kepada Tuhanmu agar Dia mematikan kami, supaya kami dapat beristirahat dari kepedihan neraka jahanam ini. Mâlik menjawab, "Kalian akan tetap berada di dalam siksa."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berseru, "Hai Malik!) dia adalah malaikat penjaga neraka (Biarlah Rabbmu membunuh kami saja") maksudnya, mematikan kami. (Dia menjawab) seruan mereka setelah seribu tahun kemudian, ("Kalian akan tetap tinggal") di dalam azab yang abadi untuk selama-lamanya.
t_id.iwq: Mereka berseru: "Hai Malik biarlah Tuhanmu membunuh kami saja". Dia menjawab: "Kamu akan tetap tinggal (di neraka ini)".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ونادوا يا مالك» هو خازن النار «ليقض علينا ربك» ليمتنا «قال» بعد ألف سنة «إنكم ماكثون» مقيمون في العذاب دائماً
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَنَادَوۡاْ يَٰمَٰلِكُ لِيَقۡضِ عَلَيۡنَا رَبُّكَۖ قَالَ إِنَّكُم مَّـٰكِثُونَ (يَا مَالِكُ: هُوَ: خَازِنُ جَهَنَّمَ, لِيَقْضِ: لِيُمِتْنَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berseru, "Hai Malik!) dia adalah malaikat penjaga neraka (Biarlah Rabbmu membunuh kami saja") maksudnya, mematikan kami. (Dia menjawab) seruan mereka setelah seribu tahun kemudian, ("Kalian akan tetap tinggal") di dalam azab yang abadi untuk selama-lamanya

ar: لَقَدۡ جِئۡنَـٰكُم بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكۡثَرَكُمۡ لِلۡحَقِّ كَـٰرِهُونَ
ar_simple: لَقَدْ جِئْنَاكُمْ بِالْحَقِّ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَكُمْ لِلْحَقِّ كَارِهُونَ
transliteration: laqad ji'nākum bil-ḥaqi walākinna aktharakum lil'ḥaqqi kārihūna

t.sahih-int: We had certainly brought you the truth, but most of you, to the truth, were averse
t.asad: INDEED, [O you sinners,] We have conveyed the truth unto you; but most of you abhor the truth
t.yusuf-ali: Verily We have brought the Truth to you: but most of you have a hatred for Truth
t.pickthall: We verily brought the Truth unto you, but ye were, most of you, averse to the Truth
t.arberry: We brought you the truth, but most of you were averse to the truth
t.hilali-khan: Indeed We have brought the truth (Muhammad SAW with the Quran), to you, but most of you have a hatred for the truth
t.itani: We have given you the truth, but most of you hate the truth.
t.mubarakpuri: Indeed We have brought the truth to you, but most of you have a hatred for the truth.
t.qarib: We have given you the Truth, but most of you, hate the Truth
t.wahiduddin: We have certainly brought the truth to you: but most of you have an aversion for the truth
t.shakir: Certainly We have brought you the truth, but most of you are averse to the truth
t.haleem: We have brought you the Truth but most of you despise it
t.maariful: (O disbelievers,) indeed We have brought to you the truth, but it is the truth that most of you detest
t.khattab: We certainly brought the truth to you, but most of you were resentful of the truth
t.ghali: Indeed We have already come to you with the Truth, but most of you are hating the Truth
t.irving: We brought you the Truth even though most of you detest the Truth
t.iwq: We had certainly brought you the truth, but most of you, to the truth, were averse

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Kami benar-benar telah memhawa kebenaran kepada kamu tetapi kebanyakan di antara kamu benci pada kebenaran itu.
t_id.sabiq: Allah berfirman, sebagai jawaban atas permohonan mereka, "Rasul Kami telah datang kepada kalian, wahai penduduk Mekah, dengan membawa agama yang benar. Tetapi hanya sedikit yang mempercayainya, dan kebanyakan kalian malah menentangnya. Mereka benci dengan kebenaran ini.
t_id.jalalayn-id: Allah swt. berfirman: (Sesungguhnya Kami benar-benar telah membawa kepada kalian) hai penduduk Mekah (kebenaran) melalui lisan rasul (tetapi kebanyakan di antara kalian benci pada kebenaran itu.)
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Kami benar-benar telah memhawa kebenaran kepada kamu tetapi kebanyakan di antara kamu benci pada kebenaran itu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: قال تعالى: «لقد جئناكم» أي أهل مكة «بالحق» على لسان الرسول «ولكن أكثركم للحق كارهون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: لَقَدۡ جِئۡنَٰكُم بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكۡثَرَكُمۡ لِلۡحَقِّ كَٰرِهُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Allah swt. berfirman: (Sesungguhnya Kami benar-benar telah membawa kepada kalian) hai penduduk Mekah (kebenaran) melalui lisan rasul (tetapi kebanyakan di antara kalian benci pada kebenaran itu)

ar: أَمۡ أَبۡرَمُوۤا۟ أَمۡرࣰا فَإِنَّا مُبۡرِمُونَ
ar_simple: أَمْ أَبْرَمُوا أَمْرًا فَإِنَّا مُبْرِمُونَ
transliteration: am abramū amran fa-innā mub'rimūna

t.sahih-int: Or have they devised [some] affair? But indeed, We are devising [a plan]
t.asad: Why - can they [who deny the truth ever] determine what [the truth] should be
t.yusuf-ali: What! have they settled some plan (among themselves)? But it is We Who settle things
t.pickthall: Or do they determine any thing (against the Prophet)? Lo! We (also) are determining
t.arberry: Or have they contrived some matter? We too are contriving
t.hilali-khan: Or have they plotted some plan? Then We too are planning
t.itani: Have they contrived some scheme? We too are contriving.
t.mubarakpuri: Or have they plotted some plan Then We too are planning.
t.qarib: Or have they thought up some scheme? We too have been scheming
t.wahiduddin: Have they determined upon a course? Then We too are determined
t.shakir: Or have they settled an affair? Then surely We are the settlers
t.haleem: Have these disbelievers thought up some scheme? We too have been scheming
t.maariful: Is it that they have firmly resolved to do something (harmful to Our messenger)? Then, We have firmly resolved (to frustrate their plan)
t.khattab: Or have they mastered some ˹evil˺ plan? Then We ˹too˺ are surely planning
t.ghali: Or (even) have they ratified a command? (i.e., A plot against you and the believers) Then surely We will be ratifying (too)
t.irving: Or have they tried to use some scheme [against you]? Well, We will try something too
t.iwq: Or have they devised some affair? But indeed, We are devising a plan

t_id.kemenag: Bahkan mereka telah menetapkan satu tipu daya (jahat), maka sesungguhnya Kami menetapkan pula.
t_id.sabiq: Bahkan, apakah orang-orang musyrik Mekah begitu rapi menyusun siasat mereka untuk mendustakan Rasululullah dan melakukan konspirasi untuk membunuhnya? Kami lebih rapi dalam menentukan ganjaran untuk mereka dan memenangkanmu, Muhammad.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Bahkan mereka telah menetapkan) yaitu orang-orang kafir Mekah telah memutuskan (suatu tipu daya) kejahatan untuk mencelakakan Nabi Muhammad (maka sesungguhnya Kami menetapkan pula) keputusan Kami untuk membuat tipu muslihat guna membinasakan mereka.
t_id.iwq: Bahkan mereka telah menetapkan satu tipu daya (jahat), maka sesungguhnya Kami menetapkan pula.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم أبرموا» أي كفار مكة: أحكموا «أمراً» في كيد محمد النبي «فإنا مبرمون» محكمون كيدنا في إهلاكهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ أَبۡرَمُوٓاْ أَمۡرٗا فَإِنَّا مُبۡرِمُونَ (أَمْ أَبْرَمُوا أَمْرًا: أَحْكَمُوا أَمْرًا فِي كَيْدِ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ - صلى الله عليه وسلم)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Bahkan mereka telah menetapkan) yaitu orang-orang kafir Mekah telah memutuskan (suatu tipu daya) kejahatan untuk mencelakakan Nabi Muhammad (maka sesungguhnya Kami menetapkan pula) keputusan Kami untuk membuat tipu muslihat guna membinasakan mereka

ar: أَمۡ یَحۡسَبُونَ أَنَّا لَا نَسۡمَعُ سِرَّهُمۡ وَنَجۡوَىٰهُمۚ بَلَىٰ وَرُسُلُنَا لَدَیۡهِمۡ یَكۡتُبُونَ
ar_simple: أَمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّا لَا نَسْمَعُ سِرَّهُمْ وَنَجْوَاهُمْ ۚ بَلَىٰ وَرُسُلُنَا لَدَيْهِمْ يَكْتُبُونَ
transliteration: am yaḥsabūna annā lā nasmaʿu sirrahum wanajwāhum balā warusulunā ladayhim yaktubūna

t.sahih-int: Or do they think that We hear not their secrets and their private conversations? Yes, [We do], and Our messengers are with them recording
t.asad: Or do they, perchance, think that We do not hear their hidden thoughts and their secret confabulations? Yea, indeed, [We do,] and Our heavenly forces are with them, recording [all]
t.yusuf-ali: Or do they think that We hear not their secrets and their private counsels? Indeed (We do), and Our messengers are by them, to record
t.pickthall: Or deem they that We cannot hear their secret thoughts and private confidences? Nay, but Our envoys, present with them, do record
t.arberry: Or do they think We hear not their secret and what they conspire together? Yes indeed, and Our messengers are present with them writing it down
t.hilali-khan: Or do they think that We hear not their secrets and their private counsel? (Yes We do) and Our Messengers (appointed angels in charge of mankind) are by them, to record
t.itani: Or do they think that We cannot hear their secrets and their conspiracies? Yes indeed, Our messengers are by them, writing down.
t.mubarakpuri: Or do they think that We hear not their secrets and their private counsel Yes (We do) and Our messengers are by them, to record.
t.qarib: Or do they think that We cannot hear their secrets and their private talk? Yes, We can, and Our messengers are with them writing it down
t.wahiduddin: Do they imagine We do not hear their secret talk and their private counsels? On the contrary, Our messengers [angels] are at their sides, recording everything
t.shakir: Or do they think that We do not hear what they conceal and their secret discourses? Aye! and Our apostles with them write down
t.haleem: Do they think We cannot hear their secret talk and their private counsel? Yes we can: Our messengers are at their sides, recording everything
t.maariful: Do they think that We do not hear their secrets and their whispers? Yes of course, Our messengers (angels) are with them who record (whatever they plan)
t.khattab: Or do they think that We do not hear their ˹evil˺ thoughts and secret talks? Yes ˹We do˺! And Our messenger-angels are in their presence, recording ˹it all˺
t.ghali: Or (even) do they reckon that We do not hear their secret and their private conferences? Yes indeed, and Our Messengers are close to them writing (everything) down
t.irving: Or do they reckon that We do not hear their secrets nor how they conspire together? Of course Our messengers are writing it (all) down in their very presence
t.iwq: Or do they think that We hear not their secrets and their private conversations? Yes, We do, and Our messengers are with them recording

t_id.kemenag: Apakah mereka mengira, bahwa Kami tidak mendengar rahasia dan bisikan-bisikan mereka? Sebenarnya (Kami mendengar), dan utusan-utusan (malaikat-malaikat) Kami selalu mencatat di sisi mereka.
t_id.sabiq: Bahkan, apakah orang-orang musyrik mengira bahwa Kami tidak mendengar pembicaraan di antara sesama mereka mengenai siasat buruk itu dan pembicaraan mereka mengenai sikap mendustakan kebenaran? Tentu Kami mendengarnya. Dan malaikat-malaikat penjaga yang selalu mengikuti mereka mencatat semua pembicaraan mereka itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Apakah mereka mengira, bahwa Kami tidak mendengar rahasia dan bisikan-bisikan mereka) yakni apa-apa yang mereka rahasiakan dari orang lain dan apa-apa yang mereka perlihatkan dengan terang-terangan di antara sesama mereka sendiri. (Sebenarnya) Kami mendengar hal tersebut (dan utusan-utusan Kami) yakni malaikat-malaikat pencatat amal perbuatan (di sisi mereka) di sisi orang-orang kafir (selalu mencatat) hal tersebut.
t_id.iwq: Apakah mereka mengira, bahwa Kami tidak mendengar rahasia dan bisikan-bisikan mereka? Sebenarnya (Kami mendengar), dan utusan-utusan (malaikat-malaikat) Kami selalu mencatat di sisi mereka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم يحسبون أنا لا نسمع سرهم ونجواهم» ما يسرون إلى غيرهم وما يجهرون به بينهم «بلى» نسمع ذلك «ورسلنا» الحفظة «لديهم» عندهم «يكتبون» ذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ يَحۡسَبُونَ أَنَّا لَا نَسۡمَعُ سِرَّهُمۡ وَنَجۡوَىٰهُمۚ بَلَىٰ وَرُسُلُنَا لَدَيۡهِمۡ يَكۡتُبُونَ (يَحْسَبُونَ: يَظُنُّونَ, وَنَجْوَاهُم: مَا تَكَلَّمُوا فِيهِ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُمْ, وَرُسُلُنَا: مَلَائِكَتُنَا الكِرَامُ الحَفَظَةُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Apakah mereka mengira, bahwa Kami tidak mendengar rahasia dan bisikan-bisikan mereka) yakni apa-apa yang mereka rahasiakan dari orang lain dan apa-apa yang mereka perlihatkan dengan terang-terangan di antara sesama mereka sendiri. (Sebenarnya) Kami mendengar hal tersebut (dan utusan-utusan Kami) yakni malaikat-malaikat pencatat amal perbuatan (di sisi mereka) di sisi orang-orang kafir (selalu mencatat) hal tersebut

ar: قُلۡ إِن كَانَ لِلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ وَلَدࣱ فَأَنَا۠ أَوَّلُ ٱلۡعَـٰبِدِینَ
ar_simple: قُلْ إِنْ كَانَ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ وَلَدٌ فَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْعَابِدِينَ
transliteration: qul in kāna lilrraḥmāni waladun fa-anā awwalu l-ʿābidīna

t.sahih-int: Say, [O Muhammad], "If the Most Merciful had a son, then I would be the first of [his] worshippers
t.asad: Say [O Prophet]: “If the Most Gracious [truly] had a son, I would be the first to worship him!”
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "If (Allah) Most Gracious had a son, I would be the first to worship
t.pickthall: Say (O Muhammad): If the Beneficent One hath a son, then, I shall be first among the worshippers. (But there is no son)
t.arberry: Say: 'If the All-merciful has a son, then I am the first to serve him
t.hilali-khan: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "If the Most Beneficent (Allah) had a son (or children as you pretend), then I am the first of Allah's worshippers [who deny and refute this claim of yours (and the first to believe in Allah Alone and testify that He has no children)]." [Tafsir At-Tabari]
t.itani: Say, “If the Most Gracious had a son, I would be the first to worship.”
t.mubarakpuri: Say: "If the Most Gracious had a son, then I am the first of the worshippers."
t.qarib: Say, "If the Merciful-to-all had a son, then I would be the first to worship him
t.wahiduddin: Say, "If the All Merciful had a son, I would be the first to worship him
t.shakir: Say: If the Beneficent Allah has a son, I am the foremost of those who serve
t.haleem: Say [Prophet], ‘If the Lord of Mercy [truly] had offspring I would be the first to worship [them], but
t.maariful: Say, “Had there been a son for the RaHmān, I would have been the first to worship.”
t.khattab: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “If the Most Compassionate ˹really˺ had offspring, I would be the first worshipper.”
t.ghali: Say, "In case The All-Merciful has a child, then I am the first among the worshippers
t.irving: SAY: "If the Mercy-giving had a son, I would be the first to worship [him]
t.iwq: Say, O Muhammad, "If the Ever-Merciful had a son, then I would be the first of his worshippers

t_id.kemenag: Katakanlah, jika benar Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah mempunyai anak, maka akulah (Muhammad) orang yang mula-mula memuliakan (anak itu).
t_id.sabiq: Katakanlah kepada orang-orang musyrik, "Jika terbukti benar bahwa Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah itu mempunyai anak, maka aku adalah orang pertama yang akan menyembah anak Tuhan itu. Tetapi alasan mereka bahwa Tuhan Sang Pemurah itu mempunyai anak, tidak terbukti benar karena begitu lemahnya. Allah Swt. Mahasuci dari segala kekurangan. Dia memiliki segala sifat sempurna."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah! Jika benar Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah mempunyai anak) seumpamanya (maka akulah orang yang mula-mula menyembahnya) menyembah anak Tuhan itu, akan tetapi telah ditetapkan, bahwa tiada anak bagi-Nya, sehingga tiada pula penyembahan itu.
t_id.iwq: Katakanlah, jika benar Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah mempunyai anak, maka akulah (Muhammad) orang yang mula-mula memuliakan (anak itu).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: قل إن كان للرحمن ولد» فرضاً «فأنا أول العابدين» للولد لكن ثبت أن لا ولد له تعالى فانتفت عبادته
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُلۡ إِن كَانَ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ وَلَدٞ فَأَنَا۠ أَوَّلُ ٱلۡعَٰبِدِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah! Jika benar Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah mempunyai anak) seumpamanya (maka akulah orang yang mula-mula menyembahnya) menyembah anak Tuhan itu, akan tetapi telah ditetapkan, bahwa tiada anak bagi-Nya, sehingga tiada pula penyembahan itu

ar: سُبۡحَـٰنَ رَبِّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَرۡشِ عَمَّا یَصِفُونَ
ar_simple: سُبْحَانَ رَبِّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ رَبِّ الْعَرْشِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ
transliteration: sub'ḥāna rabbi l-samāwāti wal-arḍi rabbi l-ʿarshi ʿammā yaṣifūna

t.sahih-int: Exalted is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Lord of the Throne, above what they describe
t.asad: Utterly remote, in His glory, is the Sustainer of the heavens and the earth - the Sustainer, in almightiness enthroned from all that they may attribute to Him by way of definition
t.yusuf-ali: Glory to the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne (of Authority)! (He is free) from the things they attribute (to him)
t.pickthall: Glorified be the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, from that which they ascribe (unto Him)
t.arberry: Glory be to the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, above that they describe
t.hilali-khan: Glorified be the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne! Exalted be He from all that they ascribe (to Him)
t.itani: Glorified be the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, beyond what they describe.
t.mubarakpuri: Glorified is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne! Exalted be He from all that they ascribe (to Him).
t.qarib: Exalted is the Lord of the heavens and the earth in His glory, Lord of the Throne, totally above what they ascribe to Him
t.wahiduddin: But -- exalted be the Lord of the heavens and earth, the Lord of the Throne -- He is far above their [false] descriptions
t.shakir: Glory to the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of power, from what they describe
t.haleem: exalted be the Lord of the heavens and earth, the Lord of the Throne- He is far above their false descriptions.’
t.maariful: Pure is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, from what they attribute (to Him)
t.khattab: Glorified is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, far above what they claim
t.ghali: All Extolment be to The Lord of the heavens and the earth, The Lord of the Throne, above whatever they describe
t.irving: Glory be to the Lord of Heaven and Earth, Lord of the Throne, ahead of what they ascribe [to Him]
t.iwq: Exalted is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Lord of the Throne, above what they describe

t_id.kemenag: Maha Suci Tuhan Yang empunya langit dan bumi, Tuhan Yang empunya 'Arsy, dari apa yang mereka sifatkan itu.
t_id.sabiq: Sungguh Mahasuci, Sang Pencipta langit dan bumi, Tuhan pencipta arasy yang mahamegah, dari semua yang digambarkan oleh orang-orang musyrik mengenai sesuatu yang tidak pantas bagi-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maha Suci Rabb Yang empunya langit dan bumi, Rabb Yang empunya Arasy) yakni Al-Kursi (dari apa yang mereka sifatkan) dari apa yang telah mereka katakan itu, berupa kedustaan terhadap-Nya, yaitu menisbatkan kepada-Nya mempunyai anak.
t_id.iwq: Maha Suci Tuhan Yang empunya langit dan bumi, Tuhan Yang empunya 'Arsy, dari apa yang mereka sifatkan itu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «سبحان رب السماوات والأرض رب العرش» الكرسي «عما يصفون» يقولون من الكذب بنسبة الولد إليه
tafsir.siraj-ar: سُبۡحَٰنَ رَبِّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَرۡشِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ (عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ: عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ اللهَ بِهِ؛ مِنَ الصَّاحِبَةِ، وَالوَلَدِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maha Suci Rabb Yang empunya langit dan bumi, Rabb Yang empunya Arasy) yakni Al-Kursi (dari apa yang mereka sifatkan) dari apa yang telah mereka katakan itu, berupa kedustaan terhadap-Nya, yaitu menisbatkan kepada-Nya mempunyai anak

ar: فَذَرۡهُمۡ یَخُوضُوا۟ وَیَلۡعَبُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ یُلَـٰقُوا۟ یَوۡمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِی یُوعَدُونَ
ar_simple: فَذَرْهُمْ يَخُوضُوا وَيَلْعَبُوا حَتَّىٰ يُلَاقُوا يَوْمَهُمُ الَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ
transliteration: fadharhum yakhūḍū wayalʿabū ḥattā yulāqū yawmahumu alladhī yūʿadūna

t.sahih-int: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised
t.asad: But leave them to indulge in idle talk and play [with words] until they face that [Judgment] Day of theirs which they have been promised
t.yusuf-ali: So leave them to babble and play (with vanities) until they meet that Day of theirs, which they have been promised
t.pickthall: So let them flounder (in their talk) and play until they meet the Day which they are promised
t.arberry: Then leave them alone to plunge and play, until they encounter that day of theirs which they are promised
t.hilali-khan: So leave them (alone) to speak nonsense and play until they meet the Day of theirs, which they have been promised
t.itani: So leave them to blunder and play, until they encounter their Day which they are promised.
t.mubarakpuri: So leave them (alone) to speak nonsense and play until they meet the Day of theirs which they have been promised.
t.qarib: So, let them indulge in idle talk and play until they face that Day of theirs which they have been promised
t.wahiduddin: So leave them alone to indulge in vain discourse and amuse themselves until they come face to face with that Day which they have been promised
t.shakir: So leave them plunging into false discourses and sporting until they meet their day which they are threatened with
t.haleem: Leave them to wade in deeper and play about, until they face the Day they have been promised
t.maariful: So, let them indulge (in their fallacy) and play, until they face their Day that they are promised
t.khattab: So let them indulge ˹in falsehood˺ and amuse ˹themselves˺ until they face their Day, which they have been warned of
t.ghali: So leave them out to wade (i.e., to become absorbed in) and play until they meet the Day which they are promised
t.irving: Leave them to speculate and play around until they face their day which they have been promised
t.iwq: Thus leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised

t_id.kemenag: Maka biarlah mereka tenggelam (dalam kesesatan) dan bermain-main sampai mereka menemui hari yang dijanjikan kepada mereka.
t_id.sabiq: Oleh karena itu, biarkan mereka tenggelam dalam kebatilan dan bermain-main dengan dunia mereka--dengan tidak bersungguh-sungguh--dan jangan menoleh kepada mereka sampai datang hari kiamat yang dijanjikan agar dibalas semua perbuatan semua orang.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka biarlah mereka tenggelam) dalam kesesatannya atau dalam kebatilannya (dan bermain-main) di dalam dunia mereka (sampai mereka menemui hari yang dijanjikan kepada mereka) yaitu azab yang dijanjikan kepada mereka pada hari kiamat nanti.
t_id.iwq: Maka biarlah mereka tenggelam (dalam kesesatan) dan bermain-main sampai mereka menemui hari yang dijanjikan kepada mereka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فذرهم يخوضوا» في باطلهم «ويلعبوا» في دنياهم «حتى يلاقوا يومهم الذي يوعدون» فيه العذاب وهو يوم القيامة
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَذَرۡهُمۡ يَخُوضُواْ وَيَلۡعَبُواْ حَتَّىٰ يُلَٰقُواْ يَوۡمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ (فَذَرْهُمْ: اتْرُكْهُمْ, يَخُوضُوا: يَتَكَلَّمُوا بِبَاطِلِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka biarlah mereka tenggelam) dalam kesesatannya atau dalam kebatilannya (dan bermain-main) di dalam dunia mereka (sampai mereka menemui hari yang dijanjikan kepada mereka) yaitu azab yang dijanjikan kepada mereka pada hari kiamat nanti

ar: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِی فِی ٱلسَّمَاۤءِ إِلَـٰهࣱ وَفِی ٱلۡأَرۡضِ إِلَـٰهࣱۚ وَهُوَ ٱلۡحَكِیمُ ٱلۡعَلِیمُ
ar_simple: وَهُوَ الَّذِي فِي السَّمَاءِ إِلَٰهٌ وَفِي الْأَرْضِ إِلَٰهٌ ۚ وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ الْعَلِيمُ
transliteration: wahuwa alladhī fī l-samāi ilāhun wafī l-arḍi ilāhun wahuwa l-ḥakīmu l-ʿalīmu

t.sahih-int: And it is Allah who is [the only] deity in the heaven, and on the earth [the only] deity. And He is the Wise, the Knowing
t.asad: for [then they will come to know that] it is He [alone] who is God in heaven and God on earth, and [that] He alone is truly wise, all-knowing
t.yusuf-ali: It is He Who is Allah in heaven and Allah on earth; and He is full of Wisdom and Knowledge
t.pickthall: And He it is Who in the heaven is Allah, and in the earth Allah. He is the Wise, the Knower
t.arberry: And it is He who in heaven is God and in earth is God; He is the All-wise, the All-knowing
t.hilali-khan: It is He (Allah) Who is the only Ilah (God to be worshipped) in the heaven and the only Ilah (God to be worshipped) on the earth. And He is the All-Wise, the All-Knower
t.itani: It is He who is God in heaven, and God on earth. He is the Wise, the Knower.
t.mubarakpuri: It is He Who is the only God in the heaven and the only God on the earth. And He is the All-Wise, the All-Knower.
t.qarib: It is He who is God in heaven, and God on earth. He is the All-Wise, the All-Knowing
t.wahiduddin: It is He who is God in heaven, and God on earth: He is the Wise One, the All Knowing
t.shakir: And He it is Who is Allah in the heavens and Allah in the earth; and He is the Wise, the Knowing
t.haleem: It is He who is God in heaven and God on earth; He is the All Wise, the All Knowing
t.maariful: He is the One who is God to be worshipped in the sky, and God to be worshipped on the earth. And He is the Wise, the All-Knowing
t.khattab: It is He Who is ˹the only˺ God in the heavens and ˹the only˺ God on the earth. For He is the All-Wise, All-Knowing
t.ghali: And He is (The One) Who in the heaven is God, and in the earth is God; and He is The Ever-Wise, the Ever-Knowing
t.irving: He Alone is God in Heaven and God on Earth; He is the Wise, Aware
t.iwq: And it is Allah who is the only deity in the heaven, and on the earth the only deity. And He is the Wise, the Knowing

t_id.kemenag: Dan Dialah Tuhan (Yang disembah) di langit dan Tuhan (Yang disembah) di bumi dan Dialah Yang Maha Bijaksana lagi Maha Mengetahui.
t_id.sabiq: Dialah Tuhan yang disembah di langit dan di bumi dengan sebenarnya. Hanya Dialah yang mempunyai hikmah amat sempurna dalam perbuatan dan kebijaksanaan-Nya, dan yang ilmu-Nya meliputi segala yang telah lalu dan yang akan datang.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Dialah Tuhan yang disembah di langit) lafal Fis Samaa-i Ilaahun kedua huruf Hamzahnya dapat dibaca Tahqiq dan Tas-hil, yakni Tuhan yang disembah di langit (dan Tuhan yang disembah di bumi) kedua Zharaf yang ada dalam ayat ini berta'alluq kepada lafal sesudahnya (dan Dialah Yang Maha Bijaksana) di dalam mengatur makhluk-Nya (lagi Maha Mengetahui) kemaslahatan-kemaslahatan mereka.
t_id.iwq: Dan Dialah Tuhan (Yang disembah) di langit dan Tuhan (Yang disembah) di bumi dan Dialah Yang Maha Bijaksana lagi Maha Mengetahui.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وهو الذي» هو «في السماء إله» بتحقيق الهمزتين وإسقاط الأولى وتسهيلها كالياء، أي معبود «وفي الأرض إله» وكل من الظرفين متعلق بما بعده «وهو الحكيم» في تدبير خلقه «العليم» بمصالحهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي فِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِلَٰهٞ وَفِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ إِلَٰهٞۚ وَهُوَ ٱلۡحَكِيمُ ٱلۡعَلِيمُ (إِلَهٌ: مَعْبُودٌ بِحَقٍّ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Dialah Tuhan yang disembah di langit) lafal Fis Samaa-i Ilaahun kedua huruf Hamzahnya dapat dibaca Tahqiq dan Tas-hil, yakni Tuhan yang disembah di langit (dan Tuhan yang disembah di bumi) kedua Zharaf yang ada dalam ayat ini berta'alluq kepada lafal sesudahnya (dan Dialah Yang Maha Bijaksana) di dalam mengatur makhluk-Nya (lagi Maha Mengetahui) kemaslahatan-kemaslahatan mereka

ar: وَتَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِی لَهُۥ مُلۡكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَیۡنَهُمَا وَعِندَهُۥ عِلۡمُ ٱلسَّاعَةِ وَإِلَیۡهِ تُرۡجَعُونَ
ar_simple: وَتَبَارَكَ الَّذِي لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا وَعِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
transliteration: watabāraka alladhī lahu mul'ku l-samāwāti wal-arḍi wamā baynahumā waʿindahu ʿil'mu l-sāʿati wa-ilayhi tur'jaʿūna

t.sahih-int: And blessed is He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them and with whom is knowledge of the Hour and to whom you will be returned
t.asad: And hallowed be He unto whom the dominion over the heavens and the earth and all that is between them belongs, and with whom the knowledge of the Last Hour rests, and unto whom you all shall be brought back
t.yusuf-ali: And blessed is He to Whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and all between them: with Him is the Knowledge of the Hour (of Judgment): and to Him shall ye be brought back
t.pickthall: And blessed be He unto Whom belongeth the Sovereignty of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, and with Whom is knowledge of the Hour, and unto Whom ye will be returned
t.arberry: Glory be to Him, to whom belongs the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth and all that between them is; with Him is the knowledge of the Hour, and to Him you shall be returned
t.hilali-khan: And blessed be He to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, and with Whom is the knowledge of the Hour, and to Whom you (all) will be returned
t.itani: And blessed is He Who has sovereignty over the heavens and the earth and what is between them. He alone has knowledge of the Hour, and to Him you will be returned.
t.mubarakpuri: And Blessed be He to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, and with Whom is the knowledge of the Hour, and to Whom you (all) will be returned.
t.qarib: And blessed is He Who has sovereignty over the heavens and the earth and everything between them. He alone has knowledge of the Hour. You will all be returned to Him
t.wahiduddin: blessed be He who has sovereignty over the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them. He alone has knowledge of the Hour, and to Him you shall be returned
t.shakir: And blessed is He Whose is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, and with Him is the knowledge of the hour, and to Him shall you be brought back
t.haleem: Exalted is He who has control of the heavens and earth and everything between them; He has knowledge of the Hour; you will all be returned to Him
t.maariful: Glorious is the One to whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and whatever lies between them. With Him is the knowledge of the Hour; and towards Him you are to be returned
t.khattab: And Blessed is the One to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything in between! With Him ˹alone˺ is the knowledge of the Hour. And to Him you will ˹all˺ be returned
t.ghali: And Supremely Blessed is He to Whom belongs the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them both; and in His Providence is the knowledge of the Hour; and to Him you will be returned
t.irving: Blessed is He Who holds control over Heaven and Earth, and anything in between them. He has knowledge of the Hour and to Him will you be returned
t.iwq: And blessed is He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them and with whom is knowledge of the Hour and to whom you will be returned

t_id.kemenag: Dan Maha Suci Tuhan Yang mempunyai kerajaan langit dan bumi; dan apa yang ada di antara keduanya; dan di sisi-Nya-lah pengetahuan tentang hari kiamat dan hanya kepada-Nya-lah kamu dikembalikan.
t_id.sabiq: Hanya Dialah yang Mahatinggi dan Mahabesar. Dia memiliki hak penuh untuk mengatur langit dan bumi dengan segala isinya, dan mengatur ruang antara langit dan bumi dengan segala makhluknya, baik yang dapat disaksikan maupun yang tidak dapat disaksikan. Dialah yang mengatur semua itu. Hanya Dia yang memiliki pengetahuan tentang datangnya hari kiamat. Hanya kepada-Nyalah kalian semua akan dikembalikan di akhirat untuk dihisab.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Maha Besar) Maha Agung (Tuhan Yang mempunyai kerajaan langit dan bumi; dan apa yang ada di antara keduanya; dan di sisi-Nyalah pengetahuan tentang hari kiamat) yakni kapan ia akan terjadi (dan hanya kepada-Nyalah kalian dikembalikan) lafal Turja'uuna dapat pula dibaca Yurja`uuna; berdasarkan qiraat kedua maka artinya: Dan hanya kepada-Nyalah mereka dikembalikan.
t_id.iwq: Dan Maha Suci Tuhan Yang mempunyai kerajaan langit dan bumi; dan apa yang ada di antara keduanya; dan di sisi-Nya-lah pengetahuan tentang hari kiamat dan hanya kepada-Nya-lah kamu dikembalikan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وتبارك» تعظم «الذي له ملك السموات والأرض وما بينهما وعنده علم الساعة» متى تقوم «وإليه يرجعون» بالياء والتاء
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَتَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِي لَهُۥ مُلۡكُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا وَعِندَهُۥ عِلۡمُ ٱلسَّاعَةِ وَإِلَيۡهِ تُرۡجَعُونَ (وَتَبَارَكَ: تَكَاثَرَتْ بَرَكَةُ اللهِ، وَكَثُرَ خَيْرُهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Maha Besar) Maha Agung (Tuhan Yang mempunyai kerajaan langit dan bumi; dan apa yang ada di antara keduanya; dan di sisi-Nyalah pengetahuan tentang hari kiamat) yakni kapan ia akan terjadi (dan hanya kepada-Nyalah kalian dikembalikan) lafal Turja'uuna dapat pula dibaca Yurja`uuna; berdasarkan qiraat kedua maka artinya: Dan hanya kepada-Nyalah mereka dikembalikan

ar: وَلَا یَمۡلِكُ ٱلَّذِینَ یَدۡعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ ٱلشَّفَـٰعَةَ إِلَّا مَن شَهِدَ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَهُمۡ یَعۡلَمُونَ
ar_simple: وَلَا يَمْلِكُ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ الشَّفَاعَةَ إِلَّا مَنْ شَهِدَ بِالْحَقِّ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ
transliteration: walā yamliku alladhīna yadʿūna min dūnihi l-shafāʿata illā man shahida bil-ḥaqi wahum yaʿlamūna

t.sahih-int: And those they invoke besides Him do not possess [power of] intercession; but only those who testify to the truth [can benefit], and they know
t.asad: And those [beings] whom some invoke beside God have it not in their power to intercede [on Judgment Day] for any but such as have [in their lifetime] borne witness to the truth, and have been aware [that God is one and unique]
t.yusuf-ali: And those whom they invoke besides Allah have no power of intercession;- only he who bears witness to the Truth, and they know (him)
t.pickthall: And those unto whom they cry instead of Him possess no power of intercession, saving him who beareth witness unto the Truth knowingly
t.arberry: Those they call upon, apart from Him, have no power of intercession, save such as have testified to the truth, and that knowingly
t.hilali-khan: And those whom they invoke instead of Him have no power of intercession; except those who bear witness to the truth (i.e. believed in the Oneness of Allah, and obeyed His Orders), and they know (the facts about the Oneness of Allah)
t.itani: Those they invoke besides Him are incapable of intercession; only those who testify to the truth and have knowledge.
t.mubarakpuri: And those whom they invoke instead of Him have no power of intercession -- except for those who bear witness to the truth knowingly, and they know.
t.qarib: Those deities they invoke besides Him have no power of intercession; only those who testify to the Truth and have full knowledge
t.wahiduddin: Those whom they invoke besides God have no power of intercession, only those who bear witness to the truth and they know
t.shakir: And those whom they call upon besides Him have no authority for intercession, but he who bears witness of the truth and they know (him)
t.haleem: Those gods they invoke besides Him have no power of intercession, unlike those who bore witness to the truth and recognized it
t.maariful: And those whom they invoke besides Him have no power to intercede; but those who bear witness to the truth, while they have knowledge (may intercede for the believers with Allah’s permission)
t.khattab: ˹But˺ those ˹objects of worship˺ they invoke besides Him have no power to intercede, except those who testify to the truth knowingly
t.ghali: And the ones they invoke, apart from Him, do not possess (the power) of intercession, excepting him who witnesses to the Truth, and they know it
t.irving: The ones whom they appeal to instead of Him control no intercession except for someone who testifies concerning the Truth, and they realize it
